news_summary: US non-farm payrolls beat expectations, lifting the US dollar and pressuring copper. Fed/BoE signals support earlier monetary tightening. On the supply side, China’s cathode output is higher, concentrate TCs rose, and smelters stay profitable, while imported inflows and off-season inventory weigh on prices. Net: rangebound SHFE/LME copper. | SMM says copper social inventories in China’s major trading markets fell 1,000 mt over the weekend to 170,000 mt. Shanghai rose slightly, while Guangdong dropped sharply. The decline was linked to reduced arrivals from Guangxi and overseas. Traders will watch this week’s lower import arrivals, which may push inventories down if consumption holds. | Amex Exploration announced discovery of a copper-rich VMS zone at its Perron property, reporting 2.40% Cu over 7.8 m (including 3.92% Cu over 4.4 m) and accompanying Zn/Au/Ag grades. It also mentions a follow-up hole at depth, awaiting assays. The article is copper-focused and exploration-stage. | Hannan reports soil geochemical sampling at the Tabalosos East target in Peru, extending sub-cropping copper-silver mineralized horizons over 24 km and confirming continuity of previously reported high-grade copper-silver zones. Additional sampling and trenching are planned, with permitting for potential drilling underway. | Hannan soil sampling continues at Tabalosos East in Peru, extending the scale and confirming continuity of copper-silver mineralization. The update supports the potential quality and extent of the mineralization at the project, which can modestly boost sentiment around future copper supply prospects, though it is not immediate production impact. | Antaike said China’s 22 surveyed smelters produced 747,200 tonnes of refined copper cathodes in July, roughly flat versus June, with output expected to rise in August. Copper concentrate treatment charges rose to $57.50/t. The report covers tin separately and includes no aluminium or nickel impact. | Latin Metals acquired an additional 700 hectares at its Auquis copper project in Peru, expanding total property size to 3,600 hectares. The company plans Phase I follow-up stream sediment and surface sampling and is negotiating local community agreements. The news is exploratory and does not affect immediate copper supply or trade flows. | The piece highlights rising copper prices alongside risks around BHP Escondida in Chile, where workers voted to reject a final labor contract and may strike unless mediation ends Aug. 13. It also cites production declines, tight supply, and generally supportive demand signals, pointing to strong bullish near-term pressure. | An interview argues the electronics industry is facing inflation alongside copper raw-material shortages. It cites growing PCB layer counts, 5G infrastructure demand, and electric-vehicle/battery needs, stressing copper-foil capacity constraints and long lead times for key equipment. Shipping costs are also elevated. | Workers at JX Nippon’s Chile operation are set to strike after wage negotiations fail. The labor action raises the risk of short-term production disruption and tighter copper supply availability. That can lift market expectations for near-term copper fundamentals and add upward pressure to copper prices.

news_summary: Analysts expect copper to rise short term as macro conditions eventually tighten less than feared while liquidity easing and stronger fundamentals improve. Supply is constrained by smelter maintenance and power restrictions, electrolytic output may fall in August, and scrap availability tightens. Demand looks supported by grid and post-flood projects and accelerating destocking. | SMM data show Shanghai bonded copper stocks declined 11,400 mt to 380,700 mt over Aug 6–13, falling for five straight weeks, though destocking slowed. Smelter output disruptions and refined copper replacing scrap reduced availability, while favorable price ratios and inflows of godown warrants after customs clearance further lowered bonded inventories. | London copper prices rose 0.4% after strikes at two Chilean mines (Codelco’s Andina and JX Nippon’s Caserones) heightened supply disruption risk. Escondida approved a contract, limiting upside. Fitch cited tightened copper and aluminum markets from supply constraints, while weaker China demand weighed sentiment. | Peruvian copper miner Cerro Verde paid $245.5 million in a disputed tax debt “under protest,” as arbitration over Peru’s tax calculations continues. With President Pedro Castillo seeking higher taxes on miners, similar disputes affecting large producers (including Buenaventura) can increase near-term cost and supply-risk expectations for copper.

Communities accuse MMG of failing to attend a scheduled dialogue. Las Bambas produces about 400,000 tonnes of copper annually, and the disruption may impede shipments. | Copper prices rebounded after two days of losses as investors turned to “buy the dip.” Support came from concerns that ongoing labor strikes in Chile and road blocks near Peru’s Las Bambas mine could disrupt copper supply. LME three-month copper rose 0.6% to $9,305.50/ton. | Copper prices fell for a third day to the lowest in almost two months, driven by weak economic data reinforcing demand concerns and nervousness ahead of central bank messaging at Jackson Hole. The selloff is echoed by declines in LME aluminum and nickel, reflecting broader risk-off pressure on industrial metals. | Copper surged after fears of renewed supply disruption at Peru’s Las Bambas mine. Residents resumed road blockades involving the MMG-owned operation, following a prior truce break under President Castillo. With Las Bambas producing about 400,000 tonnes annually, the market priced higher near-term copper tightness; LME three-month rose 0.8%. | White Metal Resources announced an updated Inferred mineral resource estimate for the Okohongo Cu-Ag project in Namibia. Using a 0.30% copper cut-off, it reports ~7.7 Mt grading 1.55% Cu (CuEq 1.82%). The news relates directly to prospective copper supply growth expectations. | The EU proposes a CBAM on certain imports to prevent carbon leakage, phasing in from 2023 and fully implemented from 2026. It would require importers to buy and surrender certificates based on embedded emissions, using EU ETS auction prices. Aluminium is explicitly in scope; carbon-costs may raise import costs and weigh on demand. | Three CODELCO unions began a strike at the Andina division after rejecting a labor agreement. The strike involves 1,300 of 1,437 workers and affects underground Rio Blanco and open-pit Sur Sur mines that produced over 184,000 tonnes of copper last year. The labor action increases near-term Chile copper supply risk.

news_summary: EMX Royalty Corp. completed the initial closing of its purchase of an NSR royalty interest on the Caserones copper-molybdenum mine in northern Chile, acquiring 0.418% effective NSR royalty for US$34.1 million. Further closing is expected by September 1. The deal is presented as improving cash flows, not changing copper supply. | Copper prices rose on Monday as risk-on sentiment returned, supported by signs China’s COVID outbreak is tapering and bargain hunting after recent weakness. Additional support came from a labor contract rejection at Chile’s El Teniente mine, raising supply concerns. Nickel and aluminum also increased alongside broader base-metal strength. | Copper jumped 2% on the LME as investors returned after a recent slump, attracted by lower prices and hopes for improving global growth and electrification-driven demand. The piece also references improving euro-zone activity and fewer China COVID cases, alongside a May refined-copper surplus shift. Aluminium and nickel rose too. | SMM expects SHFE and LME copper prices to dip further despite supportive domestic supply-demand fundamentals. July data disappointed and Fed minutes signaled tapering, weighing sentiment. Electric restrictions eased slightly but cathode supply limits still linger, while falling scrap and weaker price momentum constrain rod makers and reinforce bearish price expectations. | Playfair says core drilling is expected to begin in early September on its 100%-owned RKV copper project in Norway. Permits are approved, drill targets have been defined, and prior sampling showed strong mobile metal ion copper anomalies (with values >6,000 ppb). | Playfair Mining announced mobilization of a core drilling program for its RKV Copper Project in Norway, with permits approved and a lightweight, hand-carried drill machine arriving for September drilling. The project targets multiple high-grade copper soil anomalies (MMI Cu) consistent with potential weathering copper sulphides. | Edgemont Gold Corp. said drilling equipment is on site and Phase I drilling is about to start at its Dungate copper-gold porphyry project in B.C., targeting IP anomalies and a quartz-pyrite breccia zone reported in older drilling. It is an exploration update without assays or production impact, limiting immediate price effects. | Norden Crown Metals, with Boliden, has started ~2,500 metres of diamond drilling at the Burfjord copper-gold project in Norway, targeting IOCG-style mineralization and following up prior drill intercepts. The release details target zones and planned exploration activities, but signals no near-term mine output changes. | DR Congo has formed a commission to re-evaluate reserves and resources at China Molybdenum’s Tenke Fungurume copper-cobalt mine to verify compliance and potentially renegotiate contract terms and extensions. The review may affect perceived future copper supply, but no immediate production changes are confirmed.

Private miners warn the rates could pressure operations and even cause some mines to shut down, while supporters cite social spending needs. The bill’s progress and potential supply impact are bearish for copper. | SMM reports subdued trading in Shanghai’s imported copper market as import losses widen to 200 yuan/mt. news_summary: Chile’s environmental court approved JX Nippon’s Caserones copper mine compliance plan after SMA charged it in 2019 over alleged overpumping and tailings leaks. The $42 million plan includes returning desalinated water to the Copiapo River to address drought. Farmers challenged scope, but the court upheld SMA’s approach. | Nevada Copper reported completion of a key underground development crossing at its Pumpkin Hollow project, enabling faster development and scheduled backfilling, plus ongoing installation of ventilation fans. Financing updates include a KfW waiver extension and added liquidity via an amended promissory note to support the mine ramp-up. | Copper prices fell as China’s factory activity data pointed to slowing demand. August factory activity entered contraction due to COVID-19 containment, supply bottlenecks and high input costs. With China consuming roughly half of global copper, the weaker outlook weighed on both LME and Shanghai copper futures. | Murchison Minerals reports final assay results confirming near-surface nickel and copper mineralization at multiple targets (Syrah and 4048) within its HPM project in Quebec, with high NiEq/CuEq grab sample grades. The company plans additional prospecting and an upcoming ~3,500 m drilling program to test further mineralization. | Copper prices dropped more than 2% as global manufacturing slowed, with China reporting contraction for the first time in nearly 1.5 years. China’s state reserves also released 150,000 tonnes via auctions to curb prices. LME copper, aluminum and nickel were all lower in trading despite nickel’s stronger cash premium. | Namibia’s High Court interdicts the Ministry of Mines from granting mineral licences over the Haib Copper project area until a court review of the denied EPL renewal is finalized. The company says work was halted after non-renewal. A further hearing is scheduled for Sept. 16. | Vedanta said it wants to open dialogue with Zambia’s ZCCM and reiterated its commitment to invest $1.5bn in Konkola Copper Mines, including payments to local suppliers and funding for ESG and community projects. The comments follow a partial final arbitral award involving disputes with ZCCM and Zambia. | Nobel Resources announced a 10,000–12,500 meter diamond drilling program starting September 10, 2021 at its Algarrobo copper-gold (IOCG) project in Chile. The company targets multiple geophysical anomalies associated with near-surface copper mineralization, alongside a marketed offering and private placement to fund exploration. | Norden Crown completed Phase I (~400 line km) of UAV airborne magnetic geophysics over its Burfjord copper-gold project in Norway and plans Phase II in coming weeks. The results aim to refine and prioritize drill targets, building on prior high-grade copper intercepts from earlier drilling. Exploration continues via a Boliden-funded option. | Mower County authorities report multiple incidents of copper theft and damage to EDF Renewable Services wind turbines. Five turbines were damaged and copper wire stolen, with eight additional turbines damaged. Estimated damage for eight turbines is about $38,000, and high-voltage conditions make wire removal dangerous. Police request public help identifying vehicles. | Chile’s mining minister said the country needs about $150 billion in investment to nearly double copper output by 2050, supported by a new mining blueprint aimed at boosting traceability, reducing water use and shaping future royalties. Chile’s target depends on investment approvals for 49 projects. | Panama has begun talks with First Quantum’s local unit to renegotiate the contract for the Cobre Panama copper mine, aiming for higher royalties and stronger environmental conditions. The government indicates a “completely new” deal and possible operator change if no agreement is reached, creating near-term uncertainty for copper supply.

news_summary: Chile’s mining minister said about $150 billion is needed for a blueprint to expand “green copper” production, aiming for record output targets and requiring large project approvals and investment. The plan targets environmental measures and traceability, with current projects and royalties/regulatory constraints influencing investor expectations for copper supply. | Battery Mineral Resources announced encouraging initial assays from drilling at its Punitaqui copper-gold mine in Chile, including copper grades up to 2.06% over 9m (and other intercepts). Three drills are active and the company expects resumption of copper production mid-2022, supporting bullish copper sentiment. | Blackstone Minerals reported assay results from its maiden drilling at the Ban Khoa disseminated sulfide prospect within the Ta Khoa nickel–copper–PGE project in northern Vietnam. Highlights included 147m of disseminated nickel sulfide and potential to extend operations and complement nearby mining, supporting optimism for nickel and copper resources. | Battery Mineral Resources reports positive initial drill core assays from the Punitaqui complex in Chile, including intercepts such as 9m at 2.06% copper and other zones grading above 1% copper. Punitaqui is slated for production resumption, and these results may support near-term copper supply expectations.

news_summary: Lumina Copper agreed with workers at Chile’s Caserones mine on a three-year contract, ending an almost month-long strike that began in early August. The deal followed mediation and wage negotiations. Caserones produced about 127,000 tons of copper in 2020, making the strike an immediate local supply risk. | Battery Mineral Resources reports early drill core assay results at its newly acquired Punitaqui copper complex in Chile and outlines plans to revise resources and restart the mill around mid-2022. The program confirms copper grades near 2% in intersections, but current details suggest no immediate output change. | SMM’s roundup highlights labor and contract developments for major overseas copper producers. Chile’s Andina strike was ended via new union agreements, and Codelco reached an early bargaining deal for El Teniente. Panama is negotiating royalties and environmental terms for First Quantum’s Cobre Panama, supporting expected supply stability. | SMM data show China copper cathode output fell 1.3% MoM in August to 819,400 mt, driven by power curtailment and some maintenance, while tighter domestic scrap supply and sharply lower RCs squeezed smelter profits. September output is forecast to rise 1.6% MoM, as maintenance winds down and some plants ramp up. | A Guinea coup attempt has raised fears of disrupted bauxite exports from the West African nation, where major miners operate. Since bauxite is a key input for alumina and aluminium, the uncertainty supported a rally in aluminium prices to a 10-year high. Iron-ore project timelines were also questioned. | OZ Minerals halted operations at its Prominent Hill copper-gold mine in South Australia after a contractor was killed in an underground machinery incident. The stoppage follows police and Safework SA investigations. Prominent Hill is a major part of the company’s copper production, raising short-term supply concerns for copper.

news_summary: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service rejected a request by Hudbay Minerals to remove critical-habitat protections for jaguars tied to the proposed Rosemont open-pit copper mine in Arizona. The decision continues opposition to mining in the Santa Rita Mountains, potentially delaying mine development and copper supply. | Chakana reported significant drilling intercepts at its Soledad property in Peru, including 46 meters grading 5.64% copper, along with elevated silver and gold values. The company provided an update on resource drilling. The news may improve the project’s long-term potential but is unlikely to materially move near-term copper prices. | Brigadier Gold reported additional trench and channel sampling at its Picachos project, expanding a copper-silver discovery and adding high-grade gold-silver-copper results at the historic La Gloria mine. Reported copper assays (e.g., ~0.54% Cu over 0.72 m) may modestly bolster copper sentiment. | Camino Minerals begins discovery drilling at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru after receiving permits. The company targets extensions of the Lidia Zone and reports early trace chalcopyrite and alteration assemblages, with historical chip samples up to 5.1% copper. It is an exploration update without near-term production impact. | Camino Corp. announced it has started core discovery drilling at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru, targeting mineralization extensions at the Lidia Zone. The company notes trace chalcopyrite and historic rock samples with up to 5.1% copper, suggesting potential for new copper discoveries. | Arcadia Minerals reported grab-sampling results from its Karibib Copper-Gold Project in Namibia, confirming mineralisation across a 20 km by 2 km structural feature. Skarn and vein samples returned average copper grades of about 4.32% and 1.94%, supporting upside to copper prospectivity. It remains exploration, not production.

news_summary: SMM data show China’s major copper inventories fell 700 mt from Monday to 126,900 mt, driven mainly by declines in Shanghai and Guangdong. Shipments may not quickly lift warehouse stocks due to downstream routing, while smelter maintenance keeps arrivals weak. Inventories are expected to decline further ahead of Mid-Autumn Festival. | SMM reports August operating rates for domestic copper cathode rod producers rose 3.06 percentage points MoM, despite weaker orders from Yangzhou lockdowns. The key driver was copper falling below scrap prices, improving the cathode-vs-scrap spread and encouraging downstream purchases and restocking of cathode rods, even as inventory willingness stayed low. | Nickel prices surged to record highs in Shanghai and a seven-year peak in London, driven by strong demand and dwindling inventories. It also reports aluminium hitting its highest since 2008 on China and Guinea supply worries, while copper rose on improving smelter output and byproduct returns, keeping sentiment firmly bullish. | Nickel surged to record highs in Shanghai and above London’s seven-year peak as inventories dwindled and demand stayed strong, with refined nickel expected to remain in short supply in September. The same piece also highlights elevated prices in aluminium (supply worries in China and Guinea) and copper, reinforcing a broad bullish tone.

This blocks lower-grade Category Seven and some Category Six scrap from entering Malaysia. With limited substitution sources, scrap supply available for China tightens further, likely supporting firmer copper prices. | Rob McEwen discusses inflation concerns and expects stronger prices in gold and silver, but he also turns bullish on copper. He references McEwen Copper’s vehicle and a US$80 million financing tied to the Los Azules copper property, suggesting improving outlook and potential upward sentiment for copper. | EMX Royalty Corp. said it expects an initial quarterly after-tax royalty payment of about US$974,000 from its 0.418% net smelter return interest in Chile’s Caserones copper-molybdenum mine. The payment is based on Q2 distributions for copper and molybdenum production, reflecting royalty cash flow. | US Copper reports that evacuation orders from the Dixie Fire are being lifted, but access to its Moonlight-Superior copper property remains restricted. Although the drill rig wasn’t damaged, the drill contractor is moving off-site, power was interrupted, and drilling is unlikely to resume fully this season, delaying assay progress. | Jiangxi Copper and MCC are reportedly working to revive stalled development of Afghanistan’s Mes Aynak, a large high-grade copper deposit, by negotiating amid Taliban rule. The project remains constrained by security instability and lack of substantial construction, but efforts to restart mining improve forward copper supply expectations. | Latin Metals reports additional rock chip sampling results from its Lacsha project in Peru, highlighting broad copper and molybdenum mineralization (e.g., 72m at 0.2% Cu) and expansion of the mineralized footprint across Lacsha North and Lacsha South. news_summary: Amex Exploration reported follow-up drill results at its Perron project QF Zone, including 1.93% Cu over 8.0 m (and a higher-grade core). The company says results replicate prior discovery grades and plans additional drilling. The news is copper-geology oriented but lacks near-term supply implications. | Copper prices were flat near a one-month low as Evergrande jitters fueled market sell-offs and heightened fears about construction demand for copper. Three-month LME copper weakened through September. Risk aversion also weighed on other industrial metals, with LME aluminium and nickel edging lower. | CAVU reports 2021 drilling at its Hopper copper-gold project in Yukon, with a 16% expansion of copper-gold skarn footprint and porphyry-type mineralization intersected in one hole. Results are expected from a lab soon, but no production or quantified copper supply is provided, limiting immediate price effects. | Copper prices rebounded on the LME as investors reduced concerns that Evergrande’s troubles could spill into the global economy. Risk assets stabilized, helping three-month copper rise 0.7% and recover toward $9,000. LME aluminium also gained and nickel edged up, suggesting a mild risk-on bid. | World Copper signed a definitive agreement to acquire Cardero Resource, which owns the advanced-stage Zonia copper-oxide project in Arizona. The deal is all-stock, with an amended royalty option and debt assumption. It may improve project prospects, but offers no immediate copper supply or demand shift. | The piece argues that supply tightness is keeping copper at record levels and may worsen into a sizable 2021 deficit, supporting high prices that squeeze renewable project returns. It also notes that high prices are spurring new investment and mine expansions (e.g., Udokan, Codelco), which could add supply later. | ICSG reported global copper mine output up 4.9% in H1, with refined production also rising, led by higher output at Indonesia’s Grasberg. However, demand signals are mixed: China’s Caixin PMI contracted, Evergrande risks raised concerns, and China’s copper imports through July declined versus June.

news_summary: A Chilean court suspended a ban on BHP’s Cerro Colorado copper mine pumping groundwater for 90 days while the company prepares a new operational continuity plan. The court limits extraction to 54 liters per second and notes pumping ends if approvals for the environmental plan aren’t obtained. | London copper rebounded 3.1% as relief spread after Evergrande’s main unit said it will pay some bond interest due Sept. 23, easing default worries. The PBOC also injected liquidity as expiring loans rolled over, supporting risk sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel rose alongside copper. | Copper jumped about 3% on the LME after relief that Evergrande would pay bond interest, easing concerns about China’s property stress spilling into the broader economy. Risk sentiment was further supported by PBOC liquidity injection. Aluminum and nickel also rose, reflecting a broad bullish metals move. | Copper Mountain Mining said it has installed and begun commissioning Ball Mill 3 at its southern British Columbia Copper Mountain mine. The expansion increases milling capacity to 45,000 tonnes per day from 40,000. Electrical commissioning started, slurry is expected next month, and ramp to full production is expected this year. | Aguila American Gold says it has staked a 100% interest in the Lida copper project in Nevada’s Walker Lane mineral belt and will begin mapping, sampling, and geophysical surveys. The release discusses historical copper oxide mineralization and an IP anomaly but does not imply near-term copper output. | Peru’s Las Bambas is reported to need US$2bn to maintain its copper output. The funding requirement raises near-term concerns about whether production can be sustained, which could lead to tighter copper supply expectations and support higher copper prices if investors price in potential disruptions. | Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin reporting notes copper premiums rising in South Korea and Italy, with the EQ premium jumping, suggesting firmer physical copper pricing. Separately, it discusses procedural delays/corrections to European low-carbon aluminium differentials and some Aluminium 6063 extrusion billet premium assessments, which are more about benchmark publication than fundamentals. | Copper surged nearly 4% after markets cheered Evergrande’s agreement to pay interest on a domestic bond and after the central bank injected cash, easing worries about contagion to the economy. LME aluminium and nickel also rose sharply, indicating a broad improvement in near-term sentiment and restocking expectations. | Hudbay announced an extension of mineralization at its Copper World project in Arizona. While the news is primarily resource-focused rather than immediate production, it can still improve market expectations for future copper supply and support sentiment toward copper prices in the near term. | Hudbay announced new drilling results at its Copper World copper project in Arizona, identifying three additional deposits and confirming higher confidence in existing ones. Multiple shallow intercepts with grades above ~1% copper were reported, along with plans for an inferred resource estimate and preliminary economic assessment. | Universal Copper Ltd. completed the purchase of 100% of BA Copper Corporation, adding 4,631 hectares contiguous to its Poplar Copper Project. The company will issue 4.5 million shares and advance a planned 2021 Phase 1 drill program to expand the copper resource area. No near-term production changes are mentioned.

news_summary: BHP is reportedly in early talks with Ivanhoe Mines about potentially buying into Western Foreland, an exploration area in the Democratic Republic of the Congo near Ivanhoe’s Kamoa-Kakula copper mine. The sources stress talks are preliminary and no deal is guaranteed, with other bidders interested too. | London copper, aluminium and nickel prices fell as the U.S. dollar edged toward a one-year high, making dollar-priced metals more expensive. Tepid China demand due to public holiday and risk-off sentiment from Evergrande’s debt problems also weighed, with traders looking to upcoming U.S. payrolls for Fed taper timing signals. | Codelco’s Spence mine in Chile completed a $52m upgrade, including a new sulfide concentrator plant. The project used PENETRON ADMIX-treated concrete to meet stringent permeability needs under aggressive ore-processing chemicals. The expansion is set to enable copper extraction before year-end, potentially supporting near-term copper supply expectations. | BHP is reportedly in early talks with Ivanhoe Mines to acquire part of the Western Foreland exploration area in the DRC near the producing Kamoa-Kakula licence. While no deal is guaranteed, the potential for expanded DRC copper exposure is supportive. BHP also plans to supply nickel sulphate for an EV battery ecosystem. | Talon Metals announced assay results from 13 new drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Minnesota, highlighting shallow, thick nickel-copper sulphide intervals including grades up to 3.50% Ni and 1.40% Cu. The company expects these results to add volume to future resources, not near-term output. | Talon Metals reported assays from 13 new holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Minnesota, showing high-grade nickel-copper sulphide mineralization in the CGO East area outside the current resource. Results highlight wide, shallow intervals and potential resource expansion, but no immediate supply or trading impacts are described. | Bam Bam Resources says reverse-circulation drilling is underway at its Majuba Hill porphyry copper project in Nevada, targeting infill and expansion of oxide copper mineralization mined from the historic underground Majuba Hill Mine. QA/QC and assay details are provided. The update is supportive for near-term copper sentiment. | World Copper reported exploration results from surface rock sampling at the Escalones project, confirming two copper-porphyry targets at the northern targets. The Rio Negro zone showed multiple samples above 0.5% Cu and several above 1% Cu, supporting plans for future drilling, though results are surface-based and exploratory. | South Africa’s parliament buildings reportedly had copper wire and copper pipes stripped during renovations, with contractors suspected in the NCOP area. Officials dispute parts of the Marks Building report. The incident is security-related and appears limited in scope, with no evidence of broader supply impact for copper markets. | Bam Bam Resources says reverse-circulation drilling has started at its Majuba Hill porphyry copper project in Nevada, targeting in-fill and expansion of known oxide copper mineralization. Samples are being analyzed for copper and other metals under QA/QC procedures, potentially improving confidence in the copper resource outlook. | Comex Copper for October delivery settled 1.05% lower at $4.2020, down 4.45 cents per pound. The contract snapped a two-session winning streak, falling in four of the past six sessions and remaining off recent highs after a strong run earlier in 2021.

news_summary: Cannindah Resources says it has achieved a large “monster-sized” copper drill hit in Queensland, with only half the hole assayed so far. The discovery news can lift expectations for future copper supply potential, though incomplete assay limits certainty and reduces immediate impact on broader copper pricing. | Tinka Resources announced high-grade copper results from initial surface sampling at its Silvia NW copper-gold skarn target in Peru, including trench and rock chip values up to 12.3% copper. The find is near Antamina and remains under exploration, supporting bullish sentiment for copper prospects despite no production impact. | Copper Fox provides an update on its 100%-owned Van Dyke oxide copper project in Arizona. Work focuses on a conceptual hydrogeological model for permitting, including groundwater level/flow measurements and comparing historical water data. Preliminary core inspections confirm copper mineralization, with planned logging and sampling to estimate grade and extent. | Torq Resources commenced its first drill program at the Margarita IOCG copper-gold project in northern Chile. The initial phase targets copper-gold sulphide mineralization beneath surface copper oxide zones, with drilling planned around defined southern and northern anomalies. The news is an exploration step, not production. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced drilling assay results from its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario, reporting zones with nickel and copper grades and noting conductive sulphide likely extends below intersections based on TDEM interpretation. Additional drilling assays are pending, and no near-term production impact is provided. | Pan Global Resources announced new drilling results at its Escacena, Spain La Romana copper/tin target, reporting expanded continuity of near-surface high-grade copper mineralization and commencing new IP targets. While no production change is stated, the positive exploration update may modestly boost copper sentiment over coming weeks. | Thiess was named preferred mining-services contractor for Austral Resources’ Anthill Copper Project in Queensland, with negotiations toward a three-year deal to run two mining fleets and support copper production. Austral plans to start mining this year and produce about 40,000 tonnes of LME Grade A copper cathode over four years. | Bam Bam Resources reports sampling of previously unsampled historic drill core at the DeSoto area of its Majuba Hill copper project in Nevada. Surface rock chips and sampled intervals show elevated copper grades, with assays submitted for analysis. The update is exploration-focused and unlikely to materially move near-term copper benchmarks. | Orestone Mining announced an MT geophysical survey at its Captain gold-copper project in British Columbia, outlining a large conductive sulphide-rich target and validating the company’s porphyry-style model. The company expects next drilling to begin, but no resource or production update was provided. | Tinka Resources announced high-grade copper-gold surface sampling at its Silvia NW copper-gold skarn target in Peru, including copper assays up to about 12.3%. Results cover a 400m by 100m trend and show copper-gold correlation, suggesting strong prospectivity. This is early exploration, not production or reserve updates. | Copper Fox provided an update on its 100% owned Van Dyke oxide copper project in Arizona. Work focuses on a conceptual hydrogeological model for permitting, including groundwater level/flow measurements and review of historical water data. Preliminary inspection of Azurite drill cores confirms copper mineralization and planned sampling/logging. | Copper is described as gaining, with investors weighing improving growth momentum and China’s return. The framing suggests expectations for stronger demand and risk appetite are supporting copper prices in the near term, rather than highlighting supply disruptions or policy changes specific to copper.

news_summary: The piece links China’s property slowdown and power rationing to weaker copper demand, with downstream impacts outweighing smelter supply effects. It also says aluminium demand has turned down in China as plate/sheet producers cut or halt, implying prices may ease. Global aluminium may still be tight, but near-term demand risks dominate. | Credit Suisse warns that China’s copper demand turned negative in June amid the property downturn, and power rationing will likely hit downstream fabricators more than smelters, worsening demand contraction. It also expects China aluminium prices to ease as restrictions reduce downstream output despite continued smelter limits. | SMM reviews post-holiday conditions: LME three-month copper is largely stable, with swings driven by US PMI/dollar and Powell’s comments. Attention centers on upcoming US payrolls, power-rationing impacts on inventories, and potentially a soft short squeeze before SHFE delivery if inventory rises slightly. Scrap imports may increase. | SMM says China’s major trading-market copper inventories rose by 20,900 mt from Sept 30 to 111,000 mt after the National Day holiday. Increases were led by Shanghai and Guangdong. Power rationing continued to affect smelters and processors, with limited imported inflows; demand should improve slightly, so inventories may ease next week. | Playfair Mining reports successful drilling at its RKV copper project in Norway, moving from the Rødalen target to a second target at Storboren. Four short holes intersected sulphide-bearing amphibolite and strong MMI copper anomalies, suggesting potential copper mineralization. Samples are pending analysis; no production or concrete supply impact is announced. | Mongolia’s Justice Minister warned Rio Tinto that patience may run out unless it makes progress on the Oyu Tolgoi copper mine. The government cited delays and cost blowouts, and said it was surprised by Rio’s response to an independent July report disputing reasons for a two-year schedule slip and $1.45 billion overrun.

news_summary: China’s September copper imports rose 3% month-on-month after five months of declines, as delayed cargoes cleared port congestion following eased COVID curbs. However, Q3 imports fell to a two-year low. Shanghai bonded refined inventories dropped sharply, and copper concentrate imports increased. | Freeport has started building a $3bn copper smelter in Gresik, Indonesia, targeting 600,000t/year of copper cathode and up to 1.7Mt/year of concentrate processing. The project, near existing operations, is scheduled to start in late 2023 or early 2024 and would shift value-added output to Indonesia. | Freeport Indonesia began building a $3bn copper smelter and precious metals refinery in East Java with 1.7 million tonnes/year concentrate capacity, producing copper cathodes, pipes and wires. The project, in a Gresik industrial zone, is linked to Freeport’s Grasberg supply and targets commissioning in 2024, supporting copper downstream growth expectations. | Nobel Resources announced it signed an option agreement to acquire 100% of the La Salvadora IOCG copper-gold project in Chile, including scheduled option payments and a future NSR royalty. The release highlights copper mineralized zones and adjacent operations, but provides no near-term production impact, limiting broader price effects. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. reported additional drilling results at its 100% owned Kenbridge nickel-copper project in Ontario, including strong Ni grades over 25.5 metres (with associated copper) and additional zone intercepts. TDEM surveys support conductive zone continuity, guiding further drilling toward down-dip and along-strike extensions. | Tartisan Nickel reported additional drilling results from its Kenbridge Ni-Cu project in Ontario, including 25.5 metres of 1.13% Ni with 0.61% Cu, plus higher-grade nickel-copper intervals. Interpretation is based on TDEM-conductive zones and supports ongoing down-dip/along-strike drilling, aiding sentiment for nickel and copper. | Norilsk says Botswana has not implemented an agreed settlement and the dispute over liquidation and sale of BCL’s assets remains in London arbitration. Observers warn PNRB’s plan to acquire and restart the Selebi nickel-copper-cobalt assets may fall through without a resolution, affecting near-term supply outlooks. | District Copper announced a fall induced polarization geophysical survey over its Copper Keg project in British Columbia and completed a one-week mapping and geochemistry program. Historic interpretations suggest a buried porphyry copper system; grab samples showed copper values up to 124.5 ppm. It remains early exploration with limited immediate market impact. | The article argues copper should rebound, pointing to declining LME inventories and a potential supply squeeze after copper fell on COVID-19 Delta-driven economic worries and weaker China output. It expects EV and renewable grid growth to raise demand, supporting a near-term rally in copper prices. | Fathom Nickel reported preliminary interpretation of 2021 soil geochemistry from its Albert Lake property, showing multiple anomalous Ni zones with associated Cu and other elements, and noted drilling is ongoing with assays pending. It also received DTC eligibility approval for OTC trading, mainly impacting investor access rather than metal fundamentals. | Bitterroot Resources reported assay results from two holes at its LM Project, stating massive and semi-massive sulphides are enriched in nickel and copper, with disseminated mineralization in one hole. The company continues drilling toward additional targets, but no near-term production or supply disruption is indicated. | Copper output at Taseko Mines’ Gibraltar facility near Williams Lake rose 29% this quarter to over 34 million pounds. The company has mobilized drilling to continue spring expansion and will investigate geophysical anomalies, while operations transition smoothly to the Gibraltar Pit. Management cites strong copper costs and $50M credit acquisition for the year ahead.

Sales are planned after inspections. news_summary: SMM reports Codelco plans a 2022 benchmark premium for long-term China copper cathode contracts at $105/mt (up from $88/mt). However, import demand is sluggish, Yangshan premiums have fallen, and traders show low acceptance. Current warrant/bill premiums near ~$63/mt lag the benchmark, suggesting bearish copper sentiment. | Peru’s Antamina copper mine suspended operations after a rural community strike blocked the road used to transport output via a pipeline, with protests escalating into violence at other sites. Antamina said it cannot operate without restored order, risking a short-term copper supply cut. | Murchison Minerals begins an inaugural helicopter-supported drill program at Quebec’s HPM project, targeting the PYC nickel-copper-cobalt prospect. The company aims to test subsurface extensions of earlier surface mineralization (reported Ni and Cu grades via NiEq/CuEq). Results are pending, with no immediate production or supply impact described. | Peru’s Antamina copper-zinc mine has suspended operations amid protests. The shutdown reduces near-term copper supply availability from the mine, which can tighten balances and raise expectations for higher copper prices in the coming days as markets react to potential delays in output. | Bullet Exploration reports completion of phase-one fieldwork on its Copper Canyon copper property, including prospecting, mapping, and geochemical sampling, plus magnetics surveying. It also completed assessment work to keep its Anita property in good standing. Results are described as encouraging, but no production or major supply change is indicated. | The U.S. and EU agreed to ease trade tensions: the U.S. will lift tariffs on some EU steel and aluminum imports starting Dec. 1 via a duty-free quota, while the EU removes retaliatory tariffs on U.S. goods. The partial tariff relief should support aluminum supply and prices near term.

news_summary: Copper prices edged up as low LME exchange inventories provided support after steep recent declines. LME three-month copper rose 0.8% and Shanghai’s December contract climbed 0.6% after two weeks of declines. The planned lifting of Peru’s Antamina blockade further supported sentiment, alongside generally firmer aluminium prices. | Glencore’s Q3 production report cited lower mining grades across business units. Self-owned copper output fell 4% year-on-year to 895,500 tonnes, with weaker copper by-products. Nickel from owned sources declined 13% to 71,100 tonnes due to maintenance and operational problems, while other asset notes were largely unrelated. | SMM reports copper inventories have fallen sharply, with LME cancelled warrants rising and cash-to-three-month contango surging, tightening apparent supply and previously lifting copper prices. However, coal price limits, macro fears, power rationing on downstream demand, and sluggish orders are expected to keep copper volatile and perform poorly in November. | Max Resource reports finding a 3.7-km high-grade copper-silver zone at its CONEJO project in northeastern Colombia. If confirmed, the discovery can improve expectations for future copper supply. Such positive exploration/resource news usually supports copper sentiment in the near term, though timing and output remain uncertain.

news_summary: Ero Copper reported Q3 2021 copper production of 10,057 tonnes from the MCSA complex and completion of mill maintenance to support higher throughput in Q4. It also announced an optimized feasibility study for the Boa Esperança project that doubles life-of-mine copper output, reinforcing growth-oriented copper supply expectations. | Ero Copper reported Q3 2021 results including 10,057 tonnes of copper production at its MCSA complex, completion of mill maintenance, and guidance to achieve the high end of 2021 copper-in-concentrate output. The company attributed this to early warning and slope monitoring, with the area quarantined. No production disruption was reported. | Nevada Copper announced a marketed public offering and related private placement, raising about C$75 million, to fund ramp-up of its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine and repay related bridge loans. The release also details credit-facility deferrals/extensions. news_summary: SMM reports copper plate/sheet and strip producers’ operating rates fell to 74.19% in October as power rationing tightened after September energy controls, expanding to more provinces. Small and mid-sized firms faced production hindrance amid high copper prices and maintenance. November operating rates are expected to rise to 75.51% with easing rationing intensity and slightly better downstream demand. | SMM reports copper concentrate supply shocks (roadblocks in Peru, Chilean port high tides), China power rationing causing smelter suspensions, and tighter inventories (falling SHFE stocks, rising LME cancelled warrants) alongside a weaker import window early in 2021. These shortages and squeeze dynamics lifted copper treatment charges and Chinese premiums, supporting bullish copper pricing. | Japan and the U.S. agreed to begin talks on additional U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminium imports from Japan. No specific measures were set in the meeting, but the U.S. said talks could lead to easing tariffs, a longstanding irritant. This may support aluminium prices in the near term. | Solus Advanced Materials will restart a previously owned copper foil plant site in Quebec, Canada, with 60,000 tons annual capacity. The company plans to convert it into a battery foil production line for North American customers from battery and auto industries, aiming for mass production by 2024. | A proposed 211-mile industrial access road to Alaska’s Ambler mining district is described as enabling extraction of over 43 million tons of copper (and zinc). The report emphasizes environmental and Native community opposition and notes the Biden administration has not interrupted approvals, but it doesn’t indicate imminent production start. | Copper prices fell as China’s property sector showed weakness, reducing expectations for metals demand. Coal prices dropped, easing smelter energy costs, but a stronger U.S. dollar lifted metal prices for non-dollar buyers. The LME warehouse squeeze eased. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same risk backdrop. | Cordoba Minerals provided updates on its San Matias project in Colombia, including expected completion of a pre-feasibility study and submission of a mining technical work plan permit application, alongside ongoing environmental impact assessment work. The company is advancing the Alacran copper-gold-silver deposit toward construction.

news_summary: Copper prices rose 0.5% on the LME and edged up on the SHFE after improved risk sentiment following a Biden-Xi summit. The meeting supported wider market confidence, with the yuan strengthening. In the same snapshot, nickel was up while aluminium and zinc fell, showing mixed effects across metals. | Solus Advanced Materials reacquired its Quebec copper foil plant with capacity of 60,000 tons of copper foil for lithium-ion battery negative electrodes. The firm plans investments to expand the facility and target mass production in 2024, aiming to grow exposure to North America’s EV market. | Power Nickel started a ~4,000-metre drilling program at its Nisk project to expand a historic nickel-copper mineralization and produce a new NI 43-101 resource estimate. The company expects assay results in January and discusses potential nickel sulphate for EV batteries, but provides no new production or assay data. | Battery Mineral Resources reports encouraging San Andres drill results at Chile’s Punitaqui copper project, including multiple intercepts around ~0.7–1.4% copper and wider zones like 28.1m at 0.98% Cu. The company highlights progress toward restarting mine production mid-to-late 2022, potentially supporting copper supply expectations. | Battery Mineral Resources reported assay results from the San Andres drill program at its Punitaqui copper project in Chile, with several intercepts grading around ~0.5% to 1.4%+ copper over multiple meters. The company also cites progress toward a mid-to-late-2022 restart, supporting bullish copper sentiment. | Palladium One provided updates on its Tyko copper-nickel exploration project, including geophysical EM surveys, additional exploration permit applications, and expectations to resume drilling after permits are received. The release also cites recent high-grade Ni and Cu intercepts used in Ni-equivalent calculations, but offers no near-term production impact. | Palladium One provides updates on its Tyko copper-nickel project, including ongoing EM surveys, new exploration permit applications, and plans to schedule drilling after permits are received. It reiterates earlier drill intercept grades (Ni and Cu) and notes nickel-equivalent calculations, but no immediate production or supply impact is stated.

news_summary: Vale has ramped down its Copper Cliff Smelter into a “hot” standby state for two weeks to optimize concentrate stock levels after an incident halted production at the Totten Mine. The move pauses ore smelting temporarily, with refinery operations continuing and no customer impact expected in the article. | Cyprium Metals announced an updated Mineral Resource estimate for its Nifty copper deposit, reporting higher contained copper, including a larger heap-leachable component. The company says the increase supports its Nifty Heap Leach Restart Study and future SX-EW cathode potential. The update is project-focused rather than supply-impacting immediately. | SPC Nickel announced staking of a large land package covering Nunavut’s Muskox Ni-Cu-PGM intrusion and acquisition of an extensive historical geoscience database. The release emphasizes future evaluation and potential for feeder-dyke mineralization, but it does not change near-term production, inventories, or trade flows for nickel or copper. | BHP’s executive said a decarbonised world will require much higher metal supply, projecting nearly double copper demand and about four times nickel demand over the next 30 years. The comments were presented as modelling results supporting the long-run investment case, potentially boosting short-term sentiment. | Kodiak Copper temporarily paused its 2021 drilling program at the MPD copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia after an evacuation order for Merritt due to flooding and related road closures. news_summary: The report says market calm has returned to the LME after last month’s action to protect short holders. The LME cash premium for copper collapsed from an extreme US$1,103.50/t before the intervention to about US$15.50/t at Tuesday’s close, indicating normalization in the copper market. | SMM reports global copper cathode inventories have fallen 56.8% since Q2 2021 to 519,000 mt by Nov 12, alongside strong LME backwardation reflecting tight supply. It cautions that “resource exhaustion” is unlikely as smelter output should rebound after power rationing, with a potential inventory pivot in December. | Copperstone Resources’ Q3 2021 interim report says Sweden confirmed the Viscaria exploitation concession and the company is scaling up plans to reopen the mine in Kiruna. It highlights drilling positives, plans for higher production and concentrator capacity, and emphasizes copper demand/supply tightness. | SMM says domestic bonded copper inventories fell 9,700 mt to 206,500 mt by Nov 19, including declines in Shanghai and Guangdong zones. With the import window reopened and invoice restrictions boosting domestic spot premiums to a record 2,200 yuan/mt, importers transferred goods from bonded warehouses, tightening supply and supporting prices. | SMM reports Chinese copper inventories fell 4,800 mt to 79,500 mt by Nov 19, with declines in Guangdong and Jiangsu and first time below 80,000 mt this year. Late-week import arrivals slowed, though the import window may reopen next week. Overall inventory is expected to rise only marginally, supporting copper. | DW reports that Chile benefits because higher worldwide copper prices lift revenues from its mining sector. It notes Chile’s position as the world’s largest copper producer and highlights the financial gains to public coffers, including due to state ownership in major mining. | Korea’s SKC plans to invest $761 million to build its first European copper foil plant in Poland. Produced by its battery-focused subsidiary SK Nexilis, the facility will make copper foil for electric vehicle batteries, indicating expanded output and potentially stronger copper demand expectations. | SKC finalized plans with the Polish government to build a copper foil manufacturing facility in Stalowa Wola, targeting Europe’s EV battery supply chain. Copper foil is used as anode current collectors in lithium-ion cells. The KRW 900bn investment aims for 50,000 tons/year capacity, with construction starting next year and completion in 2024.

news_summary: Cyprium Metals reports assay results from final diamond holes at Nanadie Well, highlighting extensive copper sulphide mineralisation with several high-grade intercepts and confirming the deposit remains open. The company also notes elevated nickel values associated with some copper intervals, but provides no near-term production or supply impact. | SMM data show Chinese copper cathode inventories fell 300 mt week-on-week to 76,500 mt, the lowest level this year. The drawdown occurred in Guangdong and Tianjin as copper was shipped to higher-premium East China (Shanghai, Jiangsu). With premiums normalizing and import invoicing easing, inventories may tick up slightly by Friday. | SMM reports spot copper cathode premiums rising above 2,000 yuan/mt as social inventory falls and customs tariffs are suspended. Downstream orders weaken under high premiums, while copper rod producers facing bearish shipments and higher finished-stock levels suspend or cut output. Persistent high premiums could further lower copper rod operating rates. | Nickel prices recently fell due to pessimism on real-estate demand, then rebounded as sentiment improved. Lower coal prices and weaker demand failed to drive sharp nickel losses. Discounts to NPI narrowed, nickel sulphate demand stayed steady, inventory is low, and customs tax-control issues may raise spot premiums. | Copper Fox Metals’ CEO discusses the Schaft Creek copper-molybdenum-gold-silver project and its preliminary economic assessment. The company highlights efforts to reduce processing costs and improve recoveries, and notes project economics are highly sensitive to copper price movements and exchange rates. No near-term supply shock or policy action is mentioned. | Mosaic Minerals reports drilling intersected a 70-meter anomalous nickel zone (Pike Nickel East) and nearly 80 meters of nickeliferous mineralization, extending a nickel corridor by about 3.4 km and prompting plans for further drilling. Copper is also mentioned on the property, but aluminium appears only in risk wording. | Copper prices rose following positive Xi-Biden virtual talks, with a weaker dollar and improved investor risk sentiment. The commentary frames the move as a potential rebound from the prior month’s decline and highlights copper producers likely to benefit. The piece also references a feasibility study using high base copper assumptions. | Norilsk Nickel’s board recommended an interim dividend for nine months of 2021 totaling RUB 232.84 bn, with EGM set for Dec. 27 and dividend record date Jan. 14. The company is a major producer of nickel and copper, but the announcement does not change metal supply or demand expectations. | Ridgestone Mining announced a maiden RC drill program (about 14 holes/2,700 m) at its El Cobre high-grade copper property in Sonora, Mexico. The company cites past and recent surface sampling with copper grades up to 3.5%, targeting down-dip extensions under historical workings. | Cyprium Metals reported extensive copper sulphide intersections across all diamond drill holes at Nanadie Well, with mineralisation open along strike and across strike. The results will support a mineral resource estimate for a scoping study and preliminary metallurgical work. Elevated nickel and palladium values were found with key copper intervals.

news_summary: Universal Copper reports progress at its Poplar copper exploration project, including drilling, a mineral resource estimate update, and additional target work plus property acquisitions. It also reiterates that rising global demand for copper (EVs and electronics) should support higher copper prices, but no immediate supply or production change is announced. | Guangdong copper cathode inventories fell to 6,527 mt (below 10,000 mt), driving Guangdong spot premiums to multi-year highs on Nov 22. Elevated premiums led downstream rod plants to cut output: six halted production and operating rates dropped to 65.8%. Firms expect further rate declines this week if premiums stay high. | White Metal Resources says its partner Noronex is drilling the DorWit Cu-Ag project in Namibia with two rigs and reports initial anomalous copper from the first 10 RC holes at Otjiwaru, including an intercept of 7.0 m at 0.5% Cu. Further drilling is ongoing at other targets. | DRC’s Kamoa-Kakula mine signed contracts to build a 500,000 t/yr blister copper smelter to process concentrate from its concentrators into blister copper. The project also includes a 10-year processing deal to route early-phase concentrate to local smelting. Expansion plans target over 800,000 t/yr concentrate supply. | The article says the approved U.S. infrastructure bill will fund electrification, transit and EV charging stations, expected to increase demand for industrial metals. It links this to copper and mentions battery materials such as nickel. It also highlights Kutcho Copper’s feasibility study as supportive for future copper supply.

news_summary: Morningstar cuts iron ore assumptions but lifts average copper price expectations, citing potential US infrastructure-driven demand growth. Credit Suisse estimates the infrastructure bill could add 50–250ktpa copper demand and see supply risks easing. Nickel pig iron in China is said to have softened after exchange nickel price declines. | Morningstar highlights US infrastructure stimulus as a potential demand tailwind for copper, estimating additional demand of 50–250ktpa. Separately, Credit Suisse notes nickel pig iron prices in China have softened after nickel declines on exchanges, with expectations of a surplus as production recovers—signaling mixed near-term pricing forces for both metals. | The U.S. and EU agreed to replace Section 232 tariffs on EU steel (25%) and aluminum (10%) with tariff-rate quotas effective Jan. 1, 2022. Eligible EU products enter the U.S. duty-free up to quota volumes; above-quota shipments face remaining tariffs. Quota administration and exclusions rules add some uncertainty. | Zijin Mining approved internally the construction of a 500,000 mt/year copper smelter at the Kamoa-Kakula Copper Project in the DRC. The investment totals about $769 million, with $699 million for construction, funded from the project’s cash flow and planned over three years, boosting expectations for future copper processing capacity. | Indonesia’s president reiterated plans to halt exports of commodity raw materials, including suspending bauxite exports in 2022 and copper in 2023. The article argues short-term bauxite supply impact is limited because imports from other regions and large domestic port stocks provide about three months of feed. The policy targets alumina output, supporting aluminium-linked sentiment; copper effects are more future-oriented. | Indonesia’s president reiterated possible export bans: tin may stop in 2024, bauxite in 2022, and copper in 2023. SMM argues bauxite restrictions should have little short-term effect because inventories at ports are large and supply can be covered by other regions, limiting immediate aluminium disruption; copper effects are still pending assessment. | Surge Copper Corp. announced assay results from a drill hole at its Ootsa Property, including 422 metres grading 0.52% CuEq and 200 metres at 0.60% CuEq, plus ongoing work toward an updated resource estimate. Investors awaited remaining drill results and the updated resource; the release is copper-focused. | Rockcliff Metals reported phase-one drill results at its Copperman property in Manitoba, highlighting near-surface, high-grade copper mineralization (e.g., several intervals above ~2% CuEq). The company cites encouraging widths and VMS-hosted copper-zinc potential, with an ongoing drill program planned. | Zijin and Ivanhoe approved a $769m direct-to-blister copper smelter adjacent to the Kamoa-Kakula concentrators in the DRC. The 500,000 tpa facility is designed to process most Kamoa-Kakula concentrates and reduce reliance on third-party smelters. Construction is expected to take about three years. | Indonesia plans to stop exports of raw materials after its nickel ore shipment ban, aiming to shift trade toward semi-finished and finished goods to boost downstream investment. The explicit nickel restriction is the immediate driver, while copper export shutdown is mentioned as a next-step possibility; aluminium is not addressed.

news_summary: LME copper futures closed about 1.3% higher at an one-month high, supported by China’s plan to increase real estate credit lines and signs of tighter supply, with falling warehouse inventories and premiums for fast delivery. Nickel also rose more than 2%, with receipts falling sharply, indicating tighter availability. | SMM reports China spot premiums for 1# copper cathode have fallen about 85% to around 350 yuan/mt from a historical high of ~2,200 yuan/mt. SHFE backwardation weakened as well. Causes cited include slower domestic inventory drawdowns, easing of VAT invoice issues for imports, and reduced downstream processing amid weak terminal demand. | Rio Tinto’s CEO met Mongolia’s prime minister over unresolved issues threatening the expansion of Oyu Tolgoi, Mongolia’s major copper project. Delays, cost overruns, and investigations have led to potential contract renegotiations and possible restructuring. Uncertainty over timelines and financing may weigh on near-term copper supply expectations and sentiment. | Zijin approved a US$769 million investment for a smelter tied to its Kamoa-Kakula copper project in the DRC. The plant (blister copper) is intended to process more concentrate internally, reducing third-party smelter reliance. First phase output is underway, with construction expected to take about three years. | Indonesia plans to halt exports of raw materials as it moves toward downstream processing. After implementing a nickel ore shipment ban, President Widodo suggests bauxite export limits next year, copper exports later, and further restrictions thereafter. The policy aims to force exports of semi-finished/finished products. | Three Valley Copper closed a bought-deal equity offering to fund advancement and exploration of its Minera Tres Valles copper project in Chile, including plans for first caving operations in January 2022 and rising production in 2022. The release also cites supportive copper-price conditions and EV/infrastructure demand. | Puma Exploration reports progress on its transaction with Melius Metals to unlock value from Puma’s copper projects. Melius has advanced approvals, completed technical reports, funded additional activities, and started a 2,000-meter drilling program at the Chester copper deposit, with claims transferred to Puma. | Puma Exploration reported progress on its transaction with Melius Metals, granting Melius an option to acquire Puma’s copper projects. Melius completed technical reports, closed financings, and started a 2,000-meter drilling program at the Chester copper deposit, including additional payments and related corporate approvals for listing and financing.

news_summary: Nickel and other industrial metals rose as optimism about China’s property sector returned. The headline implies improved demand expectations for construction-related materials, providing near-term support to prices. By extension, the move is likely to affect copper and aluminium alongside nickel. | SMM reports that Xinxiang, Henan issued a red heavy-air-pollution warning, prompting local secondary copper rod plants to suspend production from Nov 25 to Nov 29. The temporary shutdown aims to curb pollution peaks and likely tightens short-term copper rod supply expectations, providing upward support for copper prices. | Peruvian opposition lawmakers submitted a bill to impeach President Pedro Castillo. The article notes Castillo had promised higher taxes on mining and oil, which previously alarmed investors about stagnant copper production. The impeachment effort is framed as a potential boon for miners, easing perceived policy risk for Peru’s copper market. | Forum Energy Metals reports final drill assay results from Rio Tinto’s 2021 program at the Janice Lake copper/silver project in Saskatchewan, highlighting expanded high-grade copper at Rafuse and related targets, plus copper boulder discoveries. With rising nickel demand for NCM cathodes, potential tightening supports expectations for higher nickel prices within weeks, though copper effects are less direct. | South32 will buy a 45% stake in Chile’s Sierra Gorda copper mine for $1.55bn upfront, plus up to $500m contingent payments, after KGHM Polska failed to exercise pre-emptive rights. The project is expected to produce about 180kt of copper this year, but closure is planned for early 2022. | Chile’s election could determine the fate of a proposed royalty tax reform that would base Chile’s copper royalty on output rather than profits and could rise up to 75% when copper prices exceed $4/lb. Industry warns this may force mine closures and cause investment delays, pressuring copper supply expectations.

news_summary: London copper eased as Asian markets turned risk-averse on renewed Sino-U.S. tensions. Reuters cited signs of a China copper inventory glut: ShFE stockpiles hit the highest since late October and the Chinese refined copper spot premium fell from a record. LME copper fell about 0.5% to $9,460/t. | Cochilco data cited by SMM show Codelco’s October copper output fell 9.9% YoY to 144,100 mt. BHP’s Escondida output dropped 15.2% to 84,700 mt, while Glencore/Anglo American’s Collahuasi rose 17.3% to 49,500 mt. | SMM data shows Guangdong copper cathode inventories at 5,900 mt on Dec 3, the lowest for the year and near historical lows. A smelter’s output is still recovering and import invoice issues limit arrivals, while downstream restocking resumed in December. Inventories likely stay low into late December, supporting copper prices. | SMM reported China’s domestic bonded copper inventories fell 5,700 mt to 183,500 mt by Dec 3, the eighth straight week of decline. Shanghai and Guangdong inventories also dropped. Warrant-related inflows were smaller and stronger customs quarantine plus slower port efficiency reduced incoming shipments, tightening near-term supply expectations. | HK-listed MMG said it will stop copper production from its Las Bambas mine in Peru by mid-December, citing unresolved demands from the Chumbivilcas community despite government meetings. The mine has faced prior shipment blockades, and concentrate stockpiles have risen, but output loss threatens near-term copper supply. | HSBC’s analyst argues copper demand is being driven by manufacturing, residential/construction, EVs, and renewable power, which require substantially more copper. On the supply side, Peru and Chile (large producers) face political/regulatory risks, keeping the market tight and potentially in deficit, supporting a bullish copper outlook.

news_summary: China’s imports of unwrought copper and copper semis rose 24.34% to 510,402.3 mt in November versus the prior month, according to customs data cited by SMM. While November imports were down 9.07% year on year, the month-on-month increase suggests improved demand/support for copper prices. | Zambia’s Project Roan is virtually complete, with commissioning starting in December and first commercial production expected in March 2022. Jubilee Metals says the project will deliver copper concentrate to the upgraded Sable Refinery from Q1 2022, adding 10,000 t/y of copper units and planning more concentrating capacity in Northern Zambia. | Cornish Metals announced new drilling at the United Downs copper–tin project, intersecting multiple zones of high-grade copper mineralisation beneath the historic United Mines. The company says high-grade copper has now been confirmed over a 500m strike length and remains open along strike and at depth, with additional drilling planned. | SMM reports quiet trading in China’s copper import market as premiums ease and buyers stay cautious. The market is described as oversupplied, with spot activity low despite high LME backwardation. Bonded zone inventories kept falling, but arriving shipments declined amid delays from customs quarantine and low port efficiency. | SMM reports China’s copper cathode imports fell to 2.71 million mt in the first ten months of 2021 (down 30.03% YoY) with sluggish import premiums. SHFE/LME ratio issues limited inflows, while global shipping shortages stranded cargoes overseas, reducing expected total imports to 4.52 million mt (down 28.10%). | Azimut and Mont Royal report discovering a highly prospective nickel-copper ultramafic intrusion at Wapatik. The headline suggests potential future mineralization, but the report is exploratory and does not indicate immediate changes to mine output, inventories, or refining capacity. Near-term price impact on nickel and copper is therefore limited. | Battery Mineral Resources reported assay results from step-out and infill drilling at the Dalmacia target within the Punitaqui copper complex in Chile, including multiple intercepts above 2% copper. The company states Punitaqui is expected to resume copper concentrate production in mid-to-late 2022, supported by ongoing drilling and permitting progress. | Chile’s copper exports rose more than 35% in November to $4.92 billion, supported by all-time-high global copper prices. The strong results contributed to a trade surplus, amid expectations of post-pandemic demand recovery and comments from Codelco that prices may dip next year due to increased supply. | Dalmacia Drilling reports a step-out hole delivering 14 meters of mineralization grading 2.44% copper at Battery Mineral Resources’ Punitaqui mine. Such exploration results can support expectations for higher future copper resources and production potential, which may provide modest short-term bullish sentiment for copper. | Sanatana Resources reports strong grab-sample results from the Molloy Zone at its Oweegee copper-gold project in British Columbia, including up to ~1.09% copper and elevated gold and silver. Management says the zone may be a copper-gold porphyry window that will be drill-tested in 2022, supported by alteration mapping and soil anomalies.

news_summary: Emmerson Resources reported strong RC drill results at its Hermitage Project in Australia, including 117m at 3.38% copper and additional copper-rich intervals. The company plans further drilling and notes assay results are still pending for some sections. The update supports positive near-term sentiment for copper exploration prospects. | Inner Mongolia’s new materials development plan (2021-2025) calls for expanding downstream capacity for high-performance aluminium (over 2 million tons by 2025) and high-performance copper products (e.g., copper poles, conductors, foil). The policy supports industry chain buildout that may boost supply expectations for both metals. | Inner Mongolia’s new materials development plan (2021-2025) calls for expanding downstream non-ferrous metal industries, targeting high-performance aluminum (including alloys, foil, rods, cables) and high-performance copper (copper poles, conductors, foil). Planned capacity additions and industrial-chain extension can lift near-term demand expectations for aluminium and copper prices. | Copper fell for a third day as Omicron concerns offset China’s stimulus effects. A stronger yuan reduced costs for buyers, but investors cut Comex longs and remained cautious. Tight Chinese stockpiles and strong imports were supportive, while rising LME availability and weak deliveries tempered gains. | SMM expects LME copper to trade rangebound at $9,350–9,680/mt and SHFE at 68,500–70,500 yuan/mt. Support comes from relatively low inventories and stable smelter/downstream operating rates, but downside risk from Omicron-driven caution, a firmer dollar, mixed US data, and year-end demand/fund outflows keeps prices from trending higher.

news_summary: LME copper futures rose for a third day as China cut reserve requirements to boost liquidity, strengthening the renminbi and making dollar-denominated copper cheaper. The piece also cites stronger Chinese imports, rapidly falling inventories in China and a shift toward lower speculative longs, with supply still described as relatively tight. | Copper prices rose Wednesday on strong Chinese import demand, but the article highlights ICSG expectations of a large refined copper surplus in 2022. It attributes the surplus to higher refining production and increased secondary scrap output, arguing demand growth won’t absorb supply, which could weigh on copper sentiment going forward. | Rockridge completed a helicopter-borne VTEM EM and magnetic survey at its Knife Lake copper project in Saskatchewan and reports encouraging identification of conductors, while field analytical results are still pending. The update supports future drilling targeting for a Cu-Zn-Co VMS deposit, with no immediate production or supply change indicated. | Copper prices fell as Omicron-related concerns eased and the dollar firmed, adding pressure despite still-low LME inventories. LME on-warrant stocks rose to a one-month high, signaling easing nearby tightness. Nickel declined as well, while aluminium rose slightly, showing mixed pressure across industrial metals. | Fathom Nickel announced updated 2021 soil geochemistry at its Albert Lake property near the historic Rottenstone mine, identifying extensive Ni-Cu-Co anomalies consistent with magmatic nickel signatures and planning winter drilling. Nickel and copper exploration potential is increased, but no immediate production changes are stated, limiting near-term price impact. | Nevada Copper reported significant operational improvements at its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine, including higher equipment availability, improved contractor productivity, development rates up 50% vs August, and consistent milling with >90% recoveries and ~4,700 tpd processing. Commissioning and ventilation upgrades support ramp-up into H1 2022.

news_summary: SMM discusses tight short-term recycled copper supply and worsening availability of copper scrap due to stricter customs/import controls, COVID disruptions, and energy ‘double control’ costs. It argues that recycled copper helps offset mineral supply gaps, but constrained supply and long clearance times keep scrap-driven copper pricing under upward pressure. | SMM reports that China’s secondary copper raw-material policy (zero solid-waste imports from 2021 and stricter standards) is tightening supply of copper scrap/feedstock. With saturated copper-rod capacity but insufficient scrap availability, competition for raw materials is expected to intensify, keeping copper-related spreads tight and supporting bullish near-term pricing. | SMM summarizes analysis that copper demand in emerging applications—EVs, charging piles, wind and solar—will rise quickly, lifting copper’s share of total demand. It cites supply tightness, including refined copper and concentrate shortages in 2021-2022, implying tighter near-term balances and supportive price conditions for copper. | SMM reports that booming EVs, charging piles, and renewable power (PV and wind) are set to lift copper demand in 2021-2023, with emerging uses breaking the prior supply-demand balance. Industrial Securities estimates show a sizable copper supply gap and cathode/concentrate shortages in 2021-2022. | SMM forecasts copper prices to decline, citing stronger US dollar/lower liquidity amid tightening expectations and weakening demand from construction, housing, and power grid cable volumes. It also notes export order declines and squeezed downstream margins. Despite a LME stock drawdown and contango/backwardation, the overall outlook remains bearish for copper. | The piece forecasts copper prices slipping moderately in 2022 after record highs, driven by macro headwinds like a stronger dollar and US rate tightening. It also cites supportive factors such as mine and refined supply growth, low visible inventories, and ongoing “green” demand, but expects overall moderation from 2021 levels.
