news_summary: Major copper miners and Chinese smelters are nearing agreement on 2022 treatment and refining charges (TC/RC). The stated gap is about $8/t, with smelters near $69/t and miners near $61/t. Analysts expect supply tightness to ease, potentially affecting copper market balance and profitability. | SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets rose 4,700 mt over the weekend to 94,800 mt, marking consecutive weeks of increases. Gains were concentrated in Shanghai and Guangdong due to more imported copper arrivals and shrinking demand, worsened by COVID-related logistics disruptions and higher premiums. Inventory is likely to keep building this week. | SMM says PBOC easing and US debt-ceiling progress may support copper, but market signals point to weaker end-year demand: SHFE backwardation shows pessimism, imports remain strong, and logistics risks persist. With spot premiums expected to weaken and sellers likely liquidate after deliveries, copper faces resistance near 70,000 yuan/mt. | China Color Corp. signed a $550 million purchasing contract for a copper smelter project in Oman/Indonesia with PT AMMANMINERAL INDUSTRI. The plan includes ~900,000 t/y copper-concentrate smelting and related port/water facilities, with China Color supplying equipment for Bennett South/Bennett Bay smelters. The project supports future copper supply processing. | South African rail operators report recurrent theft of copper cables, sometimes causing power failures and train delays or cancellations. Police and rail agencies say thieves steal and sell copper for drugs, while replacement and lost-revenue costs run into the hundreds of millions. This implies additional near-term copper demand for repairs. | Castillo Copper reported progress on exploration and drilling at its Arya copper prospect in Queensland and continued work on Zambia assets, including IP surveys targeting areas with copper anomalies. Laboratory assay delays extend some lithium option timelines, but the article contains no immediate changes to copper production or supply. | Aston Bay reports that ground EM surveys at its Storm Copper Project in Nunavut identified multiple new conductive anomalies and drill-ready shallow and deep copper targets ahead of a planned 2022 campaign. Results show conductors correlate with previously drilled high-grade copper, supporting exploration growth potential rather than near-term supply changes. | Copper Mountain Mining announced updated economics and formally approved advancing the Eva Copper Project in Queensland, citing improved NPV mainly from higher metal prices despite higher capex from inflation and COVID-related material/labour constraints. The company plans to advance detailed engineering, secure financing, and target commissioning in late 2024, supporting copper sentiment.

LME registered and total stocks rose markedly in recent weeks, easing supply concerns. The company also plans ~25,000 m of additional drilling in 2022 to expand appraisal and test new targets. | Royal Road Minerals reported initial drill results from its Guintár copper-gold project in Colombia, including porphyry-style altered intrusive rocks with mineralized stockwork and sheeted veinlets. The release cites intersections featuring copper (e.g., 0.62% Cu) alongside gold and silver, supporting bullish sentiment for copper. | Aztec Minerals and Kootenay Silver start a 22-hole, 5,000 m RC drilling program at the Cervantes gold-copper porphyry project in Sonora, targeting oxide and deeper sulfide mineralization. The work aims to expand and define gold-copper zones at multiple prospects, but no production or supply data are provided. | Camino Minerals announced discovery drilling at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru, reporting anomalous copper up to 1.05% Cu and associated cobalt, plus oxidized and sulphide mineralization. The company expects drilling permits soon, but the news is exploratory and does not signal near-term production impacts for copper markets. | Aranjin Resources reported promising maiden drilling results from the Sharga Copper Project in Mongolia, confirming a substantial VMS mineralized system. news_summary: Funds have reportedly closed their copper trading books earlier this year. Speculative positioning on LME and CME copper futures has dropped sharply since October’s high-volatility spike, when LME three-month copper briefly surged to about $10,452.50/tonne amid unusual time-spread turbulence. Reduced speculative exposure can pressure prices downward. | LME three-month aluminium fell 0.96% after LME warehouse inventories jumped by 67,750 tonnes, though declines were capped as demand stayed strong and China supply was expected to remain tight. The article also says copper dropped 0.38% with LME registered stocks rising for a fourth day. | SMM expects copper prices to remain rangebound in December due to improving global liquidity from China’s RRR cut, while Fed expectations remain a key focus. Offsetting factors include weaker fundamentals, lower concentrate TC sentiment over Peru’s Las Bambas risk, disruptions to smelter imports, and inventories likely turning up. | London copper was flat as investors awaited a key U.S. Fed decision on timing of tapering and potential earlier rate hikes. The dollar’s strength and possible liquidity tightening were a focus. LME aluminium eased, nickel was steady, and Shanghai contracts were slightly lower, indicating limited net near-term momentum. | Amarc Resources said Freeport-McMoRan will continue its earn-in into a second drilling season at the JOY copper-gold district in British Columbia, following $5.94 million invested in 2021. The update focuses on exploration, data integration, and next-season planning, with results to be released later.

news_summary: Peru’s PM warned that a 26-day road blockade blocking MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine may lead to a full production suspension later this week. Negotiations with local residents in Chumbivilcas have stalled, with residents refusing to lift the blockade, raising near-term copper supply disruption concerns. | MMG said it will be unable to continue production at the Las Bambas copper mine in Peru after failing to agree with a community blocking the transport road used to move ore. The shutdown represents an immediate copper supply disruption, likely tightening near-term market availability and supporting prices. | Barksdale Resources announced completion of a 5,000m drilling program at the Cerro Verde zone in Sonora, Mexico, with all holes intersecting assay-confirmed copper mineralization above a 0.20% cut-off and reporting several high-grade near-surface intercepts. Metallurgical column tests suggest heap-leach recovery is promising. | Global Energy Metals signed an agreement to sell a 1% NSR on Norway’s Rana nickel-copper-cobalt project to Electric Royalties, while Metals One plans to acquire 90% and fund exploration. The deal is aimed at unlocking remaining Ni-Cu potential, but implies no immediate supply change. | Global Energy Metals signed a definitive agreement to sell a 1% net smelter royalty on Norway’s Rana Ni-Cu-Co project to Electric Royalties, with Metals One planning a funded exploration program and resource update timeline. The transaction is supportive for nickel supply expectations and, secondarily, copper via the mixed deposit grades. | Peru’s Las Bambas suspended copper operations after talks to end a month-long road blockade failed. The government considered a state of emergency “as a last resort.” Las Bambas—about 2% of global supply—will end processing, likely reducing near-term copper output and supporting copper prices. | TNR Gold announced a new investment loan and updated its royalty holdings. The release is broadly positive for the copper project but does not imply immediate production. | Talon Metals updates its Tamarack nickel-copper project, reporting new high-grade drill intersections and outlining an aggressive 2022 exploration program to progress toward pre-feasibility and expand resource potential. It also establishes an ATM program to fund operations, citing strong US interest in domestic battery nickel supply.

news_summary: SMM reports the inner metal market trading higher. Shanghai nickel rises over 2.7% and Shanghai aluminium over 2.7% (around 2%+). Copper also stays high and volatile, with spot prices up for two days, though downstream buying weakens after replenishment. Overall sentiment remains supported by a weaker USD. | SMM reports a broad metal rally in China as the dollar moved higher then the market stayed bid. Shanghai nickel rose about 2.75% and Shanghai aluminium about 2.7% on supportive spot dynamics. Copper remained high and volatile for two straight days, despite weaker downstream replenishment. | MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine in Peru will shut down after talks with the Chumbivilcas community failed. The decision follows negotiations breaking down, prompting a Credit Suisse downgrade. MMG shares fell sharply, reflecting heightened risk of copper supply loss, which can tighten near-term availability and lift prices. | China will adjust tariffs effective Jan 1, 2022. As a top nickel producer, this could support supply continuity. | Kintavar announced it has completed drilling on its Wabash copper project, reporting confirmed mineralization at depth over more than 2.5 kilometers combined. news_summary: LME copper for three months rose about 0.9% to $9,534/t as oil and equities climbed, the dollar weakened, and investors turned slightly more optimistic amid Omicron concerns. Coverage highlights low copper warehouse receipts and a reported refined copper market shortage, suggesting limited near-term buffering against supply risks. | Nevada Copper reported continued positive operational performance at its Pumpkin Hollow underground mine. Development is running at the highest rate of 2021, nearly 50% higher than November, with additional bolters and ventilation fan infrastructure being delivered and commissioned. The company remains on track to mine the first high-grade stope in Q1 2022. | Wyloo Metals agreed to buy Canada’s Noront Resources after BHP exited its bid to acquire Noront. The deal targets Noront’s Eagle Nest nickel asset in the Ring of Fire. Both companies pursued the nickel supply for EV batteries, with the transaction likely supporting tighter near-term nickel supply expectations. | Ivanhoe Mines says its Kamoa-Kakula Phase 1 concentrator in the DRC has produced over 100,000 tonnes of copper in concentrate year-to-date, surpassing the top end of 2021 guidance. Phase 2 remains on track for a Q2 2022 start, targeting roughly doubling annual output. | Young environmentalists plan to push changes to Chile’s copper mining rules. If the proposed rewrite leads to tighter permitting, higher compliance requirements, or delays/constraints on production, near-term expectations for copper supply could weaken. That would likely be supportive for copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | Peru villagers have agreed to lift a copper mine blockade for a short truce, easing disruption risk at the operation. By improving prospects for near-term production and reducing supply loss expectations, the development is likely to support copper prices over the next several trading sessions.

news_summary: Biden issued proclamations replacing some Section 232 tariffs with TRQs for EU steel and aluminum. Aluminum faces in-quota treatment only up to specified TRQ volumes (and certain exclusions may count against quota), while out-of-quota volumes remain subject to tariffs. The U.S. will monitor implementation through Dec. 2023. | China’s bonded copper inventories rose 15,600 mt to 187,300 mt (Dec 24–Dec 31), the first uptick after 11 consecutive declines. Shanghai and Guangdong bonded zones both increased. Subdued import trading and severe import losses reduced demand and shipments, shifting arrivals into bonded warehouses and stopping the drawdown.

news_summary: BHP’s recent unification vote is expected to open the door to copper acquisitions, according to Macquarie analysts. The prospect of BHP pursuing copper miners adds bullish sentiment around copper supply/demand expectations, though the piece is speculative and does not confirm any deal or timing. | The piece argues that copper demand is rising rapidly due to EVs and renewables, stating supply is already short by several million tonnes, supporting higher prices. news_summary: Rio Tinto and Mongolia agreed to end their dispute over expanding the Oyu Tolgoi $6.9 billion copper-gold project. Mongolia will waive $2.4 billion in owed debt, and underground construction is set to start, with first production expected in 1H 2023, improving near-term copper sentiment. | Three Valley Copper says its Chilean Minera Tres Valles mine will temporarily suspend operations due to underperformance at the Don Gabriel open pit (lower ore and grades), and halt the ramp-up of a new block-caving project due to insufficient cash flows. The company retracts 2022–2023 guidance and seeks financing. | Three Valley Copper said it has suspended its mining operations and issued a strategic review update. The suspension implies reduced near-term copper output, likely tightening supply expectations. This would generally pressure copper prices downward over the following days as markets assess the duration and scale of reduced production. | Rio Tinto said it reached a new agreement with Mongolia to proceed with a $6.9 billion expansion of the Oyu Tolgoi copper mine. The deal enables the “undercut” work to start within days, removing a long-running obstacle and improving the near-term copper supply outlook. | Rio Tinto, Turquoise Hill and Mongolia agreed to restart underground operations at the Oyu Tolgoi copper project after years of dispute. Turquoise Hill will waive a $2.4 billion carry loan, amend funding terms, and production is expected to begin in days with first sustainable output in the first half of 2023. | SMM expects domestic policy to remain loose ahead of CNY, with PBoC rate cuts supporting ample liquidity. The decision relates to progressing exploration toward potential future copper production. The company plans social and geophysical programs before a maiden drill program, boosting exploration sentiment for potential future copper supply. | BeMetals reported new 2021 core drilling results at its Pangeni copper exploration project in Zambia, including intersections up to 0.62% Cu over 4.14 metres and multiple meaningful copper zones at the D-Prospect. The company plans follow-up work, but the announcement is exploration-focused. | Nickel led a broad base-metals decline as investors turned cautious ahead of a key Fed meeting and amid rising Ukraine tensions. Nickel futures and spot fell after prior rallies, driven partly by exchange fee/margin adjustments and profit-taking. Copper and aluminium also declined in Shanghai, reflecting risk-off sentiment despite low nickel inventories. | Newcrest has withdrawn from an option and farm-in agreement tied to the Caña Brava/Tioloma gold-copper project in Ecuador. The change introduces uncertainty around project progression and future copper development, which can modestly support copper sentiment, though any immediate impact on prices is likely small. | Playfair plans additional 1,300 metres of shallow drilling in 26 holes to trace MMI copper anomalies at its RKV copper project in Norway. 2021 results highlighted strong copper anomalies and a sulphide-rich block with notable copper and nickel grades. Drilling is delayed to spring for logistics and terrain. | Teck is reported to be joining peers with a stake in a startup focused on extracting copper from waste rock. The initiative could increase future copper recovery, implying incremental supply potential. With no near-term production scale provided, the immediate market impact is likely modest but mildly bearish for copper prices. | Big Tree Copper seeks a JSE/Cape Town listing and plans to raise over R100m. It processes copper oxide ore from old tailings and waste rock at Nababeep. Management highlights a “copper supply crunch” and EV demand growth as support for high copper prices in the near term. | Mongolia and Rio Tinto have settled their dispute over underground expansion at the Oyu Tolgoi copper-gold project, allowing underground mining construction to start. The government expects the mine to produce about 430,000 tons of copper annually once fully operational, improving forward supply visibility for copper markets. | Comex copper for January delivery settled 0.91% higher at $4.4430, gaining 4.00 cents per pound and snapping a two-session losing streak. The settlement is noted as the seventh highest this year and remains down versus recent highs, with month-to-date and year-to-date declines cited. | Mongolia and Rio Tinto settled a dispute over the underground expansion of the Oyu Tolgoi copper-gold project, enabling underground construction to start. The government says the mine is expected to produce about 430,000 tons of copper annually once fully operational, supporting copper supply expectations. | Teck Resources reached a new five-year collective agreement with United Steelworkers at Highland Valley Copper after a brief contract impasse and a 72-hour strike notice. news_summary: Environmental groups sued the U.S. Forest Service to overturn a 2017 land transfer enabling PolyMet to develop an open-pit copper (and gold) mine in Minnesota. They argue endangered species habitat would be destroyed and seek court review/resumption of land. Regulators are also being asked to reconsider PolyMet wastewater permits. | State media reports that Rio Tinto has begun underground work at a vast copper mine in Mongolia. The start of mine development can be read as progress toward future copper supply, potentially supporting sentiment around copper in the coming weeks, though detailed production timelines are not provided. | SMM’s daily review says metals rose overall, with Shanghai copper rebounding strongly after two losses (+1.30%) and Shanghai aluminium ending a losing streak (+1.87%). Shanghai nickel remained slightly down (-0.57%), though the decline narrowed. LME copper futures also rose in the afternoon. | SMM’s daily review says metals rose generally, with Shanghai copper rebounding strongly (+1.30%) after two losses and LME copper also firmer. Shanghai aluminium ended a losing streak (+1.87%) on supply and Europe-energy worries, while Shanghai nickel fell slightly (−0.57%) but the drop narrowed. | Three Valley Copper suspended operations at its Minera Tres Valles (MTV) copper mine near Salamanca, Chile, after sustained underperformance and lower-grade ore deliveries undermined mine economics. The company also paused exploration and some capex, demobilizing contractors and placing the Don Gabriel pit into care and maintenance. | BHP has started autonomous drilling at its Spence copper mine in northern Chile, using remote-operated Pit Viper 351 drills and planning to deploy five autonomous units. BHP says the program improves worker safety and is part of broader autonomy plans at Spence and nearby operations like Escondida. | Los Andes Copper reports financial results and a corporate update for its Vizcachitas copper project in Chile. The company emphasizes ongoing and planned drilling to support a pre-feasibility study, including expansion from three to five rigs and receipt of a drill permit. No production or market-moving copper supply changes are announced.

news_summary: Orogen Royalties agreed to sell its Onjo copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia to Pacific Ridge Exploration for cash and equity, plus a 2% NSR royalty. The property lies near the Mount Milligan mine and contains alkalic porphyry/copper-gold skarn targets. The transaction is exploratory, with no immediate output changes. | Allied Copper has secured an option to buy a 100% stake in the Klondike copper property in Colorado’s Paradox Copper Belt. The deal requires about $4.75m of exploration spending over four years plus cash and share issuances. The project remains at exploration stage with no near-term output. | Comex Copper futures ended the week up 4.19% to $4.4835/lb, gaining 18.05 cents. news_summary: SMM reports a midday rally in China’s base metals as the US dollar weakened. Shanghai copper rose over 3% to a three-month intraday high, while Shanghai aluminium gained over 2% for the eighth day on tight domestic supply, low inventories and improving downstream spot demand. Shanghai nickel also rose. | SMM’s midday review notes a weaker US dollar and rising industrial metals. Shanghai copper climbed over 3% to a new intraday high for more than three months. Shanghai aluminum rose over 2% for eight straight sessions, supported by tight inventories and expected supply shortages versus imports in the near term. | CATL will form a joint venture with copper-foil supplier Jiayuan Technology to build electrolytic copper foil manufacturing with 100,000 tonnes/year capacity in Guangdong, in two phases. The project targets high-performance lithium-ion battery current-collector foils, implying higher expected copper demand. | Comex copper for February delivery settled up 14.10 cents per pound, or 3.16%, at $4.6005, snapping a two-session losing streak. The move was the largest one-day dollar and percentage gain since Jan. 12, with the settlement at the highest level since Oct. 20, 2021. | Nebraska State Patrol says copper thieves are cutting the copper wire from street lights along Interstate 80 and Highway 75, leaving many lamp posts unlit and costing taxpayers over $100,000 in repairs and rewiring. The theft is linked to scrap-metal sales, but the impact appears highly localized. | SMM reports Chinese secondary copper rod production will not resume until around the Lantern Festival and recovery will be gradual. Output is delayed by Winter Olympics environmental restrictions until Feb. 20 in northern provinces. Additionally, weaker orders after Chinese New Year suppress production. The market also awaits March 1 VAT refund policy effects. | EMX assigned its Arizona state exploration permit covering part of the Parks Salyer copper target to Cactus 110 LLC, with cash and a 1.5% NSR royalty. The deal funds exploration and adds milestones tied to copper resource size, but provides no near-term production or output changes. | Aston Bay Holdings announced expanded leased land for its Mountain copper-zinc project in Virginia and extended its diamond drilling program to 3,500 metres. Copper and zinc mineralization have been observed in multiple holes, but assay results are pending, limiting any immediate impact on copper market expectations. | Los Andes Copper reported initial drill results at its Vizcachitas Chile project: 732m at 0.51% copper equivalent (with copper and molybdenum grades) and continued drilling to expand resources. The update can bolster bullish sentiment for copper given improved resource expectations, though nickel is not meaningfully addressed. | KGHM has registered its copper cathodes on China’s INE stock exchange, enabling the metal to be traded through that platform. The update may affect near-term expectations for physical copper supply and liquidity in China, potentially supporting prices, while it is not directly related to nickel or aluminium fundamentals. | Allied Copper Corp. signed a three-year option agreement to acquire 100% of the Stateline copper property near the Lisbon Valley Copper Mine in Colorado/Utah. The company plans exploration and highlights historical copper assays and future drilling targets, but provides no near-term production, smelting, or inventory impact. | ThreeD Capital says its investee Premium Nickel Resources has completed the acquisition of the Selebi and Selebi North Ni-Cu-Co mines and expects exploration to begin immediately. Metallurgical testing supports producing separate copper and nickel-cobalt concentrates, with drilling and studies planned, suggesting improved supply outlook for nickel and copper. | North American Nickel’s affiliate Premium Nickel Resources has completed the purchase of the Selebi and Selebi North Ni-Cu-Co mines in Botswana from the liquidator, and exploration is expected to begin immediately. Metallurgical testing supports redevelopment to produce separate nickel and copper-cobalt concentrates, which is mildly bullish for nickel and copper. | Mongoose Mining agreed to acquire the Steele Run IOCG copper-cobalt-gold deposit in Nova Scotia, citing historic drill core with reported high copper grades and cobalt assays. The company plans further exploration, but the release provides no new production, financing, or near-term supply change. | Nebraska State Patrol says copper thieves are cutting copper wiring from interstate and highway light posts, leaving areas in the dark. Repairs and rewiring are reportedly over $100,000 so far. Authorities cite safety risks and escalating thefts, but the impact appears localized with limited effect on broader copper markets. | Canadian firm CopperCorp was granted six exploration licences in western Tasmania covering 1,208km² across its Alpine and Skyline projects. Management says the area is prospective for copper and other metals, with planned drilling this year. The news expands tenure but does not imply near-term supply changes. | Lundin Mining said it discovered a new copper-gold mineralized system called Saúva about 15 km north of its Chapada mine in Goiás, Brazil. The announcement can boost market expectations for copper resources and future output, typically providing modest bullish sentiment for copper prices over coming days.

The plan would require a land transfer from public to private ownership. Tribal and environmental opposition centers on cultural damage and groundwater use. | SMM said China’s major copper inventories rose 12,900 mt to 198,200 mt from Feb 11 to Feb 14, reflecting smelter stock shipments after the CNY holiday. The project targets higher recycling capacity and improved impurity removal, increasing expected recovered metal output from recycling materials. | Aurubis plans a €70m, state-of-the-art bleed-treatment facility in Olen, Belgium, using hydrometallurgical processing to recover nickel and copper from electrolyte streams generated in its Belgian tankhouses. news_summary: Comex front-month copper for February delivery settled 0.63% lower at $4.4815 per pound, down 2.85 cents. The contract fell for four straight sessions, marking the largest one-day dollar and percentage decline since Feb. 11, and the lowest settlement since Feb. 8. | A market note on copper “boom” lists several copper-exposed miners and argues prices remain near record levels due to years of underinvestment and Covid-era mine disruptions, while demand strengthens from batteries and renewables. It also flags macro downside risk, but overall points to firm bullish copper sentiment. | Natural gas prices surged after the Russia-Ukraine war, with European TTF up about 10% and China’s LNG ex-factory price up sharply year-on-year. The increased energy cost (dominant in conversion costs) is squeezing profits of copper rod enterprises and may trigger gas restrictions, threatening copper rod output despite supply being higher than demand.

news_summary: SMM data shows copper inventories in China’s domestic bonded zones rose 14,500 mt to 300,600 mt as of Feb 25, with increases in Shanghai and Guangdong. The rise reflects weaker-than-expected consumption and a long-closed import window, leading traders to move recently arrived cargo into bonded storage. | Norilsk Nickel (Nornickel) will present its “South Cluster” project in Krasnoyarsk, aimed at ramping up production at the Zapolyarnaya mine and upgrading the Norilsk enrichment factory, with investments planned up to 2027. The expansion supports expectations for higher nickel supply and may also buoy copper sentiment. | Codelco reportedly lifted copper sales as price gains offset a dip in output. The phrasing suggests stronger realized copper pricing helped compensate for lower production volumes, which can support expectations for near-term copper market sentiment and price levels. | Codelco reported 2021 profits surging to $7.4 billion, supported by higher global copper prices. The company produced about 1.73 million tonnes of copper, saw cash costs rise due to higher input prices, and noted maintenance at the Chuquicamata smelter would reduce cathode output. It forecasts copper prices near current levels. | Vale executives said Russia’s invasion of Ukraine is already driving speculation around nickel prices and could influence iron ore pellet prices. The company also discussed longer-term demand: copper demand may rise ~30% by the next decade. However, the piece gives no clear near-term directional price move for copper or aluminium.

news_summary: Western sanctions against Russia intensified after bank blocking from SWIFT, raising fears of disrupted Russian exports and inputs. Russia is a key aluminum producer and supplier of gas used in aluminum production, and also has meaningful nickel mine supply. LME three-month aluminum hit a record while nickel and copper rose. | Copper Fox Metals filed its 2021 financial statements and reported work advancing its copper projects, including a Schaft Creek preliminary economic assessment update, metallurgical drilling for further testwork, and progress toward Van Dyke permitting via water resource and environmental requirements analysis. It also outlines planned 2022 exploration field programs. | Trigon Metals said it shipped the first truck load of copper concentrate from its Kombat Mine to IXM warehouses in Walvis Bay, Namibia. The shipment graded 20.41% copper and is part of 400 tonnes of stockpiled concentrate, supporting a ramp toward commercial production and near-term copper revenue. | The piece reports that Russian metal exports are sliding as sanctions interfere with commodity financing. Reduced access to trade finance and logistics can limit prompt shipments, tightening near-term supply expectations for industrial metals like copper, nickel, and aluminium and supporting bullish price pressure. | Aluminium prices surged to a record on the LME after Western nations expanded sanctions on Russia, raising supply disruption worries. Additional banking restrictions and Rusal halting alumina shipments from its Nikolaev refinery pressured the market. The piece also flags energy and sanctions spillovers that can affect other industrial metals.

The company cites drilling and data relogging but provides no immediate production or supply changes. | Copper prices rose on Tuesday after Chile, the top copper producer, reported its lowest January output since 2011, with production down 15% month-on-month and 7.5% year-on-year. No production, permitting, or logistics changes were announced. | Russia’s invasion of Ukraine is tightening supplies as LME inventories for aluminium and freely available nickel decline sharply, while European smelters cut output due to surging power prices. news_summary: The US will lift Trump-era steel and aluminum tariffs on UK imports, removing Section 232 duties on UK aluminium and allowing sustainably based volumes into the US. The UK will also drop retaliatory tariffs. This should add near-term aluminium supply and weigh on prices; copper effects are indirect. | The US and UK agreed to end Trump-era tariffs on steel and aluminum imports, removing the UK’s retaliatory tariffs and allowing duty-free flows into the US. Officials framed the deal as easing supply-demand gaps and aiding domestic producers, though it may also increase import supply pressures for aluminium prices. | The US and UK agreed to end tariffs on imported British steel and aluminum that were imposed during the Trump era. The move is intended to resolve a bilateral trade dispute, with the UK also lifting retaliatory tariffs on US exports. Annual audits are required for certain Chinese-owned UK steel. | London aluminium prices edged higher as Russia-Ukraine-related supply concerns increased, supported by Australia’s ban on exports of aluminium-making materials to Russia and output cuts by European smelters facing higher energy costs. It notes LME has no plans to ban Russian metals, while copper and nickel were weaker overall. | Britain said it resolved a US trade dispute that had left UK exporters facing steel and aluminium tariffs. Under the deal, UK aluminium and steel exporters get high-level tariff-free access to the US, taking effect June 1, after the US lifted Trump-era measures for the UK. | Talon Metals reported new high-grade nickel-copper (plus cobalt) drill intercepts at the Tamarack Nickel Project, including thick shallow sulphide intersections outside the current resource area. The company frames results as deposit expansion progress toward feasibility and a Tesla nickel-concentrate offtake after commercial production. | Talon Metals reported additional shallow, high-grade nickel and copper intercepts from drilling in the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project’s CGO West area. Results include thick nickel-copper sulphide intervals and expanded mineralization outside the current resource area, supporting future feasibility and an existing Tesla nickel concentrate offtake. | The piece’s headline says Barrick expects room to increase copper output in Zambia. That points to improved production outlook at a key African copper supply source, which can influence near-term expectations for mine supply and therefore copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | EPA proposed more stringent NESHAPs for hazardous air pollutants from primary copper smelters, including lower PM limits, standards for fugitive furnace/converter emissions, and removal of startup/shutdown/malfunction exemptions with added opacity limits. The changes could raise control and compliance costs for smelters, affecting short-term copper supply expectations. | Minerals Development Oman (MDO) outlined multiple projects, highlighting Mazoon Mining in Yanqul, which it says contains up to 16 million tonnes of copper reserves and 1.56 million tonnes annual production capacity. MDO is also exploring additional copper ore in nearby areas, alongside industrial minerals like limestone and gypsum. | Peru transporters protest higher fuel prices, threatening road and logistics disruption. More importantly for metals, Las Bambas halted operations mid-January and Southern Copper’s Cuajone was suspended for 15 days after protests blocked access to water and supplies. Output from both mines is down about 27% year-to-date.

The news is project-focused and does not imply near-term production or supply disruptions, limiting impact on short-term copper prices. | Copper prices rebounded after a volatile session, with night trading lifting both Shanghai and international 2205 contracts. Spot electrolytic copper in Shanghai saw an average rise but weakened as the “downstream epidemic” weighed on demand and water fell. Macro sentiment improved on falling yields and risk appetite, with Lunni gains supporting prices. | Shanghai nickel jumped 17% on the daily limit as supply worries intensified after Russia’s threat to demand rouble payments for natural gas raised concerns about metals supply disruptions. Higher energy costs and LME discussions around Russian metal deliveries also supported aluminium. The piece cites a Zambia copper mine disruption. | Ximen Mining announced drilling plans for its Bud-Elk copper-gold property in British Columbia, targeting disseminated copper-gold porphyry and copper-gold skarn mineralization. The company describes historical grades and confirms 10 drill sites approved for 2022, with drilling expected to start after April surveys. | SMM says China’s post-CNY copper inventory pivot appeared earlier than prior years as inventories fell sharply across Shanghai and Guangdong while overall stock declined. With low global inventories, the SHFE/spot-futures spread moved from contango to backwardation. Supply concerns (high LME prices, shipping delays) and weaker scrap economics support bullish near-term copper. | SMM reports Shanghai copper and aluminium rising on energy-supply and cost worries linked to Europe gas settlement, alongside demand suppression from spreading COVID. It also highlights aluminium inventory declines and nickel-related logistics disruptions and weak supply/demand, with SHFE nickel hitting limit up after LME gains. | Infinitum Copper commenced a ~3-week magneto-telluric geophysical survey at its La Adelita project in Mexico after crews prepared the grid and identified the Pericos copper zone. The program aims to refine geology for future development; no production, permitting, or supply changes are announced. | Barrick’s CEO said the company sees further opportunity to increase copper output in Zambia’s copper belt, leveraging the “pro-business” Zambian government and recent power improvements. Canadian firms’ interest in Zambian copper assets is noted, alongside Barrick’s continued investment posture and Lumwana operations. | The article lists commodity dividend stocks and emphasizes Southern Copper (SCCO), noting it produced 958,200 tons of copper in 2021, exceeded guidance, and reported higher revenue and record net sales. It also references rising broader commodity prices tied to the Russia-Ukraine backdrop and economic reopening. | The U.S. and UK agreed to stop U.S. Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum imports from the UK from June 1, 2022, and the UK will lift retaliatory tariffs on $500m+ of U.S. exports. The move is intended to ease supply-demand gaps and support U.S. producers under ongoing concerns about China overcapacity. | Peru approved MMG’s plan to expand the Las Bambas copper mine in Apurimac, targeting the Chalcobamba pit and related infrastructure. news_summary: The report cites ongoing declines in domestic copper warehouse receipt inventories and low-shock LME warehouse levels, with spot premiums rising versus futures and LME spot discounts narrowing. It also notes operational updates for major copper projects in Zambia and Congo, while tight recycled copper supply is partly offset by abundant concentrate supply and a stronger dollar. | Tempest Minerals says its maiden deep drilling at the Meleya project’s Orion target intersected multiple zones of copper-bearing sulphides, including 10m of copper-bearing semi-massive sulphides and additional disseminated and stringer veins. The company plans further drilling, but the report provides no production or reserves. | Copper gained modestly after prior losses despite concern that Shanghai’s COVID lockdown could curb China demand. Nickel fell sharply amid uncertainty around Tsingshan short positions and thin trading. A firmer dollar and recession fears also weighed, while aluminium rose slightly, indicating limited immediate pressure there. | Copper rebounded after two sessions of losses despite renewed worries that Shanghai’s planned COVID lockdown could curb China demand. Nickel fell sharply amid thin trade and uncertainty around Tsingshan’s major short position. Investors also cite recession risk from US yield curve inversion and a firmer dollar. | Atacama Copper announced initial exploration results from its Placeton and Caballo Muerto projects in Chile, highlighting copper- and molybdenum-related geochemical anomalies, alteration zones, and IP/magnetics targeting two high-priority porphyry targets. The company plans an April IP survey and immediate drilling, but no production changes are discussed. | The article’s headline says water scarcity is hitting Chilean copper mines. Limited water availability can disrupt mining operations and reduce near-term mine output. That supply risk can worsen short-term copper market expectations, creating downward pressure on prices over the next days to a few weeks.

news_summary: An SMM overnight wrap reports rebounds in base metals. Shanghai copper rose 0.81%, Shanghai nickel declined but narrowed (down 5.57%), and Shanghai aluminium fell slightly (about -0.13%). LME showed only lun nickel down (over 9%) while LME copper rose modestly and aluminium was flat. No new metal-specific driver is detailed. | London copper edged higher for a second session, supported by bargain-buying, but China’s planned Shanghai lockdown and rising COVID cases weighed on demand expectations. In contrast, Shanghai nickel dropped more than 5%. Chile’s Codelco plans a “non-core” asset offering, a minor producer-development signal. | London copper edged higher for a second session, supported by bargain-buying, though COVID-19 spread in China’s key consumer regions and a two-stage Shanghai lockdown raised demand concerns. In contrast, Shanghai nickel dropped over 5%, reflecting stronger negative sentiment and likely weaker near-term outlook for industrial metals demand. | Nimy Resources reports nickel-bearing gossan uncovered during drill-site preparations at its Mons nickel project in Western Australia. Portable XRF readings show up to 0.96% nickel, with additional anomalous copper and cobalt and proximity to older nickel intersections. Further geochemical sampling will test the near-surface extent. | London copper eased as China’s widening COVID-19 outbreak and Shanghai’s planned lockdown heightened concerns over near-term industrial demand. The softer tone on growth weighed on metals sentiment. Shanghai nickel also fell nearly 5%, reflecting the broader downturn in China-linked consumption expectations. | SMM reports a mixed midday session: Shanghai nickel fell sharply to expand its decline to 6.48%, while copper rose modestly (~0.64%) and aluminium ended slightly lower (~-0.15%) after an intraday rally. The piece links moves to a weaker oil complex, a slightly softer dollar, and concerns over China fuel demand. | London copper fell as COVID-19 infections and a planned two-stage Shanghai lockdown in top metals consumer China fueled concerns over near-term industrial demand. Shanghai nickel dropped about 4.7% on the same growth/demand outlook fears. Commentary pointed to potential impacts on Chinese industrial production. | FLSmidth was selected to deliver three SAG mills, three ball mills and cyclones to the Josemaria copper-gold mine in Argentina. The deal is worth about DKK110m booked in Q4 2021 and DKK490m in Q1 2022, with deliveries scheduled by late-2023, signaling progress toward copper production. | FLSmidth was selected to supply gearless SAG mills, ball mills and cyclones for the Josemaria copper-gold project in Argentina. The order value was booked in parts during Q4 2021 and Q1 2022, with equipment deliveries scheduled for late 2023, supporting long-run copper mining operations. | Copperstone Resources filed an environmental permit application to reopen the Viscaria copper mine in Kiruna, Sweden, including extensive plans for water management, tailings recycling, and environmental safeguards. The company frames the filing as a milestone toward restarting copper production, prompting a supportive supply outlook for copper. | Copperstone Resources submitted its environmental permit application to restart the Viscaria copper mine in Northern Sweden. The filing covers environmental impacts, water management, tailings recycling, and mitigation measures. Company statements frame this as a milestone toward recommencing copper production, supporting near-term expectations for copper supply. | SMM reports the LME will increase initial margin requirements for copper from $638/mt to $650/mt and for aluminium from $262/mt to $280/mt, effective after the close of business on March 31, 2022. Higher margins can curb leverage and near-term speculative demand. | SMM reports the LME will increase initial margins for copper to $650/mt from $638/mt and for aluminium to $280/mt from $262/mt, effective after the close of business on March 31, 2022. Higher margin requirements can reduce leverage and near-term trading appetite. | SMM reports Shandong bare bright copper scrap prices rose early in trading, tracking an increase in copper futures. Weak availability of raw materials reduced buyer bargaining power, but poor orders for secondary copper rod producers limited how sharply scrap could rise. The cathode-scrap spread is expected to remain stable next days. | Marimaca Copper Corp. reported results from the first seven reverse circulation drill holes at its Marimaca Oxide Deposit in Chile. Six of seven holes intersected broad near-surface green copper oxide mineralisation, with several grades above the 2019 resource model and indications the deposit remains open at depth and to the southeast. | Antofagasta CEO Ivan Arriagada said copper fundamentals remain solid and prices should stay at recently high levels over the next 12 months, citing relative supply tightness and the role of copper in energy transition. The company targets steady output despite Los Pelambres drought, while continuing Twin Metals progress. | Inomin Mines reports significant drill results at its Beaver nickel-cobalt property: a 252m interval grading 20.6% magnesium with 0.16% nickel, plus near-surface silver and copper from another hole (0.29% copper over 3m). Discoveries remain open, supporting improved short-term sentiment for nickel and, to a lesser extent, copper supply potential. | Mitsubishi is reported to be expanding its copper trading business as a Peru mine ramps up production. The development points to changes in near-term copper availability and trading activity, which can influence short-term market expectations for supply and pricing. | Los Andes Copper provides a corporate update on its Vizcachitas Chile copper-molybdenum project, reporting initial diamond drilling results (0.50% copper equivalent) and additional holes awaiting assays. The company plans to seek lifting of an injunction to resume drilling and update its resource and preliminary feasibility study. | Culpeo Minerals says its first drillhole at the Lana Corina project in Chile intersected visible copper sulphide mineralisation, with chalcopyrite increasing with depth and referencing strong historical copper intervals. The company plans further drilling and will report assays later. It remains exploration without immediate supply impact.

news_summary: Peru’s economy minister says the government will seek extra taxation from mining firms’ “excess profits” as global metals prices have risen. The move follows earlier, more ambitious tax promises that faced resistance. Because Peru is the world’s second-largest copper producer, the policy could weigh on copper sentiment. | Peru’s government says it will impose extra taxes on mining companies earning “excess profits” amid high global metal prices, as political pressure rises. With Peru the world’s second-largest copper producer and some mine production already disrupted, the tax proposal heightens margin and supply risk for copper. | Hannan Metals reports follow-up soil sampling at its Belen copper-gold porphyry target in Peru, identifying a 10-km trend of copper and gold anomalies linked to Miocene intrusions and a peripheral epithermal signature. Further sampling and planned trenching/airborne surveys aim to advance drill permitting soon. | Copper Mountain Mining announced successful commissioning of its trolley assist project at the Copper Mountain Mine in British Columbia, using pantograph-equipped electric haul trucks and a 1 km electrified ramp. The company says this should cut diesel energy costs and greenhouse gases and improve productivity, but offers no near-term copper output guidance changes. | Peru’s minister says the government will target miners’ “excess profits” from soaring global metals prices through a dialed-back tax reform. As Peru is the world’s second-largest copper producer, higher windfall taxes could affect near-term production and supply expectations, putting pressure on copper sentiment. | Great Atlantic Resources began 2022 prospecting and rock geochemical sampling at its Mascarene Property, targeting the Wheal Louisiana copper–nickel–cobalt occurrence. Historic and limited sampling reported anomalous copper and cobalt, with chalcopyrite-bearing veins intersected in recent drilling. Results will be assayed, but no production or supply changes are indicated. | Comet Resources agreed to buy Glencore’s Mt Margaret copper project in Queensland, issuing shares to Glencore’s affiliate and arranging payments through a third party. Mt Margaret is described as a past-producing, partially pre-stripped open-pit asset with sizeable copper resources. The deal supports copper sentiment but won’t alter supply immediately. | United Steelworkers ratified a five-year labor agreement with Kennecott Utah Copper covering 1,300 hourly mine workers. The contract includes lump-sum payments, yearly wage increases, and maintained/improved health and life insurance benefits, with many terms effective immediately. The deal follows rejection of an earlier offer in 2022.

news_summary: China’s March unwrought copper imports fell 8.8% year-on-year to 504,009 tonnes for the third straight monthly decline, attributed to sluggish manufacturing demand and COVID disruptions. Manufacturing PMI slipped below 50. The article also mentions aluminium exports rising in March, but the copper demand weakness is the key signal. | Goldman Sachs says copper fundamentals have tightened as global exchange inventories fell for the first time in a decade. It points to reduced Chilean output from water shortages and rising mine costs, plus sanctions-driven risks to Russian exports and freight. The firm forecasts larger refined supply gaps and higher copper prices. | Barrick Gold says it has revised plans with Pakistan to restart the large Reko Diq copper-gold project, estimating copper output of about 200,000 tons per year and investment around $7 billion. The company positions it as timely amid global copper shortages, which is likely to support near-term copper sentiment and pricing. | SMM says copper cathode rod producers’ average operating rate rose in March but remained below normal historical levels. It attributes the shortfall to late production resumption and high copper prices, plus mid-March COVID outbreaks in major regions that reduced downstream output, disrupted procurement and transportation, and forced some output cuts. | Infinitum Copper reported trench results from its La Adelita project in Mexico, including intervals averaging 1.90% copper and additional copper-bearing skarn samples up to ~1.34% copper. The company is advancing geophysics and a new drilling program, but provides no near-term production or supply disruption details. | Talon Metals purchased three additional drill rigs to expand exploration outside the main resource area at its Tamarack nickel-copper project in Minnesota. The new drilling targets multiple zones to find high-grade nickel-copper mineralization for potential battery supply, but current output is not reported as changing. | CopperBank reports initial drill core re-sampling and assays from its Copper Creek project in Arizona, confirming historic high-grade breccia-hosted copper and identifying mineralization in previously unsampled core. Over 2,800 metres of a 5,000-metre drilling program are completed; additional assays are pending for broader evaluation and resource work.

news_summary: ANZ says copper remains supported by a tight supply situation that could worsen with potential renewed COVID disruptions affecting China’s port traffic, while anticipating demand resilience from electricity grid investment. It also notes Russia’s role as a major producer could pressure inventories lower. Overall, near-term copper price momentum looks positive. | Culpeo Minerals completed induced polarisation surveys at its Quelon iron oxide-copper-gold project in Chile, identifying large geophysical anomalies and stating three zones are compelling drill targets. The company is pursuing earn-in stakes and plans a maiden drilling program. The update is exploratory and does not change near-term copper supply. | Minmetals disclosed that Las Bambas will halt its ability to sustain copper output from April 20 (Peru time) for safety reasons amid ongoing community protests over social investment commitments. Authorities and stakeholders have not resolved the impasse, with a follow-up meeting expected in coming days. | LME copper rises 1.7% to a two-week peak as low inventories and China stimulus hopes coincide with supply worries. MMG’s Las Bambas mine plans to suspend operations after a local protest begins. Aluminium stabilizes near $3,214 per tonne on technical support, suggesting near-term resilience. | SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums for warrant and B/L trades stayed flat but import market liquidity remained sluggish. Weak customs clearance and logistics efficiency, plus rising LME copper prices that worsened the price ratio, discouraged offers. Traders cited inability to transfer documents, limiting order pickup and sustaining wider losses for imports. | Copper Fox provided an update on the Schaft Creek copper-gold-molybdenum-silver project, operated by Teck, including preparation for a planned 5,000m summer drill program and progress on geotechnical, metallurgical testing, environmental baseline work, and indigenous engagement. It is mainly a near-term development planning update. | DRC border delays, truck shortages and congestion are making it far costlier and slower to move copper cathodes and raw materials to export ports (Durban, Dar es Salaam, Beira). With Kamoa-Kakula starting early and offering 30–50% higher trucking fees, producers face mounting logistics costs and potential delivery disruptions, supporting higher copper pricing.

news_summary: AuKing Mining raised $7.1m to fund drilling and exploration at the Koongie Park copper-zinc project in Western Australia, including buying Anglo Australian Resources’ remaining 25% stake. The funds will support the planned program, but the timeline is exploration-focused, implying limited immediate impact on copper prices. | China’s NBS reported copper semis output of 1.9 million mt in March (+4.6% YoY) and 4.83 million mt in Q1 (+6.8% YoY). Higher production from processors suggests stronger near-term copper consumption demand, which can be supportive for copper prices. | Zijin Mining agreed to buy stakes in Xanadu Mines, including a path to a 45.9% interest in Mongolia’s Kharmagtai copper-gold mine via its subsidiary Khuiten Metals. The Kharmagtai resource includes sizable copper, but the transaction signals investment rather than immediate production, implying limited near-term copper price pressure. | London copper edged up as hopes for additional China stimulus offset a firm dollar and growth concerns. A suspension at Peru’s Las Bambas mine added a copper supply-tightness risk. Aluminium and nickel in LME and Shanghai were broadly softer, reflecting uneven sentiment tied to China lockdown-related demand worries. | MMG said it will suspend Las Bambas operations in southern Peru from Wednesday due to safety concerns amid local protests demanding higher financial contributions and accusing MMG of unmet compensation agreements. Las Bambas is a major copper producer (~2% of global supply), so the halt points to near-term supply losses. | SolGold says its Cascabel copper project in northern Ecuador, supported by major shareholders BHP and Newcrest, could become one of the world’s lowest-cost and lowest-carbon copper mines. The feasibility study underpins a $2.7 billion development plan and notes potential gains if a second nearby deposit is successfully developed. | Latin Metals reported positive soil sampling at its Auquis copper project in Peru, outlining a 3km x 2km area of anomalous high-grade copper (up to 2,300 ppm) with coincident molybdenum. The company plans additional rock-chip sampling and mapping, but no production or near-term supply impact is indicated. | VR Resources says drilling has started on its Hecla-Kilmer copper-gold breccia target in northern Ontario, with 4–6 holes planned and assay results expected by June/July. The company previously intersected “copper and gold locally,” but this update contains no new copper assay, production, or supply-impact information.

news_summary: LME copper is on track for a third weekly decline as China’s COVID lockdowns cloud demand, while LME-approved inventories have risen to the highest since last October. Rising inventories and a stronger U.S. dollar add pressure. Limited support comes from hopes of Chinese stimulus and supply disruptions, but overall bias remains down. | Antofagasta raised capex for its Chile Los Pelambres expansion by $900m to $2.2bn, citing revised Covid-19 costs, general inflation (wages, labor incentives, logistics), and contingency. The company said delivery schedules are unchanged and copper output guidance remains, but higher costs can support near-term copper pricing expectations. | ArcWest Exploration provided an exploration update for its Oxide Peak Cu-Au project, including planned 2022 drilling of an untested porphyry target and prior geophysical/geochemical results indicating copper anomalies. The release is company/project specific with no immediate changes to mine output, inventories, or pricing mechanisms for copper, nickel, or aluminium.

news_summary: Michigan State Police are investigating reports of copper wiring theft from an agricultural irrigation pivot in St. Joseph County, Michigan. Several feet of copper were cut from pivot towers between November 2021 and April 15, causing damage. The incident is local and unlikely to affect broader copper pricing. | Industrial metals fell as China’s COVID-19 lockdown restrictions raised fears of weaker demand and economic growth. Copper hit its lowest level in over a month, while expectations for larger Fed rate hikes supported a stronger dollar, pressuring dollar-priced metals. Additional headlines include a negative outlook for copper projects and Peru mine restart. | SMM reports higher imported copper concentrate prices and TCs, with trading concentrated on May–June shipments. Protests force Las Bambas to stop production from April 20, while Peru considers a state of emergency near Cuajone; Chile is also reshaping mining rules. The market worries about long-run concentrate supply despite short-term easing. | Trinidad and Tobago scrap dealers urge the public and authorities to curb copper wire theft after viral incidents. The association says theft harms livelihoods and has been linked to fires and past deaths. It calls for faster regulation, arrests, and refusing to buy stolen items, aiming to reduce theft-driven scrap supply. | SMM says Chinese copper inventories fell 12,400 mt to 114,900 mt over the weekend, with declines across Shanghai, Guangdong, Jiangsu and others. The drawdown is attributed to downstream restocking after sharp price drops and improving inter-provincial logistics. SMM expects inventories to keep declining into the week before May Day. | Norilsk Nickel reported preliminary 1Q22 production: consolidated nickel output rose 10% y/y to 52kt, driven by recovery of Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky mines and ramp-up of the Norilsk Concentrator. Copper production stayed roughly unchanged at 91kt, with logistics and geopolitical issues not disrupting operations. | Copper fell on Monday, dropping below $10,000/t on the back of demand fears. Funds increased bearish CME copper bets, while China’s lockdowns and weaker PMI readings signaled slowing manufacturing. Supply issues remain, but attention is shifting toward weaker near-term consumption, driving bearish sentiment. | Zambia’s president said the government is very close to selecting a private investor for Mopani Copper Mines to increase output, following the state’s purchase of the mine from Glencore and ongoing need for investment. He also denied plans to return KCM to Vedanta. | Atacama Copper began a four-week trenching and channel-sampling program at its El Cofre copper-gold project in Chile. Approximately 3,000 meters of trenching will follow up geochemical and mapping targets, with initial XRF results expected in May 2022 and lab assays in June 2022. | Nornickel said Q1 nickel production rose 10% year-on-year to 51,534 tonnes after the recovery of its two mines from 2021 flooding. Copper output was stable at 91,414 tonnes. Despite Western sanctions-related logistics challenges, the company kept its 2022 nickel forecast unchanged, citing uninterrupted operations. | Resistance groups tied to Myanmar’s civil disobedience movement warn Chinese-run copper mines in Salingyi to halt operations by May 5. The timing and potential for mine disruption raise supply risk for copper, though it is unclear if the call will be implemented immediately. | Weeks of Peru-wide protests over fuel and fertilizer costs spread to mining communities. The Chinese-owned Las Bambas copper project was disrupted after community members invaded the site, prompting Las Bambas to stop producing copper. The government deployed the army, worsening uncertainty over Peru’s copper output near term.

news_summary: London copper rebounded 1.4% after hitting a more-than-two-month low, driven by hopes for additional China economic stimulus and a slight retreat in the U.S. dollar. The piece also notes lingering COVID lockdown worries and broader macro uncertainty, with aluminium and nickel referenced via markets and related company updates. | Metallic Minerals reported an inaugural NI 43-101 inferred resource at its La Plata project, centered on the Allard copper-silver porphyry deposit. The estimate includes 115.7 Mt at 0.39% copper equivalent (0.35% Cu, 4.02 g/t Ag) and plans for follow-up drilling, but no production or inventory change. | Battery Mineral Resources said it intercepted 34 meters of 1.35% copper from the Cinabrio Norte target at its Punitaqui copper mine in Chile. The result is an exploration success that modestly improves expectations for future copper resources, offering limited near-term bullish sentiment for copper prices. | The article reviews copper market drivers: a still-large but narrowing refined deficit, Chinese demand slowing tied to real estate weakness, European consumption pressure from high energy costs, and uncertainty from Russia-related sanctions and financing. It also notes limited copper-aluminium substitution, leaving copper volatility high but directionally mixed. | Peru is losing over US$110mn in copper export value due to an illegal occupation affecting the Las Bambas operation. The disruption limits copper shipments from the mine, reducing near-term export flows from one of the country’s key producers and likely worsening short-term supply expectations. | Copper is reported to be rebounding as China vows additional support for its economy. The implication is improved near-term economic activity and demand expectations for industrial metals, which tends to lift sentiment and prices for copper, the benchmark industrial metal, over coming days.

news_summary: Copper prices were under pressure Monday, with LME three-month copper slipping below $10,000 per tonne for the first time in a month. After weeks of stalled trade—too high for buyers and too tight for sellers to short—bears are moving back in, indicating renewed bearish momentum. | LME copper fell as concerns grew that COVID lockdowns in top consumer China will curb demand, while expectations for large U.S. rate hikes lifted the dollar to multi-year highs. The stronger dollar increased costs for non-dollar buyers. China stimulus hopes limited declines. Global risk-off also pressured industrial metals. | SMM says Yangshan copper premiums under warrants rose to $20–45/mt, with averages up $8.5/mt versus the previous day. Quotes under B/L for May also increased. The improved price ratio supported import transactions, while traders held firm due to high US-dollar pledge costs and related financing frictions. | Teck Resources reported profits jumping five-fold, attributing the increase to higher copper and coal prices. The headline implies copper prices are firming, which can support short-term sentiment and expectations for further strength in copper markets over the coming days. | Industrial metals rose as hopes for further Chinese stimulus outweighed COVID-related demand worries and fears from potential aggressive U.S. rate hikes. LME three-month copper edged up and LME aluminium gained. Nickel also rose, with investors weighing China’s monetary support amid lockdown impacts on industrial activity. | Golden Rim Resources signed an option and joint venture agreement with Teck Chile to explore the Loreto porphyry copper project in northern Chile. Teck may earn up to 75% via cash payments and US$17m exploration spending. The deal targets potential deeper copper mineralization, but is not a production change. | Chile’s SMA has launched a sanctions process against Glencore’s Lomas Bayas copper mine for failing to meet required water and flora/fauna monitoring standards, including missing observation-well records and improper aquifer measurement records. The charges could pressure operations or raise compliance costs in the near term. The mine produced 64,300t copper last year. | GNRI is exploring a sale of its Khoemacau copper mine in Botswana, which produces about 60,000–65,000 tonnes of copper annually. The reported possible deal value (~$1.5bn) is uncertain and no operational changes are announced, limiting immediate impact on near-term copper supply expectations. | Callinex began a 20,000-meter drilling program at its Pine Bay Project in Manitoba, focusing on infill and step-out drilling to define the high-grade Rainbow copper deposit ahead of a 43-101 resource estimate. A second rig will test geophysical targets along the district trend for additional mineralized zones. | GNRI is exploring options, including a possible sale, of its Khoemacau copper-silver mine in Botswana. Bloomberg reports advisers are identifying potential suitors, valuing the asset at nearly $1.5bn. news_summary: Rystad Energy forecasts a growing global copper deficit driven by electrification demand. The article highlights Cyprium Metals’ planned restart of Australia’s Nifty mine, showing costs well below the recent copper price and potential free cash flow. It also notes analyst expectations of Chinese demand recovery later in 2022, supporting copper prices. | China’s customs data show total exports and imports rising year-on-year. Specifically, exports of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis increased 29.2% in Jan–Apr, while copper ore and concentrates imports rose 4.5% over the same period. These trade-flow shifts can influence short-term supply expectations for aluminium and copper. | First Quantum approved a $1.25 billion expansion project in Zambia, increasing plans for copper output. The decision is likely to shift expectations toward greater copper supply, which can pressure prices in the coming days as traders start repricing the near-term supply outlook, despite construction ramp-up uncertainty. | Pacific Ridge Exploration announced it will acquire up to a 75% interest in the Chuchi porphyry copper-gold project in British Columbia. The release outlines historical drilling, copper grades, and plans for an airborne survey and summer exploration to define drill targets. It implies future supply potential, not near-term production changes. | China’s lockdowns and zero-COVID stance are keeping demand weak. Shanghai remains under restrictions and Beijing cases are rising, while Caixin Manufacturing PMI hit a two-year low. The contraction coincides with falling copper prices and an 8.8% drop in copper imports from February to March, with demand likely soft through May.

It cites the DRC’s Kamoa-Kakula ramp-up to 600,000 tonnes by Q4 2024, Zambia’s Mimbula expansion and Mopani/Uganda’s Kilembe restart plans, reinforcing a bullish copper outlook. | SMM reports Shanghai bonded-zone imported copper premiums under warrants rising to $70/mt on average and bill-of-lading premiums up to $57.5/mt. The SHFE/LME copper ratio increase keeps the import profit window open versus spot and SHFE 2205, with firm offers but thin trading as buyers balk at premiums. | Marimaca Copper reported results from 10 RC infill holes at its Marimaca oxide deposit in Chile, finding broad near-surface copper oxide mineralisation with grades exceeding the resource model in several zones. Extensions outside the prior resource block and potential expansion of the high-grade MAMIX zone were highlighted. | SMM reports that SHFE metals broadly rose on May 11, with SHFE nickel up about 3%, SHFE aluminium up 2.61%, and SHFE copper up around 0.3%. LME copper and aluminium also increased, while nickel declined, reflecting mixed global signals amid a weaker dollar and higher crude. | Copper rose 1.4% as China’s slowing COVID-19 infections eased near-term demand concerns, though “zero-COVID” restrictions remain. The report notes lockdowns and macro worries had pressured prices recently. It also cites disruptions affecting cobalt, nickel, lithium logistics and shows aluminium up while nickel falls. | Blackwolf Copper and Gold provides an exploration and permitting update for its Niblack (Cu-Au-Ag-Zn VMS) and Hyder-area projects (including Cantoo with Cu/Ag/Zn plus gold-silver). The company anticipates permits and plans multi-phase drilling, potentially expanding resources, which may modestly influence near-term copper expectations. No nickel or aluminium impacts are stated. | Copper prices rose about 1.4% as China’s COVID-19 infections slowed, easing immediate demand concerns, though partial “zero-COVID” restrictions still suppress activity. The piece notes April vehicle and cathode output weakness from lockdowns and logistics disruption, while LME aluminium also gained and nickel fell on the day. | ALX Resources said it completed six of eight planned holes in its Electra nickel-copper project in Ontario, intersecting sulphide mineralization and detecting nickel, copper and zinc via portable XRF. Additional lab results are pending and the update is exploratory, with no production or definitive resource revision. | Golden Arrow Resources agreed to option up to 85% of its Mogote copper-gold project in Argentina to Syndicate Minerals. Syndicate can earn 80% through $5m exploration spend and $1.9m cash over five years, with a further 5% after feasibility and JV formation. No immediate supply disruption is cited. | Vedanta agreed to suspend lawsuits connected to its copper mining operations in Zambia. Easing legal pressure can lower the probability of immediate operational disruptions or delays, improving near-term expectations for copper supply. This usually provides supportive sentiment for copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | Rio Tinto and Utah officials said the Kennecott refinery in Utah will begin producing tellurium, a critical mineral used in solar panels, from materials previously discarded as tailings. The U.S.-based plan targets about 20 tons/year and aims to reduce reliance on China, offering limited near-term impact on copper or aluminium prices.

news_summary: LME copper inventories climbed for over two months from a 16-year low to a seven-month high of 177,000 mt, then eased slightly to 176,550 mt by May 16. SHFE copper inventories rose 5.89% week-on-week to 55,372 mt, a two-month low, suggesting improving supply levels. | SMM reports the INE bonded copper 2207 contract rose overnight, closing at 63,280 yuan/mt (+0.80%). Copper strength was linked to a crude oil rebound and concerns about policy-driven growth risks as US Treasury yields fell. Prices are expected to trade around 63,300–63,600 yuan/ton today. | SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums at $62–84/mt, averaging $73/mt, slightly lower day-on-day, with import losses near 600 yuan/mt on the 2206 contract. A closed import window and low price ratio reduced offers and inquiries, and transactions were limited as market participants wait for logistics to resume in June. | EnGold Mines reported assays from deep drilling at its Lac La Hache property in BC, including hole AN22-05 intersecting 655m of 0.14% CuEq with chalcopyrite and bornite, plus indications of copper-bearing mineralization at depth in another hole. Results are exploration-focused with assays pending elsewhere. | Hudbay Minerals began site work at its Copper World and Rosemont copper projects in Arizona after recent legal setbacks, including surrendering a Clean Water Act permit. Opponents allege it is illegal and await further court decisions. Hudbay says it plans to extract copper, supporting potential near-term supply expectations.

news_summary: LME copper rose to its highest level in nearly two weeks, closing up 1.17%, while SHFE also gained. Support came from a weaker US dollar, improving consumption expectations, tighter Chinese cathode spot supply, and declining LME/SHFE and regional inventories. Spot premiums are expected to stay firm. | Trinidad and Tobago’s TSTT faces rising theft and vandalism of copper cables, which the minister says intensified alongside higher international copper prices. TSTT plans to shift all remaining customers from its copper network to wireless/fibre by end-August, having moved about 100,000 so far, and begins removing disused copper lines. | Global Battery Metals was granted four new surface and mineral leases in Michigan, expanding its Sawyer Camp nickel-copper exploration area. The company plans electromagnetic surveys to guide drilling targeting mafic-ultramafic intrusions potentially hosting nickel and copper mineralization. No production or reserve update is provided. | Benjamin Hill Mining reported initial diamond-drilling results from its Sonora Copper Zone in Mexico, including multiple copper-bearing intercepts (e.g., up to 1.2% Cu over 6m and 0.55% Cu over 26.54m) confirming continuity of mineralized veins. The company expects further assays to support copper potential. | Gauteng authorities say rampant infrastructure theft is rising as demand for copper pushes prices higher. Copper is allegedly smelted, mixed with legitimate supply, and exported with manipulated customs declarations. The city is considering drones and micro-dot tracing to curb theft amid rolling blackouts. | Hudbay said a U.S. District Court ruled in its favor over the Copper World project in Arizona, dismissing a preliminary injunction request and clarifying that the Army Corps of Engineers has no obligation to include Copper World in Rosemont’s NEPA review. Hudbay continues early works, drilling, and expects a PEA in Q2 2022.

news_summary: Overnight, LME and SHFE copper futures rose modestly, with spot cathode premiums in China improving. The weaker US dollar (after Fed minutes) and supportive infrastructure-growth expectations supported risk assets. Falling LME/SHFE inventories and tight China supply further limited downside, keeping near-term copper bullish. | SMM says copper inventories in major Chinese markets fell 2,200 mt to 107,600 mt by May 27, with a 12,300 mt drop versus last Friday. The decline follows lower supply and higher demand, though Shanghai rose due to more imports and logistics delays. Next week’s arrivals may lift inventories slightly. | Copper rose after a three-day slump as the dollar weakened, while worries about China’s economic slowdown persisted. Nickel on the Shanghai Futures Exchange climbed up to 5% after three days of declines. Separately, a strike at Rusal’s Guinea bauxite mine raised supply disruption concerns for aluminium-linked markets. | Copper rose after a three-day slump, supported by a weaker U.S. dollar, but the article says copper was still set for weekly losses due to worries about China’s demand amid slowing growth. It also references an aluminium-related Rusal mine strike and low Chinese nickel inventories lifting nickel prices. | SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums slipping amid oversupply and import losses. Premiums are weaker under warrants and bill-of-lading ahead of June QP, with sluggish trading and minimal buyer inquiries. The SHFE/LME ratio decline and falling SHFE copper relative pricing likely weigh on near-term copper sentiment. | Copper rebounded after three sessions of losses as a weaker U.S. dollar and hopes of China easing COVID curbs boosted demand expectations. China’s copper futures also rose. The piece also cites an illegal strike at Rusal’s Guinea bauxite mine, supporting aluminium, while aluminium and nickel prices gained on the day. | Cordoba Minerals reported diamond drilling results from its 100%-owned San Matias project in Colombia, including copper-bearing intervals at the Alacran deposit and related targets. Intercepts showed grades consistent with the resource model, but no clear evidence of a deeper porphyry source was found. No immediate market supply disruption was indicated. | C3 Metals says it will commence a 5,000-metre drilling program at its Bellas Gate copper-gold porphyry project in Jamaica, starting at the Epidote Ridge target and then testing additional porphyry and epithermal prospects. The release is exploration-focused with historic copper-gold grades but no immediate supply impact. | C3 Metals says it will start a 5,000-metre drilling program at its Bellas Gate copper-gold porphyry targets in Jamaica, beginning with the Epidote Ridge area and then moving to other porphyry and epithermal targets. The release is exploration focused and does not indicate immediate changes to copper supply or demand. | A federal judge rejected a request to block Hudbay Minerals from preparing a planned new copper mine site near Tucson. Copper supply timeline risk eased.

news_summary: LME copper held near a three-week high as China demand optimism rose. Better Chinese factory data and easing COVID-19 lockdowns, including Shanghai reopening, improved near-term consumption expectations. The U.S. dollar’s limited strength provided support, while copper was steady near $9,537/tonne and other metals were mixed to slightly higher. | SMM reports the INE bonded copper 2208 contract trended higher on May 31, closing up 0.30% at 64,430 yuan/mt. It attributes the move to a continued decline in the US dollar index and expectations around the next Fed rate-hike and balance-sheet shrink timeline. | Cordoba Minerals begins feasibility-study technical work and a 40,000-metre infill drilling program at its San Matias copper-gold-silver project in Colombia. The company cites prior PFS economics using US$3.60/lb copper and notes the current spot copper price, but provides no new supply or demand changes that would move prices. Nickel/aluminium not mentioned. | Peru protests disrupted copper mining for nearly two months, cutting production and hurting revenues for Southern Copper. The article notes rising demand drivers (EVs, infrastructure) while supply remains constrained, implying potential tightening. It also highlights a concurrent increase in copper prices during the quarter. | A South African court sentenced a PRASA worker to 12 years for stealing copper cables worth R1m (about 192kg) from a railway line. Prosecutors said cable theft harms service delivery and stressed renewed enforcement, but the incident appears localized with no clear broader effect on copper supply or pricing.
