news_summary: London copper prices climbed to the highest level in over a month as China began relaxing COVID-19 lockdowns and sentiment improved ahead of possible U.S. tariff easing. The piece links the move to expectations of a faster demand recovery, alongside a broader positive tone in industrial metals. | SMM reports sluggish copper concentrate trading and stable treatment charges, but market focus is on supply risk: Peru’s Las Bambas copper mine has been suspended for over a month with renewed conflict, and authorities extended a state of emergency. Negotiations and concentrate availability keep pricing coefficients steady, with tighter supply concerns supportive for copper. | SMM reports copper prices rebounded last week as China rolled out economy-stabilizing policies, firms resumed work, and downstream wire/cable and cathode rod demand improved with grid project start-ups and restocking. Supply tightness is supported by Las Bambas suspension and delayed Daye output, despite some regional flows pressuring spots. | Rio Tinto’s subsidiary Kennecott Exploration has started farm-in drilling at Alderan Resources’ Frisco copper project in Utah, targeting porphyry and high-grade copper at the Copper Gulch prospect. Prior drilling and historical assays show substantial copper grades, strengthening near-term optimism for copper discovery and development. | MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine in Peru has been suspended for over 40 days due to indigenous community protests, with about 3,000 contractor workers laid off. Meanwhile, treatment charges for imported copper concentrate jumped 166% to $78.66/mt by June 2, reflecting tightening concentrate availability and bullish copper sentiment. | SMM said copper inventories in major Chinese markets fell 1,500 mt to 108,900 mt as of June 6, with most regions declining after the Dragon Boat Festival. Shanghai and Guangdong dropped, while Jiangsu and Tianjin rose slightly. Outlook: supply should keep rising, but weak consumption may rebuild inventories. | Western Copper and Gold announced its 2022 exploration and drilling program at the Casino copper-gold project in Yukon. The company plans to drill and scan core to evaluate a deep MT low-resistivity copper-gold anomaly and conduct geotechnical/hydrological work for project facilities. No production impacts or copper price drivers are specified. | Ivanhoe Mines says Kamoa-Kakula set a record May output of 29,800 tonnes of copper in concentrate, despite brief planned maintenance. Phase 2 ramp-up and commissioning of a filter press support higher throughput and keep 2022 guidance toward the upper end, reinforcing near-term copper supply expectations. | The news reports that Camino has completed three drillholes at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru. Positive drilling progress can modestly lift sentiment around the project’s potential size and development outlook, which may support copper price expectations in the near term. No direct details on nickel or aluminium are provided. | Trinidad and Tobago’s Scrap Iron Dealers Association urged the Ministry of Trade to shut down the copper export industry, arguing it is the only way to stop theft of copper wires and fixtures. The group claims licensed exporters are limited and wants police to inspect export containers, potentially reducing copper export flows. | Chile’s April copper output fell 8.9% to about 420,000 tonnes versus April 2021, with declines attributed to lower refining rates and worse ore grades. Cochilco also flagged tighter concentrate supply as Chinese smelter maintenance nears end, pressuring copper concentrate TC/RC charges and refining expectations. | Infinitum Copper invites investors to visit its PDAC 2022 booth and describes its copper exploration assets, including the La Adelita copper project in Mexico and the Hot Breccia property in Arizona. The piece is promotional and does not report new discoveries, production changes, or supply-demand shocks affecting copper prices. | Scrap dealers in Trinidad and Tobago are calling for a two-to-three month suspension of copper exports until suspected theft networks are identified. The proposal includes stricter export licensing, container checks at the port, and tighter scrapyard controls on purchase of scrap, aiming to reduce the market that enables theft. | Infinity Stone Ventures begins a reconnaissance exploration program at its Zen-Whoberi copper-cobalt-(plus platinum/palladium) project in Quebec. The work aims to confirm historical results, including reported copper intercepts, before potential drill work later in 2022. The release does not suggest immediate production effects for metals markets.

news_summary: SMM says China’s copper cathode output fell in May because of intensive smelter maintenance, with delayed maintenance and some raw-material tightness in the spot market. Blister copper supply remained tight, imports were disrupted, and blister TCs kept declining. June output is forecast higher but most smelters stay in maintenance. | Nevada Copper said geotechnical issues at its Pumpkin Hollow underground mine have delayed access to East South stopes, reducing expected concentrate production in Q2 2022 and pushing most stope ore production to August. It also details progress in East North development and seeks additional funding. | SMM reports INE bonded copper 2208 trending downward, closing at 64,980 yuan/mt (down 170 yuan/mt). The drop is attributed to higher US bond yields from inflation worries and a slight rebound in the US dollar index, which weighed on copper prices and encouraged shorts. | Bell Copper announced full assay results from drillhole BS-3 at its Big Sandy porphyry copper project in Arizona, including 200 meters grading 0.42% copper and metallurgical scoping work for chalcocite recovery. The company states future drilling will test supergene enrichment; no resource is yet defined. | Granite Creek Copper announced it is starting a 2022 Induced Polarization (IP) survey at its Carmacks Cu-Au-Ag project in Yukon, Canada, to improve targeting of deeper and offset mineralization. The release focuses on exploration planning and resource/PEA work; it does not indicate production or supply changes. | Goldman Sachs metals strategist Nick Snowdon warns that predictions of copper supply constraints could prove correct, potentially driving copper to $15,000/ton. The outlook rests on sharply rising demand over the next decade from electric vehicles, power and renewable projects, while supply remains insufficient to meet that growth. | Copper Fox reported interim financial results and outlined continued exploration work across its copper projects, including metallurgical drilling updates and planned 2022 field programs. Management expects further results that could support future resource estimates, but no production or supply commitments were changed, implying limited near-term impact on copper pricing.

news_summary: LME copper ended lower on June 7, falling 0.47% to $9,699/mt, as a stronger US dollar reinforced concerns about China’s demand. While LME copper inventories dropped by 20,200 mt to a multiweek low, aluminium inventories fell to a 21-year low, yet aluminium futures still slipped. | KCM’s provisional liquidator said it will seek equity investors to fund Zambia’s Konkola Deep Mining Project and announced the resumption of open-pit mining. KCM also plans $17 million in annual maintenance shutdowns at the Nchanga copper smelter (311,000 mt capacity) alongside measures to expand production at other operations. | Cyprium Metals said initial reverse-circulation drilling at its Maroochydore copper-cobalt deposit in Western Australia extended near-surface mineralisation to 3,000m. Several intervals reported significant copper grades (up to 1.92% Cu) alongside cobalt, supporting prospects and potential future development, mildly bullish for copper sentiment. | A Chinese firm is demanding revisions to its contract for the Aynak Logar copper mining project near Kabul, Afghanistan. The Logar deposit is described as the world’s second-largest copper deposit. The contract dispute could delay development, weighing on expectations for future copper supply. | Chile’s central bank said copper exports in May totaled $3.76 billion, down 18.3% year on year. Despite the decline, Chile posted a $851 million trade surplus. Overall exports rose and imports increased as well, but the YoY fall in copper export receipts is a negative copper supply-demand signal. | CanAlaska, via partner Metal Energy, has started a 10,000-metre phase two drill program at the Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba, targeting nickel-copper sulphide mineralization near a past-producing mine. The release cites prior drilling success and notes a strengthening nickel market, but no assays or production changes are provided. | Bell Copper reports shareholder approval of a shareholder rights plan and a new 2022 fixed stock option plan, including granting 10 million incentive stock options. The company is focused on exploring copper projects in Arizona, but the release is mainly governance/compensation with no direct change to copper production or trade flows. | CanAlaska’s partner has started a 10,000-metre phase-two drilling program at the Manibridge high-grade nickel project in Manitoba, targeting extensions near a past-producing mine. The program aims to define nickel-copper sulphide mineralization; earlier drilling intersected nickel mineralization and copper/nickel were confirmed with assays pending. | Fourteen people were killed in an armed dispute between small-scale miners in southern Peru. Reuters notes the violence follows earlier unrest, including a fire allegedly set by wildcat copper miners at Southern Copper Corp’s Los Chancas project, raising risks of disruption to copper supply.

news_summary: Peruvian indigenous communities agreed on June 9 to lift protests against MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine after a shutdown lasting over 50 days. The communities have approved a 30-day truce and reopening negotiations, easing disruption risk. With Las Bambas among the top copper producers, copper supply expectations should improve. | SMM says Yangshan copper premiums under warrants were $45–60/mt, stabilizing after stopping their fall as domestic premiums rose and import losses narrowed. Importers slowed dumping and buyers preferred domestic copper. Traders showed weak willingness for only small orders, limiting sharp premium upside. | Congo’s state mining firm Gecamines and China Moly dispute over Tenke Fungurume (TFM) led to a temporary court-appointed administrator taking charge. China Moly denies any management change and says production continues normally. The enforced ruling and royalty/reserve concerns create copper risk, though impact seems limited short term. | Castillo Copper outlined strategic updates across its Australian and Zambian projects, emphasizing drilling campaigns and efforts to formalize off-take and processing paths for deposits including Big One and Cangai. The company is also advancing copper targets in Zambia via potential joint-venture funding and continued exploration. | Kodiak Copper Corp. provided an update on its fully funded 2022 exploration program at the 100% owned MPD copper-gold porphyry project in southern British Columbia. The company restarted drilling in March, added a second rig, completed 11 holes, and plans further drilling and 3D IP/soil geochemistry, with assays expected in late June. | Kodiak Copper Corp. provided an update on its fully funded 2022 exploration at the MPD copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia. The company reported drilling progress at the Gate Zone, a new 3D IP survey underway, and planned assay results in late June, with further drilling across multiple targets. | MMG plans to restart copper concentrate production at Las Bambas in Peru. The restart should lift near-term concentrate availability and reduce outage risk, which can improve expectations for copper supply/demand tightness over the coming days to weeks. | China Moly denies the DRC has taken control of its Tenke Fungurume copper-cobalt mine. Congo authorities said a court-appointed administrator assumed charge amid a shareholder dispute tied to suspected reserve underreporting and royalty payments. China Moly says management and production continue normally as talks proceed.

news_summary: SMM said Chinese copper inventories across major markets fell to 109,900 mt on June 17, down 5,800 mt from Monday. Shanghai and Jiangsu declined sharply as copper prices dropped and downstream firms restocked with pandemic-related consumption recovery. Next week’s arrivals may rise, but further inventory falls are possible if demand improves. | Nornickel and the Murmansk Region government signed a memorandum to support local investment and “Big Copper,” a green roast-leach-electrowin copper production facility in Monchegorsk. The project includes high sulphur recovery and zero-discharge water management, plus capacity reconfiguration at Kola MMC to raise non-ferrous output. | SMM data estimates global copper mines will add up to 164,000 mt of new copper capacity in 2022, alongside further expansion lifting total new concentrate output to about 1.023 million mt. Major contributors include projects in Chile, Peru, Russia, Indonesia and Zambia, signaling rising supply expectations. | Copper prices edged up as traders priced in strike risk at Chile’s Codelco, the world’s largest copper producer, alongside slightly better China activity data. LME copper rose 0.4% on the day, while aluminium weakened on a firmer dollar and softer China prospects; nickel rose modestly.

news_summary: Japanese firms are reportedly substituting away from copper as copper prices surge. Daikin plans to cut copper usage by about 60% in 2023, and Panasonic is considering air-conditioner models using aluminium instead of copper for core components. The shift may dampen copper demand and mildly lift aluminium demand. | Yangshan imported copper premiums under warrants rose modestly, while import losses versus the 2207 contract narrowed slightly. Buyers adopted a wait-and-see stance as profits improved, but sellers kept quotes largely steady. Cathode continues to clear from bonded warehouses, including non-standard sources, supporting stable near-term supply pricing. | Surge Copper reported a 96% increase in Measured & Indicated resources at its Ootsa Property to 439 Mt grading 0.32% CuEq (plus inferred tonnage). news_summary: Copper prices plunged to a 16-month low as investors grew more concerned that aggressive central-bank tightening will spur a recession. LME benchmark copper fell 4.2% to $8,409/tonne, down about 14% from early-month peak, reflecting expectations of reduced industrial demand for metals. | Chile’s copper miners (about 50,000) have launched an indefinite strike at mines and refining facilities after the Boric government announced the permanent closure of the Ventanas copper refinery and planned layoffs. The work stoppage threatens near-term copper supply from Chile, a key global producer, adding upside support to prices. | SMM said copper inventories across major Chinese markets fell 1,300 mt to 102,300 mt vs Monday, and 7,600 mt vs last Friday, with the sharpest decline in Guangdong linked to higher rod-plant operating rates. However, end-June fund withdrawals are expected to bring more sellers, less buyers, and inventories to bottom out and rise next week. | Global stocks rose after Powell reiterated a fight against inflation, but risk-off factors lingered. The dollar eased and Treasury yields moved, yet copper slid to a 16-month low near $3.7/lb. The drop is attributed to recession fears, despite a strike at Chile’s Codelco, the biggest producer. | Global stocks rebounded while copper continued to slide. Reuters notes LME copper was down 0.5% to $8,367/t after earlier touching $8,122.50. The move is linked to easing inflation expectations and a growing recession outlook from aggressive central-bank rate hikes, lowering demand expectations for commodities. | Gold slipped for a second week as recession fears weighed on risk sentiment. Copper was highlighted for posting its biggest weekly percentage loss in a year, with the most-active contract down 6.8% and Thursday settling at the lowest level in about 16 months. Macro factors and the dollar drove moves. | TNR Gold highlights its 0.4% NSR royalty on McEwen Mining’s Los Azules copper project in Argentina. McEwen’s update includes multiple long copper intercepts (up to 1.38% Cu over 54m) and progress on drilling, modeling, and site access, supported by additional project financing. | World stocks were set for a weekly gain as investors hoped falling commodities, led by copper, would curb runaway inflation. The week saw steep commodity declines amid concerns the global economy is shaky and rate hikes could hurt growth, prompting traders to reduce inflation expectations and bets on hike size. | Stocks rallied as investors tempered inflation expectations, but benchmark LME copper fell further, down 0.5% on Friday and near multi-month lows. The move reflects easing recession worries from commodity price slides; however, the article still highlights continued downside pressure on copper versus recent peaks. | Copper has fallen more than 20% from its peak, entering a technical bear market, alongside intensifying recession fears. The piece attributes weakness to rapidly rising interest rates and inflation choking global growth, with analysts saying copper’s sensitivity to recession risk is rising and could weigh on demand. | Gold was slightly firmer on Friday but down for the week, with traders waiting for inflation data to gauge Fed rate-hike expectations. The key point is copper’s large weekly drop, losing 6.8%, driven by recession fears and expectations for weaker copper demand.

news_summary: SMM reviews China’s copper rod market in H1 2022. COVID lockdowns in Shanghai and other cities sharply lowered cathode rod operating rates and hurt downstream demand. A March VAT policy and a narrowing copper-cathode/scrap spread constrained recycled supply and margins, weakening secondary rod output. Power and real-estate demand also slowed. | Xinhua reports Chilean experts say copper has fallen about 9% week-on-week to $375.6/lb, the lowest in 16 months. They link the drop to higher U.S. Fed rates boosting the dollar and recession fears that reduce global demand for copper. Brief Codelco strike news failed to support prices. | Western Copper and Gold reported positive feasibility results for its Casino copper-gold-molybdenum project, including project economics that depend on assumed base-case copper prices. The article details processing design, recovery improvements, and inflation-driven cost increases, but it does not indicate imminent production or supply disruptions that would move metal prices soon. | Fighting between Myanmar junta forces and anti-junta militias has escalated around the Chinese-owned Letpadaung copper mine near Sagaing, with nearby villages raided and residents displaced. Wanbao says production has been suspended for over 16 months, while rebels accuse the company of supporting the military. Supply disruption risk weighs on copper. | A U.S. appeals court denied Apache Stronghold’s bid to halt construction of the Oak Flat copper mine, affirming the site’s sacred status but ruling religious arguments under federal law and the First Amendment were insufficient to overturn the land swap. The ruling reduces legal risk for Resolution Copper’s project timeline.

news_summary: SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets fell by 15,300 mt from Monday to 94,500 mt, and by 26,200 mt versus last Friday. The largest drop was in Guangdong, driven by lower import inflows and shipments into warehouses, while downstream demand was steady, supporting firm spot premiums. | SMM said copper inventories in China’s bonded zones fell by 2,000 mt to 276,300 mt as of July 22, continuing a four-week decline but at a slower pace. Shanghai was flat while Guangdong declined. Poor import trading, limited warrant shipments, and steady inflow into bonded warehouses kept the drawdown modest. | Nippon Denkai will build a $150 million copper foil plant in Augusta, Georgia instead of South Carolina, targeting EV battery supply. It expects to scale output from 9,500 metric tons to 28,500 metric tons and invest up to $430 million. Officials also highlighted electricity-cost advantages and incentives. | S&P Global says energy-transition demand for copper from EVs, solar/wind and power-grid equipment will rise sharply and likely outstrip supply even with record mining and recycling. Regulatory, permitting and long mine-development timelines dampen new supply growth. The report warns net-zero goals may be threatened, implying tighter copper outlook.

news_summary: Codelco, the world’s largest copper producer, will start constructing a US$1 billion desalination plant this year in northern Chile to address a decade-long drought. The project aims to cut fresh-water use by 60% via desalinated seawater and tailings reuse, supporting stable operations around Tocopilla. | CME Group announced record open interest in copper options. The update signals rising participation in copper derivatives markets, which can increase hedging and speculative positioning. However, without information on option skew, strikes, or implied price moves, it is not a clear bullish or bearish price catalyst. | Copper Mountain Mining reported Q2 2022 results, with copper output falling to 13.3 million lb due to lower ore grade, throughput limits, crusher issues, and staffing/supply chain disruptions. The company expects higher grades and recovery in H2 after Phase 4 ore release and plant optimization, while discussing higher Q2 costs. | MMG said it suspended its full-year copper output targets for Peru’s Las Bambas after protests disrupted operations and cut production by about 60% in the second quarter. The mine is currently operating at full capacity, but no new guidance was issued and tensions remain as community truce talks expire.

news_summary: SMM reviews China’s copper rod industry, noting oversupply, low capacity utilization, and demand weakness from wire/cable and enamelled wire amid pandemic disruptions. It discusses rising secondary (scrap-based) rod capacity, competitive pricing, and the role of lower copper prices in operating-rate rebounds. Grid and new-energy orders may support recovery later. | Fabled Copper Corp. provided additional 2021 surface sampling results from its Muskwa Project in northern British Columbia, including chip and float samples with reported copper grades up to 5.22% Cu. The update is exploratory geology-focused with no immediate implications for supply, exports, or inventories. | Peruvian mining executives say they are losing faith in President Castillo’s ability to support the sector despite his tax proposals shifting. Reuters cites social protests that caused month-long production halts at two major copper mines and delayed a pipeline of new copper projects worth $53 billion, amid high copper prices. | Peruvian mining executives say President Castillo’s moderation hasn’t stopped community protests that have triggered month-long production halts at major copper mines and delayed a large $53 billion project pipeline. While copper prices have fallen but remain high, firms warn instability and permit delays could reduce future supply and investment. | Metallic Minerals says it has started its 2022 drilling campaign at the La Plata silver-gold-copper project in Colorado, aiming to extend the Allard deposit and refine copper-silver-gold targets that could lead to an expanded 2023 resource estimate. The company also extends warrant expiry; no production changes are mentioned. | Eagle Plains announced that partner Rockridge has started a ~1,000m summer diamond drilling program at the high-grade Knife Lake copper VMS project in Saskatchewan. The program targets regional zones and infill/expansion around the deposit. Rockridge is fully funded and permitted, with drilling planned through about six to eight holes. | Aztec Minerals began a 10-hole, 3,000m core drilling program at its Cervantes gold-copper project in Sonora, Mexico to expand and test oxide cap and deeper copper-gold targets. While it highlights copper-bearing intercepts, it does not change near-term production, so price impact is likely limited. | Peruvian mining executives told Reuters they have lost faith in the government, citing social protests that caused month-long halts at the Cuajone and Las Bambas copper mines and slowed project pipelines. Several expansions are waiting approvals, keeping near-term copper supply and investment sentiment under pressure despite high prices. | Copper prices fell sharply as recession fears dominated markets despite tight inventories and studies projecting long-term demand growth. The article cites IMF downgrades and weaker trade signals, with commentary that “Dr. Copper” reflects economic slowdown expectations. Overall, near-term sentiment is bearish and selling pressure is driving losses.

news_summary: SMM reports Aug 1 copper cathode premiums in Shanghai rose to 330–410 yuan/mt but still failed to stimulate buying because premiums and SHFE futures remained high. Expected weekend imports of hydro-copper did not arrive, yet backwardation and elevated prices capped demand and pulled quotes lower. Future premiums may fall further. | Chilean authorities are investigating a large sinkhole that appeared over the weekend near land operated by Canadian Lundin Mining’s copper mine in northern Chile. While the cause is unclear, such ground damage can threaten mine stability, potentially disrupting near-term copper output and expectations. | Chile is investigating a giant sinkhole at Lundin’s underground copper mine. Such an incident raises the risk of production disruptions, potential safety shutdowns, and near-term supply concerns, which can weigh on market sentiment for copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | Koba Resources completed induced polarisation (IP) surveys over its historic Blackpine cobalt-copper project ahead of maiden drilling in September, following an earlier maiden program at the nearby Colson project. The work targets extensions of copper-cobalt mineralisation, but no production changes are reported.

Rebound is expected no earlier than mid-August as arrivals increase. | SMM reports Shanghai copper cathode spot premiums sliding 160 yuan/mt day-on-day to 60-100 yuan/mt over SHFE 2208 due to continuous inflows of imported copper and traders dumping for cash after backwardation eased. With smelter overhauls finished and expectations of more imports, premiums could fall further. | Copper thieves are harvesting telecommunications cables in Mayaro, Trinidad and Tobago, repeatedly disrupting phone, internet, and point-of-sale/ATM services. TSTT attributes outages to malicious theft of fibre optic and copper cables and reports additional fibre cuts. Authorities have arrested 300 people for copper theft since January. | Fabled Copper Corp. reports progress on 2022 field exploration at its Muskwa Copper Project in northern British Columbia. Activities include sampling, geophysics, drone mapping, and a pending drill permit. It also covers copper exploration drilling updates and recent copper sales/production figures for several miners, reinforcing expectations of ongoing copper tightness. No nickel or aluminium catalysts are mentioned. | South Africa’s Hawks arrested two guards accused of cutting and stealing Transnet copper cables in Mooi River. Markets also discounted the prospect of more Fed rate hikes and recession risks. news_summary: Zambia’s copper output fell 7.4% year-on-year in H1 2022 to 364,089 mt, with exports also weakening. Output at major First Quantum Minerals mines dropped due to rains and COVID-19 restrictions, making the 2022 production target likely unattainable. The supply shortfall supports expectations for tighter copper availability. | McEwen Copper reported drill intercepts at its Los Azules project in Argentina, highlighting long intervals of copper mineralization, including several higher-grade sections (up to 1.38% Cu over 44.0 m). Drilling will resume in October to support an updated PEA, but there is no near-term supply impact. | CanAlaska announced winter 2022 drill assay results at its Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba, reporting high-grade nickel-sulphide mineralization in all drill holes and continuity beneath the former mine. The release also notes the mineralization contains copper and cobalt, but provides no production or inventory updates. | Atlantic Nickel reported record 1H22 performance from the Santa Rita nickel sulphide mine in Brazil, including concentrate production and cost figures, and stated it has hedged nickel and copper prices. The update is primarily company-specific without evidence of near-term supply disruption or major demand/policy changes. | Experts are investigating a large sinkhole near Lundin Mining’s Alcaparrosa copper mine in Chile’s Atacama desert. While the company reports no impact to personnel or equipment, development work in the underground mine has been temporarily suspended as a safety measure. Disruption at Chile’s major copper source is bearish for prices. | Thieves stole fibre optic cables, targeting copper despite police believing they sought copper wire. The scrap dealers association links the surge in theft to soaring copper prices and warns exports and utility operations could be disrupted. Authorities are considering shutting the scrap industry or banning copper sales amid rising losses and illegal scrap trading.

news_summary: SMM says copper inventory across major Chinese markets fell 500 mt to 68,900 mt week-on-week, with Guangdong hitting an SMM record low. The decline is attributed to Guangxi smelter overhaul, temporary shutdown of Guangdong lines, and limited imported inflows. High spot premiums may reduce rod producer consumption. | SMM reports copper futures falling over multiple sessions, with SHFE copper down and LME copper weaker. It cites hawkish Fed expectations and earlier price declines hurting orders. Offsetting positives include rapid destocking of domestic copper inventories, limited smelter output from maintenance and scrap tightness, and rising cable demand from infrastructure spending. | Atlantic Nickel and Appian report record 1H22 performance from the Santa Rita nickel sulfide mine in Brazil, including 8,000 t of nickel contained in concentrate, low C1 cash costs of about $4.95/lb net of by-products, and debt-free positioning. The results suggest supportive near-term nickel supply outlook. | Murchison Minerals says it has commenced Phase III diamond drilling at its HPM Quebec nickel-copper-cobalt project, targeting expansion and delineation at the Barre de Fer zone and the nearby Syrah target. The company plans ~7,500 m and references assay grades, but no production or supply changes are announced. | Digicel in Trinidad and Tobago says 10,000 customers lost service due to vandals cutting lines to steal copper over five months. Digicel notes its network is fibre (no copper), so theft damages infrastructure and forces costly repairs. Thieves are also stealing batteries at cell towers and some cable-wire arrests follow. | Nevada Copper updated operations and financing for its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine. The company is advancing restart planning but restart activities depend on securing long-term restart funding. It is in default on some obligations and seeks interim and additional financing from creditors, including shareholder Pala. | Nevada Copper reported progress on plans to restart its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine, but activities remain in temporary suspension while it seeks long-term restart funding. The company is discussing a larger financing package and may rely on further interim promissory notes. Restart timing depends on securing financing.

Rumoured Qinhuangdao resales had limited spillover, as most smelters rely on long-term orders. | Rio Tinto would have needed to raise its offer to Turquoise Hill minority shareholders to win board endorsement for a full takeover. Turquoise Hill’s chairman says talks ended, potentially blocking the plan to lift Rio’s stake to 100%. The news implies deal uncertainty rather than direct copper supply or demand changes. | Rio Tinto’s proposed takeover of a large copper mine was rebuffed, according to the article headline. The rejection likely introduces uncertainty about future ownership and development timelines for the asset, which may negatively affect near-term expectations for copper supply and thereby pressure copper prices. | SMM says copper inventory in major Chinese markets fell 400 mt to 70,300 mt from Friday, and is far lower than last year. Low imported arrivals and reduced east-China smelter output contributed to the draw, while some flow shifted to Guangdong. Resumption of smelters may later increase stockpiles. | Quebec’s environment minister set a new arsenic emission limit for the Horne copper smelter in Rouyn-Noranda at 15 nanograms per cubic metre, lower than its current 100 and above the provincial norm. The measure responds to health concerns, including higher lung cancer rates in local residents. | Quebec has allowed a copper smelter in Rouyn-Noranda to emit arsenic levels up to five times the norm. The regulatory decision may reduce compliance pressure and help sustain smelting throughput, supporting near-term copper supply expectations and potentially aiding copper prices in the coming days. | Quebec’s environment minister approved a new arsenic emissions cap for Glencore’s Horne copper smelter in Rouyn-Noranda, lowering the limit to 15 nanograms per cubic metre over five years from 100. Sichuan also restricted industrial power use (Aug 15–20). Net effect is lower regional demand, likely weighing on copper prices near term. | SMM reports China’s domestic copper inventories in bonded zones fell 22,600 mt to 194,000 mt as of Aug 19, with drops in Shanghai and Guangdong. news_summary: Culpeo Minerals reported expanded high-priority drill targets after mapping confirmed large-scale copper oxide and breccia mineralisation at the Lana Corina project in Chile. Recent drilling returned multiple substantial copper and molybdenum intercepts, and the mineralised footprint increased to 500m by 400m. The update is exploration-focused. | China’s copper scrap imports rose 4.02% year-on-year to 155,168.64 mt in July, though they fell 6% versus the previous month. The US remained the biggest supplier at 26,559.12 mt, down 15.21% month-on-month but up 16.55% year-on-year. | SMM reports East China copper scrap throughput rose sharply week-on-week as cargo holders sold some high-priced inventories, while downstream secondary rod firms urgently restocked. Copper cathode prices stayed high, spreads to scrap widened, imports fell, inventories were extremely low, and power rationing restricted output—supporting bullish near-term copper sentiment. | Aston Bay announced that drilling at its Storm Copper Project in Nunavut intersected over 68m of stratiform copper sulfide mineralization linked to a large EM conductor plate, with six other similar deeper anomalies still untested. The discovery suggests a potentially major sedimentary copper system; assays are expected next month.

news_summary: S&P Global/IHS Markit research warns a copper supply gap will open by 2025, with demand surging from electrification and recycling/substitution not closing the shortfall. It argues higher prices are the likely market response to persistent deficits, potentially constraining the net-zero energy transition. Aluminium substitution is limited to about 1% of demand. | SMM reports Shanghai spot copper premiums for cathode versus the SHFE 2209 contract fell to around 520 yuan/mt, down from the prior day, as imported copper inflows rose. Improved east/central China smelter output after power rationing also pressured premiums, though declines were moderated by still-high levels in Guangdong. | Fluor announced first copper output from Anglo American’s Quellaveco mining project in Peru. The start of production suggests incremental copper supply is beginning to reach the market, which can weigh on near-term copper price expectations, though full volume ramp-up may limit the magnitude. | QC Copper and Gold reported new drill assay results from the Opemiska property in Quebec, with multiple holes intersecting wide in-pit mineralization zones including grades up to about 1.49% copper. The program aims to upgrade inferred to indicated copper resources via strip-ratio reduction, with no immediate production impact.

news_summary: The study develops Cu2O-containing marine antifouling coatings that use amphiphilic diblock copolymers to control and prolong copper-ion release in seawater. It reports strong antifouling performance in marine field tests and comparable results to commercial self-polishing coatings. Overall, it suggests niche copper use but no immediate market impact. | Granite Creek Copper announced a non-brokered private placement to raise up to $640,000 for exploration and development. Funds target its Carmacks copper-gold-silver project and a newly acquired Star copper-nickel-PGE project in Canada. The company positions these projects for future copper output, but it doesn’t indicate immediate production changes.

news_summary: SMM reports East China copper scrap throughput rose 29% WoW to about 22,000 mt as copper prices stabilized above 63,000 yuan/mt. The cathode vs scrap spread widened, improving scrap economics and raising secondary rod operating rates to 43.51%. Demand may cool slightly, but improving logistics should support supply. | Sandfire Resources plans to invest $71.9m to expand the Motheo copper mine in Botswana, following completion of a feasibility study for the A4 deposit and processing plant upgrades. Botswana can take up to a 15% interest. | Sandfire says it has completed a definitive feasibility study to expand its Motheo copper mine in Botswana from 3.2 mtpa to 5.2 mtpa, including development of the A4 deposit and a larger processing plant. Pandemic-related disruption and weak real estate/state grid demand limit the upside, but copper-linked production should improve. | SMM says Chinese copper inventories across major markets totaled 75,000 mt, up 1,700 mt from Monday, with increases in most regions except Shanghai. Consumption is expected to improve next week as prices decline, but near-term inventories are supportive. | Copper prices fell as hawkish ECB and Fed messaging supported higher interest rates, pushing up bond yields and the US dollar and pressuring USD-priced commodities. Fundamentals also weakened: power-restricted smelters resumed production and cathode supply rose, while downstream demand/order flow stayed sluggish despite softer supply conditions. | Los Andes Copper announces closing an additional US$5 million convertible debenture investment by Queen’s Road Capital. news_summary: Forecasts for a copper “super cycle” are temporarily on hold as global demand weakens amid slowing economies. The copper spot price has fallen sharply since March, with China data, recession fears, and higher rates weighing on demand. Analysts cut forecasts, implying further near-term downside. | Hot Chili Limited announced strong copper-silver drill intercepts at its San Antonio resource with grades above current model, supporting an upgrade from Inferred to Indicated and enabling inclusion in open-pit reserve estimates. It also highlights promising Valentina results and planned follow-up drilling, boosting copper project outlook. | Hot Chili reported strong copper-silver drill intersections at Chile’s Costa Fuego, particularly upgrading the San Antonio deposit from Inferred toward Indicated status. Higher-grade results support adding ore to open-pit reserve estimates for the Pre-feasibility Study and planning further drilling at the adjacent Valentina prospect. | SMM reports spot copper concentrate trading improved, with imported copper concentrate TCs rising above $80/mt and Qinhuangdao port re-trading showing $84–85/mt levels. Inventories at the port fell week-on-week, while higher costs and potential stoppages/strikes at major mines raise concentrate supply risk. | Mitsubishi Materials will buy out remaining partners in the Onahama copper smelter in Fukushima, taking full ownership from March 31. The company says the change will improve decision-making to help the smelter handle rising electricity and fuel costs, and it plans recycled-material pre-processing. | Talon Metals and Rio Tinto’s Tamarack nickel project in central Minnesota continues to expand as explorers find thicker, higher-grade nickel-copper mineralization outside the defined resource zone. The updated results improve perceived future supply prospects for nickel and, secondarily, copper, potentially supporting prices modestly. | The headline says Rio’s bid to take over a giant copper mine faces opposition from a top investor. Such disputes over control of a major copper asset can increase uncertainty around production and supply timing, potentially weighing on short-term copper market expectations over coming days to weeks. | Comex Copper front-month for September delivery ended the week 4.66% higher at $3.5810, up 15.95 cents per pound. The contract gained for multiple sessions, reaching the highest settlement since Aug. 29, and is up modestly month-to-date despite remaining far below recent highs.

news_summary: London copper slid to a two-month low, falling about 1.1% to $7,352/t, as the U.S. dollar hit a two-decade peak and the Fed outlook turned more hawkish. Weak growth expectations pressured industrial metal demand. LME aluminium also hit an 18-month low, while ShFE nickel dropped 3.1%. | SMM says copper faced major macro pressure after a Fed rate hike with more hawkish dot plots, strengthening the US dollar and pulling LME prices down. It notes inventories fell but expected tight spot supply weakened. With RMB depreciation, copper is seen with some downside risk and fluctuating trading. | Anglo American said it will start commercial copper shipments from its Quellaveco project in Peru after successful testing and final regulatory clearance. The company revised 2022 Quellaveco guidance downward but expects strong output in 2023-2024 and tightened Chile production due to water restrictions and ore changes. | Anglo American says it will begin copper shipments from the Quellaveco project. If shipments ramp in the near term, the added physical supply could increase availability in the market and exert downward pressure on copper prices over the coming days to several weeks. | Eagle Metals says copper-based purchases dominate its volume and that copper mills remain on allocation, with limited contract flexibility. It also warns European alloy mills could shut due to high energy prices, disrupting US supply of materials like phosphor bronze. Meanwhile, EV and electrification demand is outpacing supply.

news_summary: Hindustan Copper Limited plans to expand the Malanjkhand Copper Project, increasing copper ore capacity from 2 MTPA to 5 MTPA by developing an underground mine and targeting much higher long-run production. It also mentions resuming operations at Ghatshila. Changes are largely multi-year, so near-term copper price impact is limited. | Infinitum Copper reported diamond-drill results from its La Adelita project in Mexico, including 32.25m grading 1.01% Cu and 19.30m grading 0.54% Cu, expressed as copper equivalent. The company frames the findings as expanding the skarn mineralized system, supporting future drilling plans. | Aluminum surged 8.5% on the LME after Bloomberg reported the exchange may discuss blocking new Russian metal deliveries to its warehouses. Russia’s role as a major supplier raises supply-risk concerns. Nickel also jumped about 5%, while copper was cited as potentially affected if Russian flows tighten.

news_summary: CanXGold reports completion of the first drill hole at its Phoenix property in British Columbia, targeting a large conductivity anomaly that the company frames as potential copper-related mineralization. The hole reached 337 meters and intersected altered rocks and pyrite, with a second anomaly to be drilled next. news_summary: After the National Day holiday, the spread between front-month and next-month SHFE copper contracts widened, making downstream buyers cautious and reluctant to purchase. As a result, copper cathode premiums in North China fell sharply to average about +90 yuan/mt, with trade around 62,500 yuan/mt, and quotations were lowered or discounted. Premiums are expected to drop further before delivery. | Copper prices rose, supported by renewed concerns over copper availability in LME warehouses (higher cancelled warrants) and a wider cash premium. However, Chinese demand worries, expectations of further U.S. rate hikes, a stronger dollar, and rising inventories weighed on sentiment. Aluminium and nickel fell on the day amid the same macro backdrop. | Comex copper front-month for October delivery settled 1.20% higher at $3.4535 per pound, gaining 4.10 cents. The move snapped a two-session losing streak and marked a strong one-day dollar/percentage gain. The contract is up eight of the past 10 sessions and slightly positive month-to-date.

FCAB suspended copper shipments earlier this week, prompting mines to consider alternative routes. Authorities say specialized personnel, air support and better communications will resume protected transport, but theft frequency and violence remain high. | Vale Canada began the first phase of its C$945m Copper Cliff Complex South Mine Project in Sudbury, adding about 13,000 tonnes of contained copper and 10,000 tonnes of contained nickel per year for Vale’s Copper Cliff operations. The project is expected to nearly double Copper Cliff output, adding about 13,000 tonnes of copper and 10,000 tonnes of contained nickel per annum, supporting supply sentiment. | European copper buyers face record term premiums for 2023 as they self-sanction against Russian metal following UK actions affecting UMMC-linked brands. news_summary: A weekly broker-rating roundup highlights Citi’s view of short-term headwinds for base-metals demand from sluggish China, higher US interest rates and a stronger USD. Citi trims near-term price forecasts for aluminium, copper and nickel (while being comparatively more bearish on copper). Some company ratings are upgraded on valuation, but metal-price expectations lean weaker. | Copper prices were subdued as demand uncertainty from China’s continued zero-COVID curbs and global recession fears offset worries over tight supply. LME and Shanghai copper prices gave back gains. Aluminium and nickel also declined as risk sentiment weakened, despite lower Chile output and tight inventories. | Copper rose on tightening global supplies: LME/COMEX/ShFE stocks fell and Chilean production dropped sharply. China demand improved at the margin via premiums, but gains were capped by ongoing zero-COVID uncertainty and global recession fears. Aluminium and nickel fell in the same session, showing mixed risk appetite. | SMM says spot copper concentrate trading stayed sluggish, but the weekly Imported Copper Concentrate Index rose to $86.38/mt. Clean concentrate TCs for smelters moved to about $88–89/mt, while trader-smelter deals were scarce at $81–85/mt. Higher TCs and negotiation dynamics support copper sentiment despite limited transactions. | SMM attributes last week’s copper surge to a stronger US dollar from hotter CPI and Fed expectations, alongside tight copper fundamentals in China: open import window, rising cancelled LME warrants, low inventories, and sharp SHFE backwardation. With spot premiums also seen improving, SMM expects SHFE 2211 copper to rise this week. | Nelson Mandela Bay’s electricity department is replacing copper cables with cheaper aluminium to deter cable theft and vandalism. Officials report repairing hundreds of street lights daily and arresting cable thieves, while also targeting scrapyards. The substitution is demand-shifting at the city level for copper versus aluminium. | Nelson Mandela Bay’s electricity department is replacing copper cables with cheaper aluminium to reduce cable theft and vandalism. The city reports ongoing streetlight repairs and arrests of cable thieves, and says aluminium cables are installed higher to deter removal. This may modestly shift demand from copper to aluminium locally. | SMM data shows copper inventories across major Chinese markets rose by 16,100 mt to 110,300 mt, with increases in all regions. The build is attributed to smelters shipping cathode into warehouses amid a wider SHFE front/back spread and recent import arrivals, while downstream demand is slowed by COVID conditions. | Copper Fox reports completion of 4,688 meters of metallurgical drilling at its Schaft Creek copper-gold-molybdenum-silver project, aimed at improving geometallurgical data for planned metallurgical testwork. Analytical results are pending. The update is operational but lacks production or market-impacting findings in the near term. | SMM reports Linyi East China scrap market throughput surged in Oct 10-16, especially aluminium scrap, reaching 19,200 mt (+83.6% WoW). This coincided with rising A00 aluminium prices and improved downstream recycler buying after the holiday. Aluminium supply fell on trader risk concerns, while inventories declined; prices were expected stable this week. | Copper prices were subdued as demand uncertainty outweighed supply woes. LME copper gave up gains and hovered around $7,535/t while inventories fell and Chile production dropped sharply. China’s reiterated zero-COVID stance and recession fears limited upside, with aluminium and nickel also slipping. | Element 29 Resources reported its first resource estimate for the Elida copper project in Peru, with inferred tonnes grading 0.32% copper and small molybdenum credits, and plans further drilling and metallurgical studies. It also plans to update resources at Flor de Cobre. Overall, it’s copper-positive for sentiment, not a direct near-term supply driver. | The piece says copper is range-bound after a rebound, with macro pressure from Fed rate hikes weighing on demand. It also cites bullish offsets from strong Chinese refined import growth and supply disruptions in Peru and Chile, plus LME restrictions on new Russian UMMC deliveries potentially tightening supplies. | Comex copper futures for October delivery settled 0.14% higher at $3.4555 per pound, gaining 0.50 cent. The piece summarizes recent trading—up four of six sessions, near-term month-to-date up slightly, and year-to-date still down. No fundamental catalyst is discussed.

news_summary: WBMS reported a 657,000 mt global copper supply deficit in January-August 2022, following a 283,000 mt deficit in 2021. LME stocks rose vs. end-2021, but mine and cathode output increased less than demand (up 4.3% YoY). August balances also show demand above production. | Minto Metals reported copper intercepts of 2.20% over 20 metres and 3.52% over 7.26 metres from the Minto North and Cu Keel South discovery areas. The update is copper-focused, indicating promising resource potential, but it is unlikely to materially shift near-term copper prices by itself. | Freeport-McMoRan reported a sharp profit drop as recession fears and COVID-19 lockdowns in China pressured copper prices. Reuters notes LME copper has fallen about 30% from March highs and is trading in a $7,200–$8,000 range since late August. Copper production rose year-on-year, but realized prices fell. | The piece centers on a copper market that a miner describes as “strikingly tight,” suggesting supportive near-term supply conditions. The title also notes a concurrent price drop, implying either other offsetting factors or that the tightness signal may not be translating into higher prices immediately. | Strike Copper Corp. announced an exploration agreement with the Lac La Croix First Nation for its Sungold property near Thunder Bay in Ontario. The deal includes consultation, cultural/heritage protections, and compensatory benefits. The news is supportive of continued copper exploration but lacks details on near-term production or supply impacts.

news_summary: The report says cash-for-copper trading has “grinded to a halt” around Shanghai’s free-trade zone warehouses that historically anchor global physical copper pricing. It notes traders track flows in and out of the bonded copper stockpile, implying liquidity/physical exchange conditions are deteriorating, but no specific price impact mechanism is stated. | Comex copper for October delivery settled 1.23% lower at $3.4795 per pound, losing 4.35 cents. The move snapped a two-session winning streak. The contract remains down from recent highs and slightly up month-to-date, signaling near-term bearish price pressure for copper. | Copper fell on Tuesday on the LME after the Chinese yuan weakened further against the dollar. A weaker yuan makes dollar-priced commodities costlier for China, the largest copper consumer. Three-month LME copper dropped about 0.5% to around $7,516/t as the market adjusted to FX-driven demand concerns. | Falcon Gold expands its HSP South project by staking additional claims, increasing its land position by 70% in Havre St. Pierre’s anorthosite complex. The area is described as newly recognized nickel source and contiguous to Go Metals’ recent nickel-and-copper sulphide discovery, with airborne surveys planned. | Falcon Gold reported a 70% increase in its land position at the HSP South property in Quebec, adjacent to Go Metals’ drilling and discovery of wide intervals of nickel and copper sulphides. Falcon plans airborne EM surveys and expanded staking across prospective contact, potentially boosting near-term sentiment for nickel and copper. | Cordoba Minerals reported shallow high-grade copper (0.69–0.74% Cu over 152–175 m) from infill drilling at its San Matias Alacran deposit in Colombia, with copper-gold-silver intercepts to be included in an updated mineral resource estimate. The update is sentiment-supportive but not near-term production-changing. | Elemental Altus completed the sale of its Morocco-focused copper subsidiary Aterian Resources to Eastinco, generating a 2.5% NSR royalty over 15 copper-focused projects. The company also received Eastinco shares and warrants, retaining 25% equity exposure. The release is mainly a royalty/corporate transaction with indirect copper impact. | Nickel North Exploration reports 2022 sampling and backpack drilling confirming high-grade, up to 400m-long Ni-Cu sulfide mineralization at the Hawk Ridge Project’s Gamma Zone in Northern Quebec. Reported intercepts and outcrop/backpack results include strong nickel and copper grades, supporting further 2023 drilling plans.

The immediate effect is lower near-term supply from the site, which can tighten market availability in coming days and weeks and generally exerts downward pressure on copper prices. | Glencore said its own-sourced copper production for the first nine months fell 14% to 770,500 tonnes versus the prior year. news_summary: SMM cites China customs data showing September imports of unwrought copper and copper semis rose 25.6% YoY and 2.4% MoM, linked to traditional peak-season demand and attractive import margins. Copper concentrate imports rose from Peru on Las Bambas recovery, while Chile volumes fell due to mine grade declines, water issues, and accidents. | SMM reports China imported 84,800 mt of copper anode in September, down 16.67% MoM, citing reduced shipments from Zambia, Congo (DRC), and South Africa. Maintenance at Zambia/DRC smelters, a CCS production accident, and a Durban Port strike disrupted African anode and blister copper exports, though operations are recovering. | China’s copper scrap imports rose in September to 167,000 mt (+7.99% MoM) and 1.36 million mt over Jan–Sep (+9.97% YoY), helped by higher LME copper prices boosting overseas shipments. Despite the higher inflows, domestic supply tightness persisted due to COVID disruptions, so SMM expects imports to stay near current levels. | BHP’s CEO warns of a timing mismatch where demand for decarbonization metals rises before supply can scale up, implying an eventual copper shortage. Trafigura notes copper stockpiles cover only about 4.9 days of consumption and could fall further. Multiple research reports project large mine-supply gaps, supporting a copper price rebound view. | Faraday Copper Corp. started a planned 10,000-metre Phase II diamond drill program at its Copper Creek copper project in Arizona. The work aims to expand the open-pit resource, delineate underground zones, support metallurgical testwork, and add monitoring infrastructure and new targets. No production changes are announced. | EU sectors call for cautious implementation of the CBAM/ETS as part of “Fit for 55”. CBAM would charge importers for emissions certificates from 2026, initially for aluminium and other high-carbon sectors, while companies warn soaring carbon costs and energy prices have already driven unprecedented aluminium capacity idling. | Ero Copper reported Q3 2022 results, including 11,189 tonnes of copper production and ongoing cost pressure, noting a $10.3m impact from final settlements on provisionally priced copper concentrate sales and higher operating costs. The firm also disclosed progress and financing guidance for growth projects and ongoing nickel exploration.

news_summary: SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums quoted at $135–150/mt under warrants, largely flat day-on-day, with bill-of-lading premiums also steady to slightly higher. The import market is slack, but tight supply of available goods keeps sellers aiming for higher prices, sustaining physical copper support. LME cash to 3M at $65/mt; import losses ~300 yuan/mt. | Fabled Copper Corp. reported 2022 surface sampling results from its Muskwa Copper Project in northern British Columbia, including multiple rock samples above 0.5% copper and peak assays up to 16.05% copper with gold and silver. The company plans follow-up releases, but no production or supply change is announced. | Copper prices edged lower as bleak near-term manufacturing and metals demand outweighed hopes that China may ease COVID-19 controls next year. Global risk sentiment weakened ahead of a large U.S. rate hike, likely stifling activity. Tight supply and low inventories were cited as support, leaving overall bias mixed. | Base metals prices rose on Tuesday after widespread rumors that China might change/ease its zero-Covid policy. Three-month futures gained for copper (+2.7%), aluminium (+0.9%), and nickel (+8.2%). Analysts said reopening hopes lifted China risk assets and triggered short covering; nickel additionally benefited from strong EV sales. | BHP has signed a deal with Neoen for a 70-megawatt “renewable energy baseload” contract to supply round-the-clock electricity to its Olympic Dam copper mine from mid-2025. The wind-plus-battery power will cover about half of the mine’s expected power needs, improving energy supply reliability. | Arizona Sonoran Copper says its Parks-Salyer porphyry copper deposit infill drilling returned a 217.7-metre intercept grading 1.01% copper, from multiple additional holes. The updated drilling results bolster confidence in the project’s copper resource potential, which can support sentiment toward copper markets.

news_summary: The piece reports Goldman Sachs lowered copper price assumptions, expecting a 170,000-tonne refined surplus in 2023 driven by weak Chinese property and European industrial demand. Demand growth forecasts were reduced, while the firm notes visible stocks are at 18-year lows. Net effect is bearish copper sentiment near term. | Copper prices fell after China’s weaker economic indicators and a fresh rise in COVID-19 cases increased concerns about industrial demand. Despite some support from tight physical supplies, three-month LME copper slipped around 0.5% and SHFE copper diverged. Market focus now shifts to US inflation and politics. | China’s leading copper producers urged the government to expand ore prospecting and open new mines at home and abroad amid concerns over global supply disruptions. It cites reduced operations at Peru’s Las Bambas due to blockades and a longer Chuquicamata smelter maintenance in Chile, raising short-term supply tightness expectations. | Ascendant Resources said it found high-grade copper intercepts and extended a footwall lens to a 550-metre strike length at the Venda Nova South deposit, using a 200-metre step-out drill hole at Lagoa Salgada. The results are supportive for copper project prospects and potential supply expectations. | Romios Gold reports a strong IP/MT anomaly at its Trek South project and newly discovered copper-tungsten skarn, with copper assays up to 0.98% Cu and tungsten assays from 0.04%-0.68% WO3. The company highlights drill-ready targets near inferred plutons, which could be mildly supportive for copper sentiment. | Morocco-based Aterian seeks a JV partner to develop its Agdz copper project and reports high-grade copper and silver discoveries at another Moroccan asset, Azrar. It has acquired a portfolio of Moroccan copper-silver and base metal projects and discusses funding via Rwanda trading, boosting near-term sentiment for future copper supply development.

news_summary: Comex Copper (front month) settled 2.60% lower at $3.6895 per pound, losing 9.85 cents. The contract is down for four consecutive sessions, totaling a 6.62% decline over that span, and reached the lowest settlement since Nov. 7, signaling bearish momentum for copper. | A federal appeals court set aside prior rulings and will rehear the legal challenge to the Resolution Copper project on land considered sacred by Apache Stronghold. Opponents want delays to prevent destroying Oak Flat, while supporters say the mine could meet a large share of domestic copper demand. | SMM says Chinese major-market copper inventories rose to 128,000 mt as of Nov 18, up 10,900 mt from Monday, largely driven by SHFE 2211 copper delivery. Imported arrivals were limited and domestic smelter shipments were low, despite brief spot deficits. SMM expects inventories to fall next week and consumption to improve. | Arizona’s Court of Appeals overturned a permit issuance for the proposed Resolution Copper underground mine and ordered the state to restart the Clean Water Act permitting process. The mine is targeted for massive copper output. The decision raises near-term regulatory risk of delay, potentially supporting copper market sentiment. | SMM survey data show China’s domestic bonded copper inventories fell 3,700 mt from Nov 11 to 24,600 mt as of Nov 18, including declines in Shanghai and Guangdong. The drop is linked to copper entering the domestic market after contract delivery, amid signs of rising cleared imported arrivals at ports. | Arizona’s Court of Appeals ruled the state environmental agency illegally issued a Clean Water Act permit for the proposed Resolution Copper Mine, ordering ADEQ to restart the process. The San Carlos Apache Tribe opposes the mine, and further appeals are planned, adding uncertainty and potential delay to copper supply plans. | Lundin Mining plans to fill a large sinkhole near its Alcaparrosa underground copper mine in Chile. The firm is expelling leaked water and has reduced inflow rates sharply, investing $10m so far. It also plans to pump 1.3 million cubic metres of water to local industrial users, aiming to seal the affected area. | Arizona’s Court of Appeals ruled the state environmental agency illegally issued a Clean Water Act permit for the proposed Resolution Copper mine and ordered the permit process restarted. The San Carlos Apache Tribe argues the project would destroy Oak Flat, while the company reviews the decision. The mine is projected to produce large volumes of copper.

news_summary: Cobre Limited reported high-grade assay results from drill hole NCP20A at its Comet Target in Botswana’s Kalahari Copper Belt. Results include a 30m copper-silver intersection averaging 1.25% Cu and standout intervals up to 10.9% Cu, reinforcing the potential for an economic deposit and more drilling. | New World Resources reported “exceptional” deep drilling results at its Antler copper project in Arizona, including an 8.1% copper interval over 10.7 metres and ongoing mineralisation open at depth. The company says results could raise overall resource grade and indicated tonnes as scoping and pre-feasibility studies continue. | Copper rose to a three-week high as more Chinese cities eased COVID curbs, lifting expectations for fuller economic reopening. A weaker U.S. dollar also supported LME prices. The article notes aluminium and nickel gains as well, reflecting improved short-term demand sentiment for industrial metals. | Giga Metals said it will exit copper exploration in Piaui State, Brazil, because the program has not generated enough support to justify ongoing budgets. The company plans to focus instead on its Turnagain nickel project, while previous work targeted sediment-hosted copper mineralization. Near-term price effects are likely minimal.

news_summary: Goldman Sachs forecasts copper prices will reach record levels as a looming supply shortage meets growing demand, leaving the physical market in deficit in 2023. Glencore’s CEO also expects a “huge deficit,” reinforcing a bullish near-term supply-tightness narrative for copper. | China’s unwrought and copper-product imports rose 5.8% in November, supported by expectations of stronger demand next year as Beijing backs the property sector and eases COVID restrictions. Customs data show more inflows and bonded stocks. The article links improved sentiment with higher LME and Shanghai copper prices in November. | Fabled Copper Corp. announced 2022 surface fieldwork results from its Muskwa Copper Project, including assays from Target 11 and surrounding areas. Several samples show copper mineralization (some >0.5% Cu). The company plans additional releases, but the update is exploratory with no production impact. | Ring of Fire Metals (Wyloo Metals’ renamed unit) signed an MoU with Webequie First Nation to collaborate on exploration and negotiations for the proposed Eagle’s Nest mine in Ontario’s Ring of Fire. The deposit is described as high-grade nickel-copper, but the agreement signals partnership framework rather than near-term output. | Glencore says a huge copper deficit is coming, arguing current prices do not reflect the tightness. It highlights lagging mine development, elevated country/operational risks, and reduced copper investment (capex) expectations, while demand is supported by grid expansion and energy-transition targets. | The headline reports that Vale plans to split off its metals group to better capitalize on battery demand. Because the move is framed around battery-linked metals, including nickel and copper, it may boost sentiment for near-term supply and demand expectations, supporting prices even if immediate physical effects are unclear.

news_summary: London and Shanghai copper prices rose to multi-month highs as China eased COVID-19 curbs and property support improved demand expectations. Goldman lifted its copper price forecast and cited tightening visible stocks. Non-ferrous prices broadly moved higher, with aluminium and nickel also up, supported by a weaker dollar. | SMM says China’s domestic bonded copper inventories rose 4,200 mt from Dec 2 to 32,600 mt by Dec 9. Shanghai bonded inventories increased to 29,200 mt, while Guangdong was unchanged. The build followed exports from smelters and bill-of-lading arrivals, with sluggish port inquiries tied to low SHFE/LME ratio and softer Yangshan premiums. | Cordoba Minerals and JCHX will jointly develop Cordoba’s Alacran copper-gold-silver project in Colombia, with JCHX acquiring a 50% stake for $100m. Payments are tied to closing, feasibility study/EIA submission, and EIA approval. The deal could affect copper expectations but appears too slow for near-term price pressure. | Azimut and KGHM International sign a JV for Quebec’s Kukamas copper-gold property. KGHM can earn an initial 50% stake by spending $5m over four years and may invest at least $4.2m more to add 20% to fund a PEA. No production timing or near-term supply impact is stated. | Vale plans to separate its base metals operations from its iron ore business, covering nickel assets in Canada and copper/nickel production elsewhere. Management says the move will attract capital and better position Vale to meet expected EV and battery demand, with nickel and copper receiving more focused investment. | Vale plans to split its base metals business from its iron ore unit in mid-2023, isolating nickel assets including Sudbury and Voisey’s Bay. Executives cite surging battery-driven demand for nickel and copper, alongside major planned capex to expand output, supporting a bullish near-term sentiment for both metals. | US copper scrap discounts for No.1 and No.2 scrap were unchanged in recent assessments, with traders citing quiet physical demand and stable spreads into year-end. Fastmarkets’ Comex March copper settled higher, supporting a floor for brass-related prices. Overall, copper signals are mixed-to-stable and unlikely to drive a major move.

news_summary: China’s copper concentrate imports rose sharply in Nov 2022, but December saw frequent supply-side and port disruptions. The TC level is unchanged from Q4 2022. Since treatment charges impact smelter economics but this update is flat, any near-term copper price effect is likely limited. | The piece says copper could fall nearly 20% over the next 12 months. It attributes the expected decline to demand cooling amid a likely economic slowdown, while supplies are described as staying stable. The message points to weaker copper demand and downside price pressure. | Copper prices fell as markets focused on poor near-term demand prospects. Surging COVID infections in top consumer China and recession fears raised expectations of slower industrial activity. Traders looked to upcoming Chinese manufacturing PMIs for clues. LME copper ended down 0.2% and technical levels were highlighted. | Altiplano Metals reported November 2022 operating results from its Farellon copper-gold mine in Chile. Copper grade improved to 2.10% from 1.87% in October, with higher production, processing, and copper sales of 131,153 pounds. Revenue rose to about US$308,000 after costs, alongside stockpiling and ongoing ramp-up. | SMM reports that China’s 2023 import and export tariff settings for copper-related products will remain unchanged from 2022, effective Jan 1. The update indicates no new tariff shock for cross-border copper product flows, likely stabilizing expectations rather than adding bullish or bearish near-term price pressure. | An SMM survey says copper cathode-rod operating rates improved in H2 2022 due to restocking when copper prices fell and tight copper scrap supply that lifted scrap prices above cathode. The company also outlined continued capital and development work. The funding and restart momentum may modestly improve near-term expectations for copper supply. | Osisko Bermuda agreed to a revised deal involving a silver stream and a backstop copper stream to help fund Metals Acquisition Corp’s $1.1B purchase of the CSA copper-silver mine in New South Wales. Aluminium also dropped on sliding energy prices that raise smelting economics. news_summary: SMM surveys Chinese copper industry-chain firms as CNY approaches amid a renewed COVID outbreak. Many copper rod, pipe, sheet/strip, and wire/cable makers plan earlier closures due to weaker orders and lower operating rates. Resumption dates are mostly unchanged for some large firms, but overall demand appears soft pre-holiday. | Copper prices rose as expectations grow that China will support its ailing property sector, improving demand outlook and helping inventory restocking. LME copper was up 1.2%. Nickel fell sharply to a one-month low on supply expectations after Tsingshan’s new electronickel project started production. Aluminium also edged higher. | Copper prices rose on hopes China will add property-sector support measures, improving the near-term demand outlook. LME three-month copper and SHFE February copper both increased, while Chile production fell. In contrast, Shanghai nickel fell on supply expectations from Tsingshan’s new project starting production. Aluminium also edged up. | SMM says copper inventories in China’s domestic bonded zones increased by 2,800 mt from Dec 30, 2022, led by a rise in Shanghai (+3,000 mt) while Guangdong fell slightly. With sluggish imported trading ahead of Chinese New Year, SMM expects bonded inventories to grow further, weighing on sentiment. | Copper Mountain Mining reports that after a Dec. 27 ransomware attack, its Copper Mountain Mine resumed crusher and mill operations by early January and is stabilizing at full production. The company says it kept shipping copper concentrate on schedule during downtime from inventory to Vancouver, limiting immediate copper supply disruption. | Taseko reported 2022 Gibraltar copper sales of 101 million pounds and production of 97 million pounds, but noted December output was hit by an unplanned mill downtime triggered by a sitewide power outage and extreme cold. Throughput fell to ~63,000 tons/day in December versus ~88,000 in October-November. | Copper rose to a three-week high on a weaker U.S. dollar and optimism that China will boost property demand. LME three-month copper climbed about 1.2%. The piece also references Chile output declines supporting copper, while nickel weakness reflects Tsingshan project-related supply expectations and aluminium advanced. | Copper Mountain Mining said it reopened its southern British Columbia copper mine after a Dec. 27 ransomware attack. The company restarted its primary crusher and reported deliveries were unaffected. The restart should ease near-term supply disruption concerns, providing a modest supportive tone for copper markets. | AL-Monitor reports that Saudi Arabia intends to increase copper mining output to satisfy rising global demand. The announcement is expected to influence near-term market expectations for copper supply and balances, potentially supporting trading sentiment, depending on how quickly production changes versus global demand growth. | Fluor won an EPCM contract for Eldorado Gold’s Hellas Gold Skouries project in Greece. The mine is expected to produce about 67 million pounds of copper annually, with construction starting in 2023 and first production targeted for 2H 2025. The contract may modestly support copper supply expectations.

news_summary: SMM says its imported copper concentrate index fell $1.44/mt WoW as Chile’s Ventanas port shut after a fire disrupted concentrate shipments and suppressed spot TC quotes for Feb–Mar cargoes. It also cites weaker Chile output, royalty bill review, mine disruptions, and rising Chinese port concentrate inventories. | SMM says Chinese copper inventories totaled 122,500 mt as of Jan 9, up 5,000 mt from Jan 6 and sharply year-on-year. COVID-19 cases disrupted production and pre-holiday shutdowns increased stockpiling. Smelters sped up shipments to dump in-plant inventories, while processing demand stayed cautious, implying inventories may keep rising. | Northwest Copper says it has drilled 41.20 metres of 3.20% copper equivalent in the 421 zone at its Stardust project. The reported results can strengthen expectations for potential future copper supply, providing a mild supportive cue for copper prices in the near term as investors react to exploration progress. | First Quantum says it wants to appeal a Panama court order that would halt its copper mine. The move could delay or overturn the shutdown, improving expectations for near-term copper supply and tightening the immediate balance. | Transparency Market Research projects continued growth in the global copper market, driven by demand from electronics, telecommunications, construction, automotive/EVs, and medical equipment in emerging economies. It also highlights rising use of oxygen-free copper. The piece is a forecast with no near-term supply disruption or inventory change details.

Near-term copper price impact is expected to be minimal. | The article highlights copper reaching above $9,000, attributing the rally to improving demand outlook as China reopens. By strengthening expectations for industrial consumption in the near term, the news supports higher copper prices and market sentiment toward physical and forward demand. | Luminex reports that Anglo American has started a maiden five-hole, 3,200-metre drill program at the Pegasus project’s Medusa copper-porphyry target. The company cites geochemical and geophysical signs of copper mineralization and notes further drilling is contingent on initial results. No production or resource updates are provided. | Golden Arrow commenced an IP-resistivity survey and plans its first diamond drilling program in Q1 at the San Pietro Cu-Au-Co project in Chile. The company aims to refine drill targets for copper and cobalt using geophysics and prior drilling data. It is exploration activity with no immediate production impact. | Government data suggest India, historically a copper exporter, is set to remain a net importer of copper for the fifth straight year in 2022-23. The shift aligns with the May 2018 closure of Sterlite Copper’s 400,000 tpa smelter in Tamil Nadu, reducing domestic supply and lifting import needs. | India is likely to remain a net importer of copper for the fifth year in 2022-23 as domestic refined production stayed lower after Sterlite Copper’s Tuticorin smelter shutdown in 2018. Rising consumption from power, electronics, infrastructure and EVs sustains deficits, requiring continued imports and supporting copper tightness. | Copper on the LME surged past $9,000/t and rose for a fifth straight session, up as much as 1.8% on Wednesday. The rally is linked to a weaker US dollar and improving expectations that China’s demand will rebound after the COVID wave. Interest-rate-hike hopes also support copper prices. | Copper prices topped $9,000 per tonne on the LME, up 1.1% and in a fifth straight daily gain, as traders expect China’s border reopening to revive demand. Analysts also note the weaker U.S. dollar could support dollar-priced metals, though some caution copper may not rebound as strongly as other commodities. | Armed robbers in Chile stole 13 shipping containers of copper from a port near San Antonio, taken from Codelco shipments destined for China. Estimated value is about $4.4 million. The heist adds pressure on transport and port security, following prior thefts from northern copper cathode trains.
