news_summary: Comex copper for January delivery settled 2.15% higher at $4.1545 per pound, gaining 8.75 cents. The market extended a five-session rally, reaching its highest settlement since June 15, 2022. It is also up month-to-date and year-to-date, underscoring near-term bullish momentum. | Power Nickel reports assay results extending the PN-22-009 nickel-copper-Co-PGE mineralized interval to 40.3m, with high-grade Ni-Cu values and plans to extend drilling further in Q1 2023. The update supports continuity of the Nisk resource, but provides no near-term production change. | Palladium One reported additional drill intercepts at its Tyko Project in Ontario, including high-grade massive sulphide assays with up to about 10.3% nickel and 2.9% copper over 1.8 meters. The company frames results as reinforcing West Pickle’s potential and plans further exploration to connect mineral zones in 2023. | Palladium One reported additional high-grade drill intercepts at its Tyko nickel-copper project, including zones with up to ~10.3% nickel and ~2.9% copper over drill intervals. The release focuses on exploration success and connecting mineralized zones, without any near-term production or supply-change information. | Glencore said vandals attacked its copper mine in Peru. The incident is expected to disrupt operations and raise short-term supply uncertainty, which typically weighs on copper pricing as markets adjust for potential output losses and timing delays. | Comex copper front-month settled 0.76% higher at $4.1860, gaining 3.15 cents per pound. The move extends a six-session rally, with the settlement at the highest since June 13, 2022. The piece frames copper as trending upward month-to-date and year-to-date.

The updated geologic model is expected to increase contained resources and improve mine economics, supporting potential development of the copper project in Quebec. | BHP selected seven companies for its BHP Xplor accelerator to fast-track early-stage exploration of copper and nickel. The firms receive funding up to $500,000, mentorship, and in-kind services to accelerate testing of new concepts. The programme may support longer-term supply prospects, but timing is distant. | BHP selected seven companies for its BHP Xplor programme to fast-track early-stage exploration for copper and nickel. The piece cites supportive China property/economic stimulus, low LME/SHFE/COMEX inventories, and a weaker US dollar easing monetary-tightening concerns, offering bullish near-term upside despite COVID-case risks.

news_summary: China’s reopening after the zero-COVID policy is framed as a catalyst for economic recovery. Traders are already positioning for stronger demand, including copper, alongside iron ore and Chinese tech stocks. The report cites a sharp rise in China’s CSI 300 index, reinforcing bullish sentiment for commodities exposed to Chinese demand. | China’s copper concentrate imports in December 2022 fell MoM, attributed to Peru’s political protests disrupting Las Bambas and Antapaccay deliveries and US port truck driver strikes reducing exports to China. Chinese smelter winter stockpiling also ended, with fewer scheduled inbound shipments in December, tightening supply visibility. | The U.S. EPA plans a final determination under the Clean Water Act to prevent Northern Dynasty from storing mine waste for the proposed Pebble copper/gold project in Alaska’s Bristol Bay. The decision would halt future development if it causes similar harm, reducing potential copper supply. | China imported 347,400 mt of copper cathode in Dec 2022, down MoM and sharply YoY, citing a continued closure of the import window, slower year-end customs clearance, and COVID disruptions. SMM expects January imports to fall further, while exports rise, with logistics delays pushing volumes into February. | Vedanta is reported to be scrapping plans to sell its “mega” copper smelter in India. The decision could affect expectations for copper refining capacity and operational continuity. Near-term sentiment may be mildly supportive for copper, though the headline alone provides limited detail on timing or production impact. | Teck Resources produced 270,500 mt of copper in FY2022, below guidance, citing the December 2022 closure of its Highland Valley copper mine in Canada after an engineering accident. The issue has stabilized. Teck forecasts higher FY2023 copper output of 390,000–445,000 mt, adjusting supply expectations. | Faraday Copper Corp. reported drill results from its Copper Creek Project in Arizona, including 33.77m at 3.43% copper (and a longer 65.20m at 1.88% copper) and additional lower-grade copper intervals. The company says Phase II drilling could expand mineralization and the resource. | The Biden EPA blocked Alaska’s Pebble Mine from advancing, rejecting developers’ plan under the Clean Water Act due to unacceptable impacts on the Bristol Bay salmon ecosystem. By halting a major proposed gold and copper project, the decision reduces potential copper supply, weighing on copper price expectations. Broader mining actions are mentioned for other minerals. | Prismo Metals agreed to acquire a 75% interest in Infinitum Copper’s Hot Breccia porphyry-copper skarn project in Arizona, including a $350,000 payment and share issuance. Prismo plans to begin exploration and drilling soon, but near-term copper output impact is limited. | The article headline says U.S. federal authorities used a “rare veto” to block an Alaska mine plan involving copper (and gold). Denying the project can delay future copper supply and worsen short-term supply expectations, which generally creates bearish price pressure for copper markets. | The article headline says federal authorities veto a plan for a copper mine in Alaska. If the veto delays or blocks the project, market expectations for near-term copper supply may worsen, supporting higher copper prices over the following days to weeks. (Gold is mentioned but isn’t tracked here.) | The U.S. EPA used Clean Water Act veto authority to prohibit activities linked to the proposed Pebble Mine in Alaska, effectively ending the project. The mine is aimed at developing copper and gold. The decision cites risks to Bristol Bay salmon habitat and removes a potential future copper supply source. | The United States notified the WTO of its intent to appeal panel reports on Section 232 tariffs and quotas for steel and aluminum, arguing Article XXI security exceptions should not be questioned. Because appellate review is deadlocked, disputes are effectively on hold, implying no near-term requirement to remove or change measures. | Candente Copper reports shareholder approval of a $4 million private placement with Fortescue’s unit, via issuance of 22.2 million shares at $0.18, expected to close around Feb. 6 subject to TSX approval. The funding supports development of its Cañariaco copper project in Peru. | Teck missed its 2022 copper guidance, producing 270,500 tonnes versus the estimate of 273,000–290,000, citing geotechnical issues, unplanned maintenance, and extreme weather. It also trimmed QB2 copper startup output expectations for 2023 (150–180,000 tonnes), worsening near-term copper supply outlook.

news_summary: The U.S. EPA blocked the proposed Pebble Mine in Alaska, citing unacceptable impacts on Bristol Bay’s salmon ecosystem. The project is described as the world’s most significant undeveloped copper and gold resource. The decision deals a heavy blow to future copper supply development, likely pressuring copper prices lower. | Vale SA said Q4 2022 nickel production fell 1.3% to 47,400 tonnes, while nickel sales volumes jumped 30.2% to 58,200 tonnes by drawing on pent-up stocks to meet contracts. The company also noted weaker iron ore output and ongoing expectations for 2023 nickel production. | US EPA blocked the proposed Pebble Mine in Alaska under the Clean Water Act due to expected damage to Bristol Bay salmon habitat. The mine was a large undeveloped copper (and gold) resource; blocking it reduces prospective copper supply, which can support near-term copper price expectations despite possible appeals. | Chile’s copper production rose slightly month-on-month in December 2022 but fell year-on-year, with output down across the year. The report attributes the decline to worsening ore grades, limited water resources, and maintenance issues. It also notes lower concentrate recovery and weaker performance at major mines like Escondida. | Atlantic Nickel reports record 2022 performance at its Santa Rita nickel-copper sulphide mine in Brazil, including higher concentrate output and details of hedging for 2022-2023. Realized prices were about US$12.23/lb Ni and US$3.70/lb Cu, but the release is mainly historical, limiting immediate price pressure. | Glencore said it will keep its 2023 output guidance despite a 12% copper production plunge in 2022, citing issues at Katanga and disruptions including an Antapaccay suspension after protests. Nickel production rose 5% on improved operations, but overall guidance stayed unchanged, limiting near-term price impact. | Capstone Copper reported 2022 nine-month copper production of 136.3 kt with lower C1 cash costs and provided 2023 guidance. It expects declining C1 costs overall, with Mantoverde ramp-up timing and sulphuric acid price assumptions reducing costs, while Cozamin costs rise from new tailings/paste-backfill commissioning. | Camino Minerals signed a non-binding earn-in and joint venture letter of intent with Nittetsu for Peru’s Los Chapitos copper exploration project. Nittetsu can earn 35% through CAD $10.1 million in payments and expenditures over three years. Camino remains operator while due diligence proceeds toward a definitive agreement. | Spectra7 says data center operators are shifting toward active copper cables as switches move to 56G PAM4 signaling, since passive copper has length limits. It highlights GaugeChanger chips enabling 100–800Gbps copper connectivity, expecting continued growth in PAM4 interfaces. This points to incremental copper interconnect demand, though it’s a marketing release. | Copper Mountain Mining said it will release its Q4 and full-year 2022 results on March 27, 2023, and host a conference call. The company produces copper from its southern British Columbia mine, which could affect near-term expectations for copper supply, but no new production or guidance figures were provided. | Vedanta dropped plans to sell its Sterlite Copper smelter in Tamil Nadu and is considering restarting the idled facility shuttered in 2018. A reopening would boost Indian copper output and reduce import needs, amid peak local demand tied to electrification and renewables. | The EPA used its Clean Water Act veto to block the proposed Pebble Mine in Alaska, citing potential harm to Bristol Bay’s salmon fishery. The project is described as a major undeveloped copper resource. Companies and state officials condemn the decision, and legal challenges are expected, increasing copper supply uncertainty. | MMG has placed its Las Bambas copper mine in Peru on care and maintenance after protests linked to the impeachment and arrest of a former Peruvian president escalated community opposition. The shutdown removes a large share of near-term copper supply from the world’s second-largest copper-producing country.

news_summary: Peru’s political crisis is reported to cut off access to roughly 2% of the world’s copper supply. With supply tightening from a key origin, market expectations for near-term availability worsen, likely increasing copper prices over the coming days and into the next few weeks. | Taseko says it is in talks with Sojitz to buy the 12.5% effective interest it does not already control in the Gibraltar copper mine in British Columbia. Any deal would include an upfront payment and five years of annual payments tied to minimum and contingent payments based on Gibraltar copper revenues. | Taseko Mines said it is in confidential discussions with Sojitz Corp. about buying Sojitz’s effective interest linked to Taseko’s Gibraltar copper mine stake. Any deal would involve upfront and five-year annual payments, with contingent amounts tied to Gibraltar copper revenues. No binding agreement is signed. | Haywood Securities says Filo Mining’s new drill results from its Filo del Sol project in Argentina show the longest interval yet, improving perceived project potential. This can strengthen takeover appeal and bolster sentiment around the company’s future copper supply outlook, offering a mild bullish signal for copper-linked markets. | Future Metals reports additional nickel-copper assay “hits” at its Panton project. The update is interpreted as improved potential resource prospects for both nickel and copper. Such discovery-related news can modestly support short-term market sentiment by raising expectations for future supply, even without immediate production details. | Sojitz will sell its 12.5% stake in Canada’s Gibraltar copper-molybdenum mine to Taseko. The divestment cites unstable operations, higher environmental-liability risks, deteriorating grades, and recent unplanned mill downtime and power outages that disrupted December production. Output is sizable, so supply-risk concerns may weigh on copper. | Arizona senators Sinema and Kelly urged the Interior Department to classify copper as a critical mineral to secure domestic supply for the power grid and clean energy buildout. Some conservation and tribal groups oppose the designation, citing environmental and sacred-site concerns near the Resolution mine.

Glencore’s Antapaccay restart hasn’t offset unrest-driven supply risks, keeping copper sentiment bearish.

news_summary: Copper futures edged lower as the US Dollar Index rebounded ahead of upcoming US inflation data, raising investor caution. LME and SHFE copper finished down. Domestic copper inventories fell modestly, mainly on stronger Guangdong consumption, but east China stocks still rose. With demand still weak, prices may drift lower. | SMM data show China’s mainstream copper stocks rose 3,400 mt to 316,300 mt over the weekend. The increase is tempered by limited imported arrivals and smelters slowing shipments to Shanghai amid spot-premium levels. Consumption in Shanghai is improving, while downstream replenishment may increase if prices stop falling. | London copper edged up 0.2% but stayed near a one-week low as traders were cautious ahead of U.S. CPI. The dollar’s rise weighed, but China bank-loan data supported demand hopes. LME aluminium also rose 0.4%, while SHFE nickel fell 3.4% amid weaker sentiment. | SMM reports the Imported Copper Concentrate Index falling to $79.38/mt, with further TCs reduced amid tepid spot activity. Improving logistics at American mines is offset by South America shipment issues affecting contract deliveries. Deals show lower clean ore TCs (e.g., $75/mt and $65/mt), tempering buyer inquiries and sentiment for copper. | After the Chinese Lantern Festival, copper scrap sales picked up as sellers moved aggressively amid high copper prices and inventories built before the CNY holiday due to weak consumption. Scrap supply is described as adequate, with still-large volumes available at Ningbo port, despite prior loss-making conditions late December to January. | Nevada Copper said its Pumpkin Hollow underground mine restart is progressing, with completed dike crossings, definition drilling/assays of initial EN Zone stopes, and a construction contract for key mill/ore-handling and dewatering systems. The company targets a mill restart in Q3 2023 and ramp-up toward nameplate capacity, supporting near-term copper supply expectations. | Trilogy Metals will publish updated feasibility study results for its Arctic copper-zinc-lead-silver-gold project in Alaska, and hold an investor call/webinar. The project is copper-dominant, which can affect market expectations about future copper supply, but no immediate production change is indicated.

news_summary: Comex copper for February delivery settled up 1.48% at $4.0870 per pound, gaining 5.95 cents. The contract was up on two of the past three sessions and remains above recent lows, though still below its 52-week high and off its 2023 settlement peak. | LME and SHFE copper rose overnight as the U.S. dollar index fell, with traders focused on upcoming U.S. CPI and interest-rate expectations. Chinese tracked copper stocks increased, but inventory growth slowed due to reduced smelter shipments to warehouses and scarce imported arrivals, while downstream demand remained tepid. | Copper edged higher as the U.S. dollar weakened ahead of U.S. inflation data. LME three-month copper rose 0.2% and Shanghai March copper gained 0.7%. Gains were also supported by Freeport-McMoRan cutting its copper sales forecast after rains and landslides shut the Grasberg mine until end-month. | ASX-listed Xanadu Mines advances its Kharmagtai copper-gold deposit in Mongolia near Rio Tinto’s Oyu Tolgoi, citing a large resource, PEA economics and new exploration and funding steps with Zijin. Approval-driven funding supports longer-term supply expectations, but first copper concentrate is targeted for 2027, limiting near-term price impact. | Meridian Mining UK reported that georeferenced historical BP archives uncovered the strongest copper stream anomaly at its Cabaçal Alvorada prospect (peak 164 ppm Cu) and plans additional mapping/geochemical/ground geophysics followed by diamond drilling. The company also restarted near-mine programs and is progressing a PEA for the project. | Ivanhoe Electric announced a major resource upgrade at its Santa Cruz copper project in Arizona, increasing indicated tonnage and contained copper, adding resources at East Ridge and Texaco, and reporting high potential recovery from metallurgical leach-float tests. The update is intended to underpin an initial economic assessment later in Q3 2023. | Copper prices have stalled since early February as China’s demand recovery disappoints. CME data show copper futures near recent lows. The bearish tone is reinforced by rising SHFE inventories, a stronger dollar, weaker manufacturing, and disruptions including strikes and temporary mine shutdowns in Peru alongside Chile’s output decline. | New Energy Metals announced TSX Venture Exchange approval for its Troitsa Copper property option, outlining cash and share payments to potentially acquire a 100% interest. It also provided an update on a Roslyn lithium option agreement, but the only direct tracked-metal item is the copper property approval, with no near-term production impact described.
